CONFIGURATION_H_VERSION.name: CONFIGURATION_H_VERSION
CONFIGURATION_H_VERSION.comment:
Configuration.h

Basic settings such as:

- Type of electronics
- Type of temperature sensor
- Printer geometry
- Endstop configuration
- LCD controller
- Extra features

Advanced settings can be found in Configuration_adv.h

STRING_CONFIG_H_AUTHOR.name: STRING_CONFIG_H_AUTHOR
STRING_CONFIG_H_AUTHOR.comment: Author info of this build printed to the host during boot and M115

CUSTOM_VERSION_FILE.name: CUSTOM_VERSION_FILE
CUSTOM_VERSION_FILE.comment: Path from the root directory (no quotes)

MOTHERBOARD.name: MOTHERBOARD
MOTHERBOARD.comment: Choose the name from boards.h that matches your setup

SERIAL_PORT.name: SERIAL_PORT
SERIAL_PORT.comment:
Select the serial port on the board to use for communication with the host.
This allows the connection of wireless adapters (for instance) to non-default port pins.
Serial port -1 is the USB emulated serial port, if available.
Note: The first serial port (-1 or 0) will always be used by the Arduino bootloader.

BAUDRATE.name: BAUDRATE
BAUDRATE.comment:
Serial Port Baud Rate
This is the default communication speed for all serial ports.
Set the baud rate defaults for additional serial ports below.

250000 works in most cases, but you might try a lower speed if
you commonly experience drop-outs during host printing.
You may try up to 1000000 to speed up SD file transfer.

BAUD_RATE_GCODE.name: BAUD_RATE_GCODE
BAUD_RATE_GCODE.comment: Enable G-code M575 to set the baud rate

SERIAL_PORT_2.name: SERIAL_PORT_2
SERIAL_PORT_2.comment:
Select a secondary serial port on the board to use for communication with the host.
Currently Ethernet (-2) is only supported on Teensy 4.1 boards.

SERIAL_PORT_3.name: SERIAL_PORT_3
SERIAL_PORT_3.comment:
Select a third serial port on the board to use for communication with the host.
Currently supported for AVR, DUE, SAMD51, LPC1768/9, STM32/STM32F1/HC32, and Teensy 4.x

RS485_SERIAL_PORT.name: RS485_SERIAL_PORT
RS485_SERIAL_PORT.comment: Select a serial port to communicate with RS485 protocol

M485_PROTOCOL.name: M485_PROTOCOL
M485_PROTOCOL.comment: Check your host for protocol compatibility

BLUETOOTH.name: BLUETOOTH
BLUETOOTH.comment: Enable the Bluetooth serial interface on AT90USB devices

CUSTOM_MACHINE_NAME.name: CUSTOM_MACHINE_NAME
CUSTOM_MACHINE_NAME.comment: Name displayed in the LCD "Ready" message and Info menu

CONFIGURABLE_MACHINE_NAME.name: CONFIGURABLE_MACHINE_NAME
CONFIGURABLE_MACHINE_NAME.comment: Add G-code M550 to set/report the machine name

MACHINE_UUID.name: MACHINE_UUID
MACHINE_UUID.comment:
Printer's unique ID, used by some programs to differentiate between machines.
Choose your own or use a service like https://www.uuidgenerator.net/version4

X_DRIVER_TYPE.name: X_DRIVER_TYPE
X_DRIVER_TYPE.comment:
Stepper Drivers

These settings allow Marlin to tune stepper driver timing and enable advanced options for
stepper drivers that support them. You may also override timing options in Configuration_adv.h.

Use TMC2208/TMC2208_STANDALONE for TMC2225 drivers and TMC2209/TMC2209_STANDALONE for TMC2226 drivers.

Options: A4988, A5984, DRV8825, LV8729, TB6560, TB6600, TMC2100,
         TMC2130, TMC2130_STANDALONE, TMC2160, TMC2160_STANDALONE,
         TMC2208, TMC2208_STANDALONE, TMC2209, TMC2209_STANDALONE,
         TMC2240, TMC2660, TMC2660_STANDALONE,
         TMC5130, TMC5130_STANDALONE, TMC5160, TMC5160_STANDALONE

AXIS4_NAME.name: AXIS4_NAME
AXIS4_NAME.comment:
Additional Axis Settings

Define AXISn_ROTATES for all axes that rotate or pivot.
Rotational axis coordinates are expressed in degrees.

AXISn_NAME defines the letter used to refer to the axis in (most) G-code commands.
By convention the names and roles are typically:
  'A' : Rotational axis parallel to X
  'B' : Rotational axis parallel to Y
  'C' : Rotational axis parallel to Z
  'U' : Secondary linear axis parallel to X
  'V' : Secondary linear axis parallel to Y
  'W' : Secondary linear axis parallel to Z

Regardless of these settings the axes are internally named I, J, K, U, V, W.

X_ENABLE_ON.name: X_ENABLE_ON
X_ENABLE_ON.comment: For Inverting Stepper Enable Pins (Active Low) use 0, Non Inverting (Active High) use 1

DISABLE_X.name: DISABLE_X
DISABLE_X.comment:
Disable axis steppers immediately when they're not being stepped.
WARNING: When motors turn off there is a chance of losing position accuracy!

INVERT_X_DIR.name: INVERT_X_DIR
INVERT_X_DIR.comment: Invert the stepper direction. Change (or reverse the motor connector) if an axis goes the wrong way.

INVERT_E0_DIR.name: INVERT_E0_DIR
INVERT_E0_DIR.comment: For direct drive extruder v9 set to true, for geared extruder set to false.

EXTRUDERS.name: EXTRUDERS
EXTRUDERS.comment: This defines the number of extruders

DEFAULT_NOMINAL_FILAMENT_DIA.name: DEFAULT_NOMINAL_FILAMENT_DIA
DEFAULT_NOMINAL_FILAMENT_DIA.comment: Generally expected filament diameter (1.75, 2.85, 3.0, ...). Used for Volumetric, Filament Width Sensor, etc.

SINGLENOZZLE.name: SINGLENOZZLE
SINGLENOZZLE.comment: For Cyclops or any "multi-extruder" that shares a single nozzle.

SINGLENOZZLE_STANDBY_TEMP.name: SINGLENOZZLE_STANDBY_TEMP
SINGLENOZZLE_STANDBY_TEMP.comment:
Save and restore temperature and fan speed on tool-change.
Set standby for the unselected tool with M104/106/109 T...

SWITCHING_EXTRUDER.name: SWITCHING_EXTRUDER
SWITCHING_EXTRUDER.comment: A dual extruder that uses a single stepper motor

SWITCHING_EXTRUDER_SERVO_ANGLES.name: SWITCHING_EXTRUDER_SERVO_ANGLES
SWITCHING_EXTRUDER_SERVO_ANGLES.comment: Angles for E0, E1[, E2, E3]

MECHANICAL_SWITCHING_EXTRUDER.name: MECHANICAL_SWITCHING_EXTRUDER
MECHANICAL_SWITCHING_EXTRUDER.comment: Switch extruders by bumping the toolhead. Requires EVENT_GCODE_TOOLCHANGE_#.

SWITCHING_NOZZLE.name: SWITCHING_NOZZLE
SWITCHING_NOZZLE.comment:
A dual-nozzle that uses a servomotor to raise/lower one (or both) of the nozzles.
Can be combined with SWITCHING_EXTRUDER.

SWITCHING_NOZZLE_E1_SERVO_NR.name: SWITCHING_NOZZLE_E1_SERVO_NR
SWITCHING_NOZZLE_E1_SERVO_NR.comment: If two servos are used, the index of the second

SWITCHING_NOZZLE_SERVO_ANGLES.name: SWITCHING_NOZZLE_SERVO_ANGLES
SWITCHING_NOZZLE_SERVO_ANGLES.comment: A pair of angles for { E0, E1 }. For Dual Servo use two pairs: { { lower, raise }, { lower, raise } }

SWITCHING_NOZZLE_SERVO_DWELL.name: SWITCHING_NOZZLE_SERVO_DWELL
SWITCHING_NOZZLE_SERVO_DWELL.comment: Dwell time to wait for servo to make physical move

SWITCHING_NOZZLE_LIFT_TO_PROBE.name: SWITCHING_NOZZLE_LIFT_TO_PROBE
SWITCHING_NOZZLE_LIFT_TO_PROBE.comment: Lift toolheads out of the way while probing

MECHANICAL_SWITCHING_NOZZLE.name: MECHANICAL_SWITCHING_NOZZLE
MECHANICAL_SWITCHING_NOZZLE.comment: Switch nozzles by bumping the toolhead. Requires EVENT_GCODE_TOOLCHANGE_#.

PARKING_EXTRUDER.name: PARKING_EXTRUDER
PARKING_EXTRUDER.comment:
Two separate X-carriages with extruders that connect to a moving part
via a solenoid docking mechanism. Requires SOL1_PIN and SOL2_PIN.

MAGNETIC_PARKING_EXTRUDER.name: MAGNETIC_PARKING_EXTRUDER
MAGNETIC_PARKING_EXTRUDER.comment:
Two separate X-carriages with extruders that connect to a moving part
via a magnetic docking mechanism using movements and no solenoid

project   : https://www.thingiverse.com/thing:3080893
movements : https://youtu.be/0xCEiG9VS3k
            https://youtu.be/Bqbcs0CU2FE

PARKING_EXTRUDER_PARKING_X.name: PARKING_EXTRUDER_PARKING_X
PARKING_EXTRUDER_PARKING_X.comment: X positions for parking the extruders

PARKING_EXTRUDER_GRAB_DISTANCE.name: PARKING_EXTRUDER_GRAB_DISTANCE
PARKING_EXTRUDER_GRAB_DISTANCE.comment: (mm) Distance to move beyond the parking point to grab the extruder

PARKING_EXTRUDER_SOLENOIDS_INVERT.name: PARKING_EXTRUDER_SOLENOIDS_INVERT
PARKING_EXTRUDER_SOLENOIDS_INVERT.comment: If enabled, the solenoid is NOT magnetized with applied voltage

PARKING_EXTRUDER_SOLENOIDS_PINS_ACTIVE.name: PARKING_EXTRUDER_SOLENOIDS_PINS_ACTIVE
PARKING_EXTRUDER_SOLENOIDS_PINS_ACTIVE.comment: LOW or HIGH pin signal energizes the coil

PARKING_EXTRUDER_SOLENOIDS_DELAY.name: PARKING_EXTRUDER_SOLENOIDS_DELAY
PARKING_EXTRUDER_SOLENOIDS_DELAY.comment: (ms) Delay for magnetic field. No delay if 0 or not defined.

MANUAL_SOLENOID_CONTROL.name: MANUAL_SOLENOID_CONTROL
MANUAL_SOLENOID_CONTROL.comment: Manual control of docking solenoids with M380 S / M381

MPE_FAST_SPEED.name: MPE_FAST_SPEED
MPE_FAST_SPEED.comment: (mm/min) Speed for travel before last distance point

MPE_SLOW_SPEED.name: MPE_SLOW_SPEED
MPE_SLOW_SPEED.comment: (mm/min) Speed for last distance travel to park and couple

DIFFERENTIAL_EXTRUDER.name: DIFFERENTIAL_EXTRUDER
DIFFERENTIAL_EXTRUDER.comment:
Differential Extruder

The X and E steppers work together to create a differential drive system.
Simple  : E steps = X + E   ; X steps = X  (E drives a loop, X stays the same)
Balanced: E steps = X + E/2 ; X steps = X - E/2  (Dual loop system)

SWITCHING_TOOLHEAD.name: SWITCHING_TOOLHEAD
SWITCHING_TOOLHEAD.comment:
Switching Toolhead

Support for swappable and dockable toolheads, such as
the E3D Tool Changer. Toolheads are locked with a servo.

MAGNETIC_SWITCHING_TOOLHEAD.name: MAGNETIC_SWITCHING_TOOLHEAD
MAGNETIC_SWITCHING_TOOLHEAD.comment:
Magnetic Switching Toolhead

Support swappable and dockable toolheads with a magnetic
docking mechanism using movement and no servo.

ELECTROMAGNETIC_SWITCHING_TOOLHEAD.name: ELECTROMAGNETIC_SWITCHING_TOOLHEAD
ELECTROMAGNETIC_SWITCHING_TOOLHEAD.comment:
Electromagnetic Switching Toolhead

Parking for CoreXY / HBot kinematics.
Toolheads are parked at one edge and held with an electromagnet.
Supports more than 2 Toolheads. See https://youtu.be/JolbsAKTKf4

SWITCHING_TOOLHEAD_Y_POS.name: SWITCHING_TOOLHEAD_Y_POS
SWITCHING_TOOLHEAD_Y_POS.comment: (mm) Y position of the toolhead dock

SWITCHING_TOOLHEAD_Y_CLEAR.name: SWITCHING_TOOLHEAD_Y_CLEAR
SWITCHING_TOOLHEAD_Y_CLEAR.comment: (mm) Minimum distance from dock for unobstructed X axis

SWITCHING_TOOLHEAD_X_POS.name: SWITCHING_TOOLHEAD_X_POS
SWITCHING_TOOLHEAD_X_POS.comment: (mm) X positions for parking the extruders

PRIME_BEFORE_REMOVE.name: PRIME_BEFORE_REMOVE
PRIME_BEFORE_REMOVE.comment: Prime the nozzle before release from the dock

MIXING_EXTRUDER.name: MIXING_EXTRUDER
MIXING_EXTRUDER.comment:
"Mixing Extruder"
  - Adds G-codes M163 and M164 to set and "commit" the current mix factors.
  - Extends the stepping routines to move multiple steppers in proportion to the mix.
  - Optional support for Repetier Firmware's 'M164 S<index>' supporting virtual tools.
  - This implementation supports up to two mixing extruders.
  - Enable DIRECT_MIXING_IN_G1 for M165 and mixing in G1 (from Pia Taubert's reference implementation).

MIXING_STEPPERS.name: MIXING_STEPPERS
MIXING_STEPPERS.comment: Number of steppers in your mixing extruder

MIXING_VIRTUAL_TOOLS.name: MIXING_VIRTUAL_TOOLS
MIXING_VIRTUAL_TOOLS.comment: Use the Virtual Tool method with M163 and M164

DIRECT_MIXING_IN_G1.name: DIRECT_MIXING_IN_G1
DIRECT_MIXING_IN_G1.comment: Allow ABCDHI mix factors in G1 movement commands

GRADIENT_MIX.name: GRADIENT_MIX
GRADIENT_MIX.comment: Support for gradient mixing with M166 and LCD

MIXING_PRESETS.name: MIXING_PRESETS
MIXING_PRESETS.comment: Assign 8 default V-tool presets for 2 or 3 MIXING_STEPPERS

GRADIENT_VTOOL.name: GRADIENT_VTOOL
GRADIENT_VTOOL.comment: Add M166 T to use a V-tool index as a Gradient alias

HOTEND_OFFSET_X.name: HOTEND_OFFSET_X
HOTEND_OFFSET_X.comment:
Offset of the extruders (uncomment if using more than one and relying on firmware to position when changing).
The offset has to be X=0, Y=0 for the extruder 0 hotend (default extruder).
For the other hotends it is their distance from the extruder 0 hotend.

DISABLE_E.name: DISABLE_E
DISABLE_E.comment: Disable the extruder when not stepping

DISABLE_OTHER_EXTRUDERS.name: DISABLE_OTHER_EXTRUDERS
DISABLE_OTHER_EXTRUDERS.comment: Keep only the active extruder enabled

MMU_MODEL.name: MMU_MODEL
MMU_MODEL.comment:
Multi-Material Unit
Set to one of these predefined models:

  PRUSA_MMU1           : Průša MMU1 (The "multiplexer" version)
  PRUSA_MMU2           : Průša MMU2
  PRUSA_MMU2S          : Průša MMU2S (Requires MK3S extruder with motion sensor, EXTRUDERS = 5)
  PRUSA_MMU3           : Průša MMU3  (Requires MK3S extruder with motion sensor and MMU firmware version 3.x.x, EXTRUDERS = 5)
  EXTENDABLE_EMU_MMU2  : MMU with configurable number of filaments (ERCF, SMuFF or similar with Průša MMU2 compatible firmware)
  EXTENDABLE_EMU_MMU2S : MMUS with configurable number of filaments (ERCF, SMuFF or similar with Průša MMU2 compatible firmware)

Requires NOZZLE_PARK_FEATURE to park print head in case MMU unit fails.
See additional options in Configuration_adv.h.

PSU_CONTROL.name: PSU_CONTROL
PSU_CONTROL.comment:
Power Supply Control

Enable and connect the power supply to the PS_ON_PIN.
Specify whether the power supply is active HIGH or active LOW.

PSU_DEFAULT_OFF.name: PSU_DEFAULT_OFF
PSU_DEFAULT_OFF.comment: Keep power off until enabled directly with M80

PSU_POWERUP_DELAY.name: PSU_POWERUP_DELAY
PSU_POWERUP_DELAY.comment: (ms) Delay for the PSU to warm up to full power

LED_POWEROFF_TIMEOUT.name: LED_POWEROFF_TIMEOUT
LED_POWEROFF_TIMEOUT.comment: (ms) Turn off LEDs after power-off, with this amount of delay

PSU_OFF_REDUNDANT.name: PSU_OFF_REDUNDANT
PSU_OFF_REDUNDANT.comment: Second pin for redundant power control

PSU_OFF_REDUNDANT_INVERTED.name: PSU_OFF_REDUNDANT_INVERTED
PSU_OFF_REDUNDANT_INVERTED.comment: Redundant pin state is the inverse of PSU_ACTIVE_STATE

PS_ON1_PIN.name: PS_ON1_PIN
PS_ON1_PIN.comment: Redundant pin required to enable power in combination with PS_ON_PIN

PS_ON_EDM_PIN.name: PS_ON_EDM_PIN
PS_ON_EDM_PIN.comment: External Device Monitoring pins for external power control relay feedback. Fault on mismatch.

PS_EDM_RESPONSE.name: PS_EDM_RESPONSE
PS_EDM_RESPONSE.comment: (ms) Time to allow for relay action

POWER_OFF_TIMER.name: POWER_OFF_TIMER
POWER_OFF_TIMER.comment: Enable M81 D<seconds> to power off after a delay

POWER_OFF_WAIT_FOR_COOLDOWN.name: POWER_OFF_WAIT_FOR_COOLDOWN
POWER_OFF_WAIT_FOR_COOLDOWN.comment: Enable M81 S to power off only after cooldown

PSU_POWERUP_GCODE.name: PSU_POWERUP_GCODE
PSU_POWERUP_GCODE.comment: G-code to run after power-on (e.g., case light on)

PSU_POWEROFF_GCODE.name: PSU_POWEROFF_GCODE
PSU_POWEROFF_GCODE.comment: G-code to run before power-off (e.g., case light off)

AUTO_POWER_CONTROL.name: AUTO_POWER_CONTROL
AUTO_POWER_CONTROL.comment: Enable automatic control of the PS_ON pin

AUTO_POWER_E_FANS.name: AUTO_POWER_E_FANS
AUTO_POWER_E_FANS.comment: Turn on PSU for E Fans

AUTO_POWER_CONTROLLERFAN.name: AUTO_POWER_CONTROLLERFAN
AUTO_POWER_CONTROLLERFAN.comment: Turn on PSU for Controller Fan

AUTO_POWER_CHAMBER_FAN.name: AUTO_POWER_CHAMBER_FAN
AUTO_POWER_CHAMBER_FAN.comment: Turn on PSU for Chamber Fan

AUTO_POWER_COOLER_FAN.name: AUTO_POWER_COOLER_FAN
AUTO_POWER_COOLER_FAN.comment: Turn on PSU for Cooler Fan

POWER_TIMEOUT.name: POWER_TIMEOUT
POWER_TIMEOUT.comment: (s) Turn off power if the machine is idle for this duration

POWER_OFF_DELAY.name: POWER_OFF_DELAY
POWER_OFF_DELAY.comment: (s) Delay of poweroff after M81 command. Useful to let fans run for extra time.

AUTO_POWER_E_TEMP.name: AUTO_POWER_E_TEMP
AUTO_POWER_E_TEMP.comment: (°C) PSU on if any extruder is over this temperature

AUTO_POWER_CHAMBER_TEMP.name: AUTO_POWER_CHAMBER_TEMP
AUTO_POWER_CHAMBER_TEMP.comment: (°C) PSU on if the chamber is over this temperature

AUTO_POWER_COOLER_TEMP.name: AUTO_POWER_COOLER_TEMP
AUTO_POWER_COOLER_TEMP.comment: (°C) PSU on if the cooler is over this temperature
