DUMMY_THERMISTOR_998_VALUE.name: DUMMY_THERMISTOR_998_VALUE
DUMMY_THERMISTOR_998_VALUE.comment: Dummy thermistor constant temperature readings, for use with 998 and 999

MAX31865_SENSOR_OHMS_0.name: MAX31865_SENSOR_OHMS_0
MAX31865_SENSOR_OHMS_0.comment: Resistor values when using MAX31865 sensors (-5) on TEMP_SENSOR_0 / 1 / 2 / BED

MAX31865_CALIBRATION_OHMS_0.name: MAX31865_CALIBRATION_OHMS_0
MAX31865_CALIBRATION_OHMS_0.comment: (Ω) Typically 430 for Adafruit PT100; 4300 for Adafruit PT1000

TEMP_RESIDENCY_TIME.name: TEMP_RESIDENCY_TIME
TEMP_RESIDENCY_TIME.comment: (seconds) Time to wait for hotend to "settle" in M109

TEMP_WINDOW.name: TEMP_WINDOW
TEMP_WINDOW.comment: (°C) Temperature proximity for the "temperature reached" timer

TEMP_HYSTERESIS.name: TEMP_HYSTERESIS
TEMP_HYSTERESIS.comment: (°C) Temperature proximity considered "close enough" to the target

TEMP_BED_RESIDENCY_TIME.name: TEMP_BED_RESIDENCY_TIME
TEMP_BED_RESIDENCY_TIME.comment: (seconds) Time to wait for bed to "settle" in M190

TEMP_BED_WINDOW.name: TEMP_BED_WINDOW
TEMP_BED_WINDOW.comment: (°C) Temperature proximity for the "temperature reached" timer

TEMP_BED_HYSTERESIS.name: TEMP_BED_HYSTERESIS
TEMP_BED_HYSTERESIS.comment: (°C) Temperature proximity considered "close enough" to the target

TEMP_CHAMBER_RESIDENCY_TIME.name: TEMP_CHAMBER_RESIDENCY_TIME
TEMP_CHAMBER_RESIDENCY_TIME.comment: (seconds) Time to wait for chamber to "settle" in M191

TEMP_CHAMBER_WINDOW.name: TEMP_CHAMBER_WINDOW
TEMP_CHAMBER_WINDOW.comment: (°C) Temperature proximity for the "temperature reached" timer

TEMP_CHAMBER_HYSTERESIS.name: TEMP_CHAMBER_HYSTERESIS
TEMP_CHAMBER_HYSTERESIS.comment: (°C) Temperature proximity considered "close enough" to the target

TEMP_SENSOR_REDUNDANT_SOURCE.name: TEMP_SENSOR_REDUNDANT_SOURCE
TEMP_SENSOR_REDUNDANT_SOURCE.comment:
Redundant Temperature Sensor (TEMP_SENSOR_REDUNDANT)

Use a temp sensor as a redundant sensor for another reading. Select an unused temperature sensor, and another
sensor you'd like it to be redundant for. If the two thermistors differ by TEMP_SENSOR_REDUNDANT_MAX_DIFF (°C),
the print will be aborted. Whichever sensor is selected will have its normal functions disabled; i.e. selecting
the Bed sensor (-1) will disable bed heating/monitoring.

For selecting source/target use: COOLER, PROBE, BOARD, CHAMBER, BED, E0, E1, E2, E3, E4, E5, E6, E7
TEMP_SENSOR_REDUNDANT_SOURCE.notes: The sensor that will provide the redundant reading.

TEMP_SENSOR_REDUNDANT_TARGET.name: TEMP_SENSOR_REDUNDANT_TARGET
TEMP_SENSOR_REDUNDANT_TARGET.comment: The sensor that we are providing a redundant reading for.

TEMP_SENSOR_REDUNDANT_MAX_DIFF.name: TEMP_SENSOR_REDUNDANT_MAX_DIFF
TEMP_SENSOR_REDUNDANT_MAX_DIFF.comment: (°C) Temperature difference that will trigger a print abort.

HEATER_0_MINTEMP.name: HEATER_0_MINTEMP
HEATER_0_MINTEMP.comment:
Below this temperature the heater will be switched off
because it probably indicates a broken thermistor wire.

HEATER_0_MAXTEMP.name: HEATER_0_MAXTEMP
HEATER_0_MAXTEMP.comment:
Above this temperature the heater will be switched off.
This can protect components from overheating, but NOT from shorts and failures.
(Use MINTEMP for thermistor short/failure protection.)

HOTEND_OVERSHOOT.name: HOTEND_OVERSHOOT
HOTEND_OVERSHOOT.comment:
Thermal Overshoot
During heatup (and printing) the temperature can often "overshoot" the target by many degrees
(especially before PID tuning). Setting the target temperature too close to MAXTEMP guarantees
a MAXTEMP shutdown! Use these values to forbid temperatures being set too close to MAXTEMP.

PIDTEMP.name: PIDTEMP
PIDTEMP.comment:
Temperature Control

 (NONE) : Bang-bang heating
PIDTEMP : PID temperature control (~4.1K)
MPCTEMP : Predictive Model temperature control. (~1.8K without auto-tune)
PIDTEMP.notes: See the PID Tuning Guide at https://reprap.org/wiki/PID_Tuning

PID_MAX.name: PID_MAX
PID_MAX.comment: Limit hotend current while PID is active (see PID_FUNCTIONAL_RANGE below); 255=full current

PID_K1.name: PID_K1
PID_K1.comment: Smoothing factor within any PID loop

MIN_POWER.name: MIN_POWER
MIN_POWER.comment: Min power to improve PID stability (0..PID_MAX). Get the power from the temperature report ('M105' => @:nnn) and try P*2-20 to P*2-10.

PID_DEBUG.name: PID_DEBUG
PID_DEBUG.comment: Print PID debug data to the serial port. Use 'M303 D' to enable/disable.

PID_PARAMS_PER_HOTEND.name: PID_PARAMS_PER_HOTEND
PID_PARAMS_PER_HOTEND.comment: Use separate PID parameters for each extruder (useful for mismatched extruders) Set/get with G-code: M301 E[extruder number, 0-2]

DEFAULT_KP_LIST.name: DEFAULT_KP_LIST
DEFAULT_KP_LIST.comment:
Specify up to one value per hotend here, according to your setup.
If there are fewer values, the last one applies to the remaining hotends.

BANG_MAX.name: BANG_MAX
BANG_MAX.comment: Limit hotend current while in bang-bang mode; 255=full current

MPC_AUTOTUNE.name: MPC_AUTOTUNE
MPC_AUTOTUNE.comment:
Model Predictive Control for hotend

Use a physical model of the hotend to control temperature. When configured correctly this gives
better responsiveness and stability than PID and removes the need for PID_EXTRUSION_SCALING
and PID_FAN_SCALING. Enable MPC_AUTOTUNE and use M306 T to autotune the model.
MPC_AUTOTUNE.notes: Include a method to do MPC auto-tuning (~6.3K bytes of flash)

MPC_AUTOTUNE_DEBUG.name: MPC_AUTOTUNE_DEBUG
MPC_AUTOTUNE_DEBUG.comment: Enable MPC debug logging (~870 bytes of flash)

MPC_EDIT_MENU.name: MPC_EDIT_MENU
MPC_EDIT_MENU.comment: Add MPC editing to the "Advanced Settings" menu. (~1.3K bytes of flash)

MPC_AUTOTUNE_MENU.name: MPC_AUTOTUNE_MENU
MPC_AUTOTUNE_MENU.comment: Add MPC auto-tuning to the "Advanced Settings" menu. (~350 bytes of flash)

MPC_MAX.name: MPC_MAX
MPC_MAX.comment: (0..255) Current to nozzle while MPC is active.

MPC_PTC.name: MPC_PTC
MPC_PTC.comment: Hotend power changes with temperature (e.g., PTC heat cartridges).

MPC_HEATER_ALPHA.name: MPC_HEATER_ALPHA
MPC_HEATER_ALPHA.comment: Temperature coefficient of resistance of the heat cartridges.

MPC_HEATER_REFTEMP.name: MPC_HEATER_REFTEMP
MPC_HEATER_REFTEMP.comment: (°C) Reference temperature for MPC_HEATER_POWER and MPC_HEATER_ALPHA.

MPC_SENSOR_RESPONSIVENESS.name: MPC_SENSOR_RESPONSIVENESS
MPC_SENSOR_RESPONSIVENESS.comment: (K/s per ∆K) Rate of change of sensor temperature from heat block.

MPC_AMBIENT_XFER_COEFF.name: MPC_AMBIENT_XFER_COEFF
MPC_AMBIENT_XFER_COEFF.comment: (W/K) Heat transfer coefficients from heat block to room air with fan off.

MPC_AMBIENT_XFER_COEFF_FAN255.name: MPC_AMBIENT_XFER_COEFF_FAN255
MPC_AMBIENT_XFER_COEFF_FAN255.comment: (W/K) Heat transfer coefficients from heat block to room air with fan on full.

MPC_FAN_0_ALL_HOTENDS.name: MPC_FAN_0_ALL_HOTENDS
MPC_FAN_0_ALL_HOTENDS.comment: For one fan and multiple hotends MPC needs to know how to apply the fan cooling effect.

FILAMENT_HEAT_CAPACITY_PERMM.name: FILAMENT_HEAT_CAPACITY_PERMM
FILAMENT_HEAT_CAPACITY_PERMM.comment:
Filament Heat Capacity (joules/kelvin/mm)
Set at runtime with M306 H<value>
FILAMENT_HEAT_CAPACITY_PERMM.notes: 0.0056 J/K/mm for 1.75mm PLA (0.0149 J/K/mm for 2.85mm PLA). 0.0036 J/K/mm for 1.75mm PETG (0.0094 J/K/mm for 2.85mm PETG). 0.00515 J/K/mm for 1.75mm ABS (0.0137 J/K/mm for 2.85mm ABS). 0.00522 J/K/mm for 1.75mm Nylon (0.0138 J/K/mm for 2.85mm Nylon).

MPC_SMOOTHING_FACTOR.name: MPC_SMOOTHING_FACTOR
MPC_SMOOTHING_FACTOR.notes: (0.0...1.0) Noisy temperature sensors may need a lower value for stabilization.

MPC_MIN_AMBIENT_CHANGE.name: MPC_MIN_AMBIENT_CHANGE
MPC_MIN_AMBIENT_CHANGE.comment: (K/s) Modeled ambient temperature rate of change, when correcting model inaccuracies.

MPC_STEADYSTATE.name: MPC_STEADYSTATE
MPC_STEADYSTATE.comment: (K/s) Temperature change rate for steady state logic to be enforced.

MPC_TUNING_POS.name: MPC_TUNING_POS
MPC_TUNING_POS.comment: (mm) M306 Autotuning position, ideally bed center at first layer height.

EVENT_GCODE_AFTER_MPC_TUNE.name: EVENT_GCODE_AFTER_MPC_TUNE
EVENT_GCODE_AFTER_MPC_TUNE.comment: G-code to execute after MPC tune finished and Z raised.

MAX_BED_POWER.name: MAX_BED_POWER
MAX_BED_POWER.comment:
Max Bed Power
Applies to all forms of bed control (PID, bang-bang, and bang-bang with hysteresis).
When set to any value below 255, enables a form of PWM to the bed that acts like a divider
so don't use it unless you are OK with PWM on your bed. (See the comment on enabling PIDTEMPBED)
MAX_BED_POWER.notes: limits duty cycle to bed; 255=full current

PIDTEMPBED.name: PIDTEMPBED
PIDTEMPBED.comment:
PID Bed Heating

The PID frequency will be the same as the extruder PWM.
If PID_dT is the default, and correct for the hardware/configuration, that means 7.689Hz,
which is fine for driving a square wave into a resistive load and does not significantly
impact FET heating. This also works fine on a Fotek SSR-10DA Solid State Relay into a 250W
heater. If your configuration is significantly different than this and you don't understand
the issues involved, don't use bed PID until someone else verifies that your hardware works.

With this option disabled, bang-bang will be used. BED_LIMIT_SWITCHING enables hysteresis.

MIN_BED_POWER.name: MIN_BED_POWER
MIN_BED_POWER.comment: Min power to improve PID stability (0..MAX_BED_POWER). Get the power from the temperature report ('M105' => B@:nnn) and try P*2-20 to P*2-10.

PID_BED_DEBUG.name: PID_BED_DEBUG
PID_BED_DEBUG.comment: Print Bed PID debug data to the serial port. Use 'M303 D' to enable/disable.

DEFAULT_BED_KP.name: DEFAULT_BED_KP
DEFAULT_BED_KP.comment:
120V 250W silicone heater into 4mm borosilicate (MendelMax 1.5+)
from FOPDT model - kp=.39 Tp=405 Tdead=66, Tc set to 79.2, aggressive factor of .15 (vs .1, 1, 10)

BED_LIMIT_SWITCHING.name: BED_LIMIT_SWITCHING
BED_LIMIT_SWITCHING.comment: FIND YOUR OWN: "M303 E-1 C8 S90" to run autotune on the bed at 90 degreesC for 8 cycles.
BED_LIMIT_SWITCHING.notes: Keep the bed temperature within BED_HYSTERESIS of the target

PELTIER_BED.name: PELTIER_BED
PELTIER_BED.comment:
Peltier Bed - Heating and Cooling

A Peltier device transfers heat from one side to the other in proportion to the amount of
current flowing through the device and the direction of current flow. So the same device
can both heat and cool.

When "cooling" in addition to rejecting the heat transferred from the hot side to the cold
side, the dissipated power (voltage * current) must also be rejected. Be sure to set up a
fan that can be powered in sync with the Peltier unit.

This feature is only set up to run in bang-bang mode because Peltiers don't handle PWM
well without filter circuitry.

Since existing 3D printers are made to handle relatively high current for the heated bed,
we can use the heated bed power pins to control the Peltier power using the same G-codes
as the heated bed (M140, M190, etc.).

A second GPIO pin is required to control current direction.
Two configurations are possible: Relay and H-Bridge

(At this time only relay is supported. H-bridge requires 4 MOS switches configured in H-Bridge.)

Power is handled by the bang-bang control loop: 0 or 255.
Cooling applications are more common than heating, so the pin states are commonly:
  LOW  = Heating = Relay Energized
  HIGH = Cooling = Relay in "Normal" state

PELTIER_DIR_HEAT_STATE.name: PELTIER_DIR_HEAT_STATE
PELTIER_DIR_HEAT_STATE.comment: The relay pin state that causes the Peltier to heat

BED_ANNEALING_GCODE.name: BED_ANNEALING_GCODE
BED_ANNEALING_GCODE.comment: Add 'M190 R T' for more gradual M190 R bed cooling.

PIDTEMPCHAMBER.name: PIDTEMPCHAMBER
PIDTEMPCHAMBER.comment:
PID Chamber Heating

If this option is enabled set PID constants below.
If this option is disabled, bang-bang will be used and CHAMBER_LIMIT_SWITCHING will enable
hysteresis.

The PID frequency will be the same as the extruder PWM.
If PID_dT is the default, and correct for the hardware/configuration, that means 7.689Hz,
which is fine for driving a square wave into a resistive load and does not significantly
impact FET heating. This also works fine on a Fotek SSR-10DA Solid State Relay into a 200W
heater. If your configuration is significantly different than this and you don't understand
the issues involved, don't use chamber PID until someone else verifies that your hardware works.

MAX_CHAMBER_POWER.name: MAX_CHAMBER_POWER
MAX_CHAMBER_POWER.comment:
Max Chamber Power
Applies to all forms of chamber control (PID, bang-bang, and bang-bang with hysteresis).
When set to any value below 255, enables a form of PWM to the chamber heater that acts like a divider
so don't use it unless you are OK with PWM on your heater. (See the comment on enabling PIDTEMPCHAMBER)
MAX_CHAMBER_POWER.notes: limits duty cycle to chamber heater; 255=full current

MIN_CHAMBER_POWER.name: MIN_CHAMBER_POWER
MIN_CHAMBER_POWER.comment: Min power to improve PID stability. (0..MAX_CHAMBER_POWER) Get the power from the temperature report ('M105' => C@:nnn) and try P*2-20 to P*2-10.

PID_CHAMBER_DEBUG.name: PID_CHAMBER_DEBUG
PID_CHAMBER_DEBUG.comment: Print Chamber PID debug data to the serial port. Use 'M303 D' to enable/disable.

DEFAULT_CHAMBER_KP.name: DEFAULT_CHAMBER_KP
DEFAULT_CHAMBER_KP.comment:
Lasko "MyHeat Personal Heater" (200w) modified with a Fotek SSR-10DA to control only the heating element
and placed inside the small Creality printer enclosure tent.

PID_OPENLOOP.name: PID_OPENLOOP
PID_OPENLOOP.comment: Puts PID in open loop. M104/M140 sets the output power from 0 to PID_MAX

SLOW_PWM_HEATERS.name: SLOW_PWM_HEATERS
SLOW_PWM_HEATERS.comment: PWM with very low frequency (roughly 0.125Hz=8s) and minimum state time of approximately 1s useful for heaters driven by a relay

PID_FUNCTIONAL_RANGE.name: PID_FUNCTIONAL_RANGE
PID_FUNCTIONAL_RANGE.comment: If the temperature difference between the target temperature and the actual temperature is more than PID_FUNCTIONAL_RANGE then the PID will be shut off and the heater will be set to min/max.

PID_EDIT_MENU.name: PID_EDIT_MENU
PID_EDIT_MENU.comment: Add PID editing to the "Advanced Settings" menu. (~700 bytes of flash)

PID_AUTOTUNE_MENU.name: PID_AUTOTUNE_MENU
PID_AUTOTUNE_MENU.comment: Add PID auto-tuning to the "Advanced Settings" menu. (~250 bytes of flash)

PREVENT_COLD_EXTRUSION.name: PREVENT_COLD_EXTRUSION
PREVENT_COLD_EXTRUSION.comment:
Prevent extrusion if the temperature is below EXTRUDE_MINTEMP.
Add M302 to set the minimum extrusion temperature and/or turn
cold extrusion prevention on and off.

*** IT IS HIGHLY RECOMMENDED TO LEAVE THIS OPTION ENABLED! ***

PREVENT_LENGTHY_EXTRUDE.name: PREVENT_LENGTHY_EXTRUDE
PREVENT_LENGTHY_EXTRUDE.comment:
Prevent a single extrusion longer than EXTRUDE_MAXLENGTH.
Note: For Bowden Extruders make this large enough to allow load/unload.

THERMAL_PROTECTION_HOTENDS.name: THERMAL_PROTECTION_HOTENDS
THERMAL_PROTECTION_HOTENDS.comment:
Thermal Protection provides additional protection to your printer from damage
and fire. Marlin always includes safe min and max temperature ranges which
protect against a broken or disconnected thermistor wire.

The issue: If a thermistor falls out, it will report the much lower
temperature of the air in the room, and the firmware will keep
the heater on.

If you get "Thermal Runaway" or "Heating failed" errors the
details can be tuned in Configuration_adv.h
THERMAL_PROTECTION_HOTENDS.notes: Enable thermal protection for all extruders

THERMAL_PROTECTION_BED.name: THERMAL_PROTECTION_BED
THERMAL_PROTECTION_BED.comment: Enable thermal protection for the heated bed

THERMAL_PROTECTION_CHAMBER.name: THERMAL_PROTECTION_CHAMBER
THERMAL_PROTECTION_CHAMBER.comment: Enable thermal protection for the heated chamber

THERMAL_PROTECTION_COOLER.name: THERMAL_PROTECTION_COOLER
THERMAL_PROTECTION_COOLER.comment: Enable thermal protection for the laser cooling

COREXY.name: COREXY
COREXY.comment:
Enable one of the options below for CoreXY, CoreXZ, or CoreYZ kinematics,
either in the usual order or reversed

MARKFORGED_INVERSE.name: MARKFORGED_INVERSE
MARKFORGED_INVERSE.comment: Enable for an inverted Markforged kinematics belt path

BELTPRINTER.name: BELTPRINTER
BELTPRINTER.comment: Enable for a belt style printer with endless "Z" motion

ARTICULATED_ROBOT_ARM.name: ARTICULATED_ROBOT_ARM
ARTICULATED_ROBOT_ARM.comment: Articulated robot (arm). Joints are directly mapped to axes with no kinematics.

FOAMCUTTER_XYUV.name: FOAMCUTTER_XYUV
FOAMCUTTER_XYUV.comment:
For a hot wire cutter with parallel horizontal axes (X, I) where the heights of the two wire
ends are controlled by parallel axes (Y, J). Joints are directly mapped to axes (no kinematics).

POLARGRAPH_MAX_BELT_LEN.name: POLARGRAPH_MAX_BELT_LEN
POLARGRAPH_MAX_BELT_LEN.comment: (mm) Belt length at full extension. Override with M665 H.

PEN_UP_DOWN_MENU.name: PEN_UP_DOWN_MENU
PEN_UP_DOWN_MENU.comment: Add "Pen Up" and "Pen Down" to the MarlinUI menu

DEFAULT_SEGMENTS_PER_SECOND.name: DEFAULT_SEGMENTS_PER_SECOND
DEFAULT_SEGMENTS_PER_SECOND.comment:
Make delta curves from many straight lines (linear interpolation).
This is a trade-off between visible corners (not enough segments)
and processor overload (too many expensive sqrt calls).

DELTA_HOME_TO_SAFE_ZONE.name: DELTA_HOME_TO_SAFE_ZONE
DELTA_HOME_TO_SAFE_ZONE.comment: After homing move down to a height where XY movement is unconstrained

DELTA_CALIBRATION_MENU.name: DELTA_CALIBRATION_MENU
DELTA_CALIBRATION_MENU.comment:
Delta calibration menu
Add three-point calibration to the MarlinUI menu.
See http://minow.blogspot.com/index.html#4918805519571907051

DELTA_AUTO_CALIBRATION.name: DELTA_AUTO_CALIBRATION
DELTA_AUTO_CALIBRATION.comment: G33 Delta Auto-Calibration. Enable EEPROM_SETTINGS to store results.

DELTA_CALIBRATION_DEFAULT_POINTS.name: DELTA_CALIBRATION_DEFAULT_POINTS
DELTA_CALIBRATION_DEFAULT_POINTS.comment: Default number of probe points : n*n (1 -> 7)

PRINTABLE_RADIUS.name: PRINTABLE_RADIUS
PRINTABLE_RADIUS.comment: Print surface diameter/2 minus unreachable space (avoid collisions with vertical towers).

DELTA_DIAGONAL_ROD.name: DELTA_DIAGONAL_ROD
DELTA_DIAGONAL_ROD.comment: Center-to-center distance of the holes in the diagonal push rods.

DELTA_HEIGHT.name: DELTA_HEIGHT
DELTA_HEIGHT.comment: Distance between bed and nozzle Z home position
DELTA_HEIGHT.notes: (mm) Get this value from G33 auto calibrate

DELTA_ENDSTOP_ADJ.name: DELTA_ENDSTOP_ADJ
DELTA_ENDSTOP_ADJ.comment: (mm) Get these values from G33 auto calibrate

DELTA_RADIUS.name: DELTA_RADIUS
DELTA_RADIUS.comment: Horizontal distance bridged by diagonal push rods when effector is centered.
DELTA_RADIUS.notes: (mm) Get this value from G33 auto calibrate

DELTA_TOWER_ANGLE_TRIM.name: DELTA_TOWER_ANGLE_TRIM
DELTA_TOWER_ANGLE_TRIM.comment:
Trim adjustments for individual towers
tower angle corrections for X and Y tower / rotate XYZ so Z tower angle = 0
measured in degrees anticlockwise looking from above the printer
DELTA_TOWER_ANGLE_TRIM.notes: (mm) Get these values from G33 auto calibrate

DELTA_RADIUS_TRIM_TOWER.name: DELTA_RADIUS_TRIM_TOWER
DELTA_RADIUS_TRIM_TOWER.comment: Delta radius and diagonal rod adjustments

SCARA.name: SCARA
SCARA.comment:
SCARA kinematics

- Positive angles rotate counterclockwise when looking down from above.
- Theta is the shoulder angle. 0 degrees aligns to Cartesian positive X.
- Psi is the elbow angle. 0° is straight, ±180° is completely folded (right-handed positive, left-handed negative).

The Morgan SCARA was developed by QHARLEY in South Africa in 2012-2013.
Implemented and slightly reworked by JCERNY in June, 2014.

Mostly Printed SCARA is an open source design by Tyler Williams. See:
  https://www.thingiverse.com/thing:2487048
  https://www.thingiverse.com/thing:1241491

DEFAULT_SEGMENTS_PER_SECOND.name: DEFAULT_SEGMENTS_PER_SECOND
DEFAULT_SEGMENTS_PER_SECOND.comment: If movement is choppy try lowering this value

SCARA_LINKAGE_1.name: SCARA_LINKAGE_1
SCARA_LINKAGE_1.comment: Length of inner and outer support arms. Measure arm lengths precisely.

SCARA_OFFSET_X.name: SCARA_OFFSET_X
SCARA_OFFSET_X.comment:
SCARA tower offset (position of shoulder axis relative to bed zero position)
This needs to be reasonably accurate as it defines the printbed position in the SCARA space.

MIDDLE_DEAD_ZONE_R.name: MIDDLE_DEAD_ZONE_R
MIDDLE_DEAD_ZONE_R.comment: Radius of unreachable area near shoulder axis

SCARA_ELBOW_DIR.name: SCARA_ELBOW_DIR
SCARA_ELBOW_DIR.comment: Direction of elbow bend. 1 = right-handed (counterclockwise), -1 = left-handed (clockwise)

SCARA_CROSSTALK_FACTOR.name: SCARA_CROSSTALK_FACTOR
SCARA_CROSSTALK_FACTOR.comment:
This defines how shoulder movement affects the distal arm angle. Common values:
0.0 if distal arm retains its angle relative to proximal arm (e.g. if elbow motor rides on proximal arm)
1.0 if distal arm retains its angle relative to cartesian X axis (e.g. Morgan and MPSCARA)
A two-stage reduction with an intermediate pulley on the shoulder axis has crosstalk =
intermediate_pulley_teeth/elbow_pulley_teeth (first reduction stage does not affect crosstalk)

SCARA_HOME_THETA.name: SCARA_HOME_THETA
SCARA_HOME_THETA.comment:
Shoulder and elbow angles when in home position. If left undefined, cartesian home is used and
angles are calculated by inverse kinematics (note: M665 home offsets are still angles)

SCARA_CALIBRATION.name: SCARA_CALIBRATION
SCARA_CALIBRATION.comment: Enable M360-M364 to calibrate SCARA angles

AXEL_TPARA.name: AXEL_TPARA
AXEL_TPARA.comment: Enable for TPARA kinematics and configure below

TPARA_LINKAGE_1.name: TPARA_LINKAGE_1
TPARA_LINKAGE_1.comment: Length of inner and outer support arms. Measure arm lengths precisely.

TPARA_SHOULDER_AXIS_HEIGHT.name: TPARA_SHOULDER_AXIS_HEIGHT
TPARA_SHOULDER_AXIS_HEIGHT.comment: Height of the Shoulder axis (pivot) relative to the tower floor

TPARA_ARM_X_HOME_POS.name: TPARA_ARM_X_HOME_POS
TPARA_ARM_X_HOME_POS.comment:
The position of the last linkage relative to the robot arm origin
(intersection of the base axis and floor) when at the home position
TPARA_ARM_X_HOME_POS.notes: (mm) Measured from shoulder axis to tool holder axis in home position

TPARA_ARM_Z_HOME_POS.name: TPARA_ARM_Z_HOME_POS
TPARA_ARM_Z_HOME_POS.comment: (mm) Measured from tool holder axis to the floor

TPARA_OFFSET_X.name: TPARA_OFFSET_X
TPARA_OFFSET_X.comment:
TPARA Workspace offset relative to the tower (position of workspace origin relative to robot Tower origin )
This needs to be reasonably accurate as it defines the printbed position in the TPARA space.
TPARA_OFFSET_X.notes: (mm) to coincide with minimum radius MIDDLE_DEAD_ZONE_R, and W(0,0,0) is reachable

TPARA_TCP_OFFSET_X.name: TPARA_TCP_OFFSET_X
TPARA_TCP_OFFSET_X.comment:
TPARA tool connection point offset, relative to the tool moving frame origin which is in the last linkage axis,
(TCP: tool center/connection point) of the robot,
the plane of measured offset must be alligned with home position plane
TPARA_TCP_OFFSET_X.notes: (mm) Tool flange: 27 (distance from pivot to bolt holes), extruder tool: 50.0,

TPARA_TCP_OFFSET_Z.name: TPARA_TCP_OFFSET_Z
TPARA_TCP_OFFSET_Z.comment: (mm) Tool flange (bottom): -6 (caution as Z 0 posiion will crash second linkage to the floor, -35 is safe for testing with no tool), extruder tool (depends on extruder): -65.0

FEEDRATE_SCALING.name: FEEDRATE_SCALING
FEEDRATE_SCALING.comment: Convert XY feedrate from mm/s to degrees/s on the fly

MIDDLE_DEAD_ZONE_R.name: MIDDLE_DEAD_ZONE_R
MIDDLE_DEAD_ZONE_R.comment:
Radius around the center where the arm cannot reach
For now use a hardcoded uniform limit, although it should be calculated, or fix a limit for each axis angle

TPARA_MAX_L1L2_ANGLE.name: TPARA_MAX_L1L2_ANGLE
TPARA_MAX_L1L2_ANGLE.comment: Max angle between L1 and L2

POLAR.name: POLAR
POLAR.comment:
POLAR Kinematics
 developed by Kadir ilkimen for PolarBear CNC and babyBear
 https://github.com/kadirilkimen/Polar-Bear-Cnc-Machine
 https://github.com/kadirilkimen/babyBear-3D-printer

A polar machine can have different configurations.
This kinematics is only compatible with the following configuration:
       X : Independent linear
  Y or B : Polar
       Z : Independent linear

For example, PolarBear has CoreXZ plus Polar Y or B.

Motion problem for Polar axis near center / origin:

3D printing:
Movements very close to the center of the polar axis take more time than others.
This brief delay results in more material deposition due to the pressure in the nozzle.

Current Kinematics and feedrate scaling deals with this by making the movement as fast
as possible. It works for slow movements but doesn't work well with fast ones. A more
complicated extrusion compensation must be implemented.

Ideally, it should estimate that a long rotation near the center is ahead and will cause
unwanted deposition. Therefore it can compensate the extrusion beforehand.

Laser cutting:
Same thing would be a problem for laser engraving too. As it spends time rotating at the
center point, more likely it will burn more material than it should. Therefore similar
compensation would be implemented for laser-cutting operations.

Milling:
This shouldn't be a problem for cutting/milling operations.

DEFAULT_SEGMENTS_PER_SECOND.name: DEFAULT_SEGMENTS_PER_SECOND
DEFAULT_SEGMENTS_PER_SECOND.comment: If movement is choppy try lowering this value

POLAR_FAST_RADIUS.name: POLAR_FAST_RADIUS
POLAR_FAST_RADIUS.comment:
Movements fall inside POLAR_FAST_RADIUS are assigned the highest possible feedrate
to compensate unwanted deposition related to the near-origin motion problem.

POLAR_CENTER_OFFSET.name: POLAR_CENTER_OFFSET
POLAR_CENTER_OFFSET.comment:
Radius which is unreachable by the tool.
Needed if the tool is not perfectly aligned to the center of the polar axis.

FEEDRATE_SCALING.name: FEEDRATE_SCALING
FEEDRATE_SCALING.comment: Convert XY feedrate from mm/s to degrees/s on the fly

ENDSTOPPULLUPS.name: ENDSTOPPULLUPS
ENDSTOPPULLUPS.comment: Enable pullup for all endstops to prevent a floating state

ENDSTOPPULLUP_XMIN.name: ENDSTOPPULLUP_XMIN
ENDSTOPPULLUP_XMIN.comment: Disable ENDSTOPPULLUPS to set pullups individually

ENDSTOPPULLDOWNS.name: ENDSTOPPULLDOWNS
ENDSTOPPULLDOWNS.comment: Enable pulldown for all endstops to prevent a floating state

ENDSTOPPULLDOWN_XMIN.name: ENDSTOPPULLDOWN_XMIN
ENDSTOPPULLDOWN_XMIN.comment: Disable ENDSTOPPULLDOWNS to set pulldowns individually

X_MIN_ENDSTOP_HIT_STATE.name: X_MIN_ENDSTOP_HIT_STATE
X_MIN_ENDSTOP_HIT_STATE.comment:
Endstop "Hit" State
Set to the state (HIGH or LOW) that applies to each endstop.

ENDSTOP_INTERRUPTS_FEATURE.name: ENDSTOP_INTERRUPTS_FEATURE
ENDSTOP_INTERRUPTS_FEATURE.comment:
Enable this feature if all enabled endstop pins are interrupt-capable.
This will remove the need to poll the interrupt pins, saving many CPU cycles.

ENDSTOP_NOISE_THRESHOLD.name: ENDSTOP_NOISE_THRESHOLD
ENDSTOP_NOISE_THRESHOLD.comment:
Endstop Noise Threshold

Enable if your probe or endstops falsely trigger due to noise.

- Higher values may affect repeatability or accuracy of some bed probes.
- To fix noise install a 100nF ceramic capacitor in parallel with the switch.
- This feature is not required for common micro-switches mounted on PCBs
  based on the Makerbot design, which already have the 100nF capacitor.

DETECT_BROKEN_ENDSTOP.name: DETECT_BROKEN_ENDSTOP
DETECT_BROKEN_ENDSTOP.comment: Check for stuck or disconnected endstops during homing moves.

DISTINCT_E_FACTORS.name: DISTINCT_E_FACTORS
DISTINCT_E_FACTORS.comment:
With this option each E stepper can have its own factors for the
following movement settings. If fewer factors are given than the
total number of extruders, the last value applies to the rest.

DEFAULT_AXIS_STEPS_PER_UNIT.name: DEFAULT_AXIS_STEPS_PER_UNIT
DEFAULT_AXIS_STEPS_PER_UNIT.comment:
Default Axis Steps Per Unit (linear=steps/mm, rotational=steps/°)
Override with M92 (when enabled below)
                                     X, Y, Z [, I [, J [, K...]]], E0 [, E1[, E2...]]

EDITABLE_STEPS_PER_UNIT.name: EDITABLE_STEPS_PER_UNIT
EDITABLE_STEPS_PER_UNIT.comment: Enable support for M92. Disable to save at least ~530 bytes of flash.

DEFAULT_MAX_FEEDRATE.name: DEFAULT_MAX_FEEDRATE
DEFAULT_MAX_FEEDRATE.comment:
Default Max Feed Rate (linear=mm/s, rotational=°/s)
Override with M203
                                     X, Y, Z [, I [, J [, K...]]], E0 [, E1[, E2...]]

LIMITED_MAX_FR_EDITING.name: LIMITED_MAX_FR_EDITING
LIMITED_MAX_FR_EDITING.comment: Limit edit via M203 or LCD to DEFAULT_MAX_FEEDRATE * 2

DEFAULT_MAX_ACCELERATION.name: DEFAULT_MAX_ACCELERATION
DEFAULT_MAX_ACCELERATION.comment:
Default Max Acceleration (speed change with time) (linear=mm/(s^2), rotational=°/(s^2))
(Maximum start speed for accelerated moves)
Override with M201
                                     X, Y, Z [, I [, J [, K...]]], E0 [, E1[, E2...]]

LIMITED_MAX_ACCEL_EDITING.name: LIMITED_MAX_ACCEL_EDITING
LIMITED_MAX_ACCEL_EDITING.comment: Limit edit via M201 or LCD to DEFAULT_MAX_ACCELERATION * 2

DEFAULT_ACCELERATION.name: DEFAULT_ACCELERATION
DEFAULT_ACCELERATION.comment:
Default Acceleration (speed change with time) (linear=mm/(s^2), rotational=°/(s^2))
Override with M204

  M204 P    Acceleration
  M204 R    Retract Acceleration
  M204 T    Travel Acceleration
DEFAULT_ACCELERATION.notes: X, Y, Z and E acceleration for printing moves

DEFAULT_TRAVEL_ACCELERATION.name: DEFAULT_TRAVEL_ACCELERATION
DEFAULT_TRAVEL_ACCELERATION.comment: X, Y, Z acceleration for travel (non printing) moves

CLASSIC_JERK.name: CLASSIC_JERK
CLASSIC_JERK.comment:
Default Jerk limits (mm/s)
Override with M205 X Y Z . . . E

"Jerk" specifies the minimum speed change that requires acceleration.
When changing speed and direction, if the difference is less than the
value set here, it may happen instantaneously.

TRAVEL_EXTRA_XYJERK.name: TRAVEL_EXTRA_XYJERK
TRAVEL_EXTRA_XYJERK.comment: Additional jerk allowance for all travel moves

LIMITED_JERK_EDITING.name: LIMITED_JERK_EDITING
LIMITED_JERK_EDITING.comment: Limit edit via M205 or LCD to DEFAULT_aJERK * 2

JD_HANDLE_SMALL_SEGMENTS.name: JD_HANDLE_SMALL_SEGMENTS
JD_HANDLE_SMALL_SEGMENTS.comment: Use curvature estimation instead of just the junction angle for small segments (< 1mm) with large junction angles (> 135°).

S_CURVE_ACCELERATION.name: S_CURVE_ACCELERATION
S_CURVE_ACCELERATION.comment:
S-Curve Acceleration

This option eliminates vibration during printing by fitting a Bézier
curve to move acceleration, producing much smoother direction changes.

See https://github.com/synthetos/TinyG/wiki/Jerk-Controlled-Motion-Explained

S_CURVE_FACTOR.name: S_CURVE_FACTOR
S_CURVE_FACTOR.comment: Define to use 4th instead of 6th order motion curve
S_CURVE_FACTOR.notes: Initial and final acceleration factor, ideally 0.1 to 0.4. Shouldn't generally require tuning.

Z_MIN_PROBE_USES_Z_MIN_ENDSTOP_PIN.name: Z_MIN_PROBE_USES_Z_MIN_ENDSTOP_PIN
Z_MIN_PROBE_USES_Z_MIN_ENDSTOP_PIN.comment:
Enable this option for a probe connected to the Z-MIN pin.
The probe replaces the Z-MIN endstop and is used for Z homing.
(Automatically enables USE_PROBE_FOR_Z_HOMING.)

USE_PROBE_FOR_Z_HOMING.name: USE_PROBE_FOR_Z_HOMING
USE_PROBE_FOR_Z_HOMING.comment: Force the use of the probe for Z-axis homing

Z_MIN_PROBE_PIN.name: Z_MIN_PROBE_PIN
Z_MIN_PROBE_PIN.comment:
Z_MIN_PROBE_PIN

Override this pin only if the probe cannot be connected to
the default Z_MIN_PROBE_PIN for the selected MOTHERBOARD.

 - The simplest option is to use a free endstop connector.
 - Use 5V for powered (usually inductive) sensors.

 - For simple switches...
   - Normally-closed (NC) also connect to GND.
   - Normally-open (NO) also connect to 5V.

PROBE_MANUALLY.name: PROBE_MANUALLY
PROBE_MANUALLY.comment:
The "Manual Probe" provides a means to do "Auto" Bed Leveling without a probe.
Use G29 repeatedly, adjusting the Z height at each point with movement commands
or (with LCD_BED_LEVELING) the LCD controller.

FIX_MOUNTED_PROBE.name: FIX_MOUNTED_PROBE
FIX_MOUNTED_PROBE.comment:
A Fix-Mounted Probe either doesn't deploy or needs manual deployment.
  (e.g., an inductive probe or a nozzle-based probe-switch.)

NOZZLE_AS_PROBE.name: NOZZLE_AS_PROBE
NOZZLE_AS_PROBE.comment:
Use the nozzle as the probe, as with a conductive
nozzle system or a piezo-electric smart effector.

Z_PROBE_SERVO_NR.name: Z_PROBE_SERVO_NR
Z_PROBE_SERVO_NR.comment: Z Servo Probe, such as an endstop switch on a rotating arm.

Z_SERVO_ANGLES.name: Z_SERVO_ANGLES
Z_SERVO_ANGLES.comment: Z Servo Deploy and Stow angles

Z_SERVO_MEASURE_ANGLE.name: Z_SERVO_MEASURE_ANGLE
Z_SERVO_MEASURE_ANGLE.comment: Use if the servo must move to a "free" position for measuring after deploy

Z_SERVO_DEACTIVATE_AFTER_STOW.name: Z_SERVO_DEACTIVATE_AFTER_STOW
Z_SERVO_DEACTIVATE_AFTER_STOW.comment: Deactivate the servo when probe is stowed

BLTOUCH.name: BLTOUCH
BLTOUCH.comment: The BLTouch probe uses a Hall effect sensor and emulates a servo.

MAGLEV4.name: MAGLEV4
MAGLEV4.comment:
MagLev V4 probe by MDD

This probe is deployed and activated by powering a built-in electromagnet.

MAGLEV_TRIGGER_PIN.name: MAGLEV_TRIGGER_PIN
MAGLEV_TRIGGER_PIN.comment: Set to the connected digital output

MAGLEV_TRIGGER_DELAY.name: MAGLEV_TRIGGER_DELAY
MAGLEV_TRIGGER_DELAY.comment: Changing this risks overheating the coil

TOUCH_MI_PROBE.name: TOUCH_MI_PROBE
TOUCH_MI_PROBE.comment:
Touch-MI Probe by hotends.fr

This probe is deployed and activated by moving the X-axis to a magnet at the edge of the bed.
By default, the magnet is assumed to be on the left and activated by a home. If the magnet is
on the right, enable and set TOUCH_MI_DEPLOY_XPOS to the deploy position.

Also requires: BABYSTEPPING, BABYSTEP_ZPROBE_OFFSET, Z_SAFE_HOMING,
               and a minimum Z_CLEARANCE_FOR_HOMING of 10.

TOUCH_MI_RETRACT_Z.name: TOUCH_MI_RETRACT_Z
TOUCH_MI_RETRACT_Z.comment: Height at which the probe retracts

TOUCH_MI_DEPLOY_XPOS.name: TOUCH_MI_DEPLOY_XPOS
TOUCH_MI_DEPLOY_XPOS.comment: For a magnet on the right side of the bed

BD_SENSOR.name: BD_SENSOR
BD_SENSOR.comment:
Bed Distance Sensor

Measures the distance from bed to nozzle with accuracy of 0.01mm.
For information about this sensor https://github.com/markniu/Bed_Distance_sensor
Uses I2C port, so it requires I2C library markyue/Panda_SoftMasterI2C.

BD_SENSOR_PROBE_NO_STOP.name: BD_SENSOR_PROBE_NO_STOP
BD_SENSOR_PROBE_NO_STOP.comment: Probe bed without stopping at each probe point

BIQU_MICROPROBE_V1.name: BIQU_MICROPROBE_V1
BIQU_MICROPROBE_V1.comment:
BIQU MicroProbe

A lightweight, solenoid-driven probe.
For information about this sensor https://github.com/bigtreetech/MicroProbe

Also requires PROBE_ENABLE_DISABLE

SOLENOID_PROBE.name: SOLENOID_PROBE
SOLENOID_PROBE.comment: A probe that is deployed and stowed with a solenoid pin (SOL1_PIN)

Z_PROBE_SLED.name: Z_PROBE_SLED
Z_PROBE_SLED.comment: A sled-mounted probe like those designed by Charles Bell.

SLED_DOCKING_OFFSET.name: SLED_DOCKING_OFFSET
SLED_DOCKING_OFFSET.comment: The extra distance the X axis must travel to pickup the sled. 0 should be fine but you can push it further if you'd like.

RACK_AND_PINION_PROBE.name: RACK_AND_PINION_PROBE
RACK_AND_PINION_PROBE.comment: A probe deployed by moving the x-axis, such as the Wilson II's rack-and-pinion probe designed by Marty Rice.

MAG_MOUNTED_PROBE.name: MAG_MOUNTED_PROBE
MAG_MOUNTED_PROBE.comment:
Magnetically Mounted Probe
For probes such as Euclid, Klicky, Klackender, etc.

MAG_MOUNTED_PROBE_SERVO_NR.name: MAG_MOUNTED_PROBE_SERVO_NR
MAG_MOUNTED_PROBE_SERVO_NR.comment:
Magnetically Mounted Probe with a Servo mechanism
Probe Deploy and Stow both follow the same basic sequence:
 - Rotate the SERVO to its Deployed angle
 - Perform XYZ moves to deploy or stow the PROBE
 - Rotate the SERVO to its Stowed angle

MAG_MOUNTED_DEPLOY_1.name: MAG_MOUNTED_DEPLOY_1
MAG_MOUNTED_DEPLOY_1.comment: Move to side Dock & Attach probe

DUET_SMART_EFFECTOR.name: DUET_SMART_EFFECTOR
DUET_SMART_EFFECTOR.comment:
Duet Smart Effector (for delta printers) - https://docs.duet3d.com/en/Duet3D_hardware/Accessories/Smart_Effector
When the pin is defined you can use M672 to set/reset the probe sensitivity.

SMART_EFFECTOR_MOD_PIN.name: SMART_EFFECTOR_MOD_PIN
SMART_EFFECTOR_MOD_PIN.comment: Connect a GPIO pin to the Smart Effector MOD pin

SENSORLESS_PROBING.name: SENSORLESS_PROBING
SENSORLESS_PROBING.comment:
Use StallGuard2 to probe the bed with the nozzle.
Requires stallGuard-capable Trinamic stepper drivers.
CAUTION: This can damage machines with Z lead screws.
         Take extreme care when setting up this feature.

Z_PROBE_ALLEN_KEY.name: Z_PROBE_ALLEN_KEY
Z_PROBE_ALLEN_KEY.comment:
Allen key retractable z-probe as seen on many Kossel delta printers - https://reprap.org/wiki/Kossel#Autolevel_probe
Deploys by touching z-axis belt. Retracts by pushing the probe down.

Z_PROBE_ALLEN_KEY_DEPLOY_1.name: Z_PROBE_ALLEN_KEY_DEPLOY_1
Z_PROBE_ALLEN_KEY_DEPLOY_1.comment:
2 or 3 sets of coordinates for deploying and retracting the spring loaded touch probe on G29,
if servo actuated touch probe is not defined. Uncomment as appropriate for your printer/probe.

NOZZLE_TO_PROBE_OFFSET.name: NOZZLE_TO_PROBE_OFFSET
NOZZLE_TO_PROBE_OFFSET.comment:
Nozzle-to-Probe offsets { X, Y, Z }

X and Y offset
  Use a caliper or ruler to measure the distance (in mm) from the tip of
  the Nozzle to the center-point of the Probe in the X and Y axes.

Z offset
- For the Z offset use your best known value and adjust at runtime.
- Common probes trigger below the nozzle and have negative values for Z offset.
- Probes triggering above the nozzle height are uncommon but do exist. When using
  probes such as this, carefully set Z_CLEARANCE_DEPLOY_PROBE and Z_CLEARANCE_BETWEEN_PROBES
  to avoid collisions during probing.

Tune and Adjust
-  Probe Offsets can be tuned at runtime with 'M851', LCD menus, babystepping, etc.
-  PROBE_OFFSET_WIZARD (Configuration_adv.h) can be used for setting the Z offset.

Assuming the typical work area orientation:
 - Probe to RIGHT of the Nozzle has a Positive X offset
 - Probe to LEFT  of the Nozzle has a Negative X offset
 - Probe in BACK  of the Nozzle has a Positive Y offset
 - Probe in FRONT of the Nozzle has a Negative Y offset

Some examples:
  #define NOZZLE_TO_PROBE_OFFSET { 10, 10, -1 }   // Example "1"
  #define NOZZLE_TO_PROBE_OFFSET {-10,  5, -1 }   // Example "2"
  #define NOZZLE_TO_PROBE_OFFSET {  5, -5, -1 }   // Example "3"
  #define NOZZLE_TO_PROBE_OFFSET {-15,-10, -1 }   // Example "4"

    +-- BACK ---+
    |    [+]    |
  L |        1  | R <-- Example "1" (right+,  back+)
  E |  2        | I <-- Example "2" ( left-,  back+)
  F |[-]  N  [+]| G <-- Nozzle
  T |       3   | H <-- Example "3" (right+, front-)
    | 4         | T <-- Example "4" ( left-, front-)
    |    [-]    |
    O-- FRONT --+
NOZZLE_TO_PROBE_OFFSET.notes: (mm) X, Y, Z distance from Nozzle tip to Probe trigger-point

PROBING_TOOL.name: PROBING_TOOL
PROBING_TOOL.comment: Enable and set to use a specific tool for probing. Disable to allow any tool.

PROBE_WAKEUP_TIME_MS.name: PROBE_WAKEUP_TIME_MS
PROBE_WAKEUP_TIME_MS.comment: (ms) Time for the probe to wake up

PROBING_MARGIN.name: PROBING_MARGIN
PROBING_MARGIN.comment:
Most probes should stay away from the edges of the bed, but
with NOZZLE_AS_PROBE this can be negative for a wider probing area.

XY_PROBE_FEEDRATE.name: XY_PROBE_FEEDRATE
XY_PROBE_FEEDRATE.comment:
X and Y axis travel speed between probes.
Leave undefined to use the average of the current XY homing feedrate.

Z_PROBE_FEEDRATE_FAST.name: Z_PROBE_FEEDRATE_FAST
Z_PROBE_FEEDRATE_FAST.comment: Feedrate for the first approach when double-probing (MULTIPLE_PROBING == 2)

Z_PROBE_FEEDRATE_SLOW.name: Z_PROBE_FEEDRATE_SLOW
Z_PROBE_FEEDRATE_SLOW.comment: Feedrate for the "accurate" probe of each point

PROBE_ACTIVATION_SWITCH.name: PROBE_ACTIVATION_SWITCH
PROBE_ACTIVATION_SWITCH.comment:
Probe Activation Switch
A switch indicating proper deployment, or an optical
switch triggered when the carriage is near the bed.

PROBE_TARE.name: PROBE_TARE
PROBE_TARE.comment:
Tare Probe (determine zero-point) prior to each probe.
Useful for a strain gauge or piezo sensor that needs to factor out
elements such as cables pulling on the carriage.

PROBE_TARE_STATE.name: PROBE_TARE_STATE
PROBE_TARE_STATE.comment: State to write pin for tare

PROBE_TARE_MENU.name: PROBE_TARE_MENU
PROBE_TARE_MENU.comment: Display a menu item to tare the probe

PROBE_TARE_ONLY_WHILE_INACTIVE.name: PROBE_TARE_ONLY_WHILE_INACTIVE
PROBE_TARE_ONLY_WHILE_INACTIVE.comment: Fail to tare/probe if PROBE_ACTIVATION_SWITCH is active

PROBE_ENABLE_DISABLE.name: PROBE_ENABLE_DISABLE
PROBE_ENABLE_DISABLE.comment:
Probe Enable / Disable
The probe only provides a triggered signal when enabled.

MULTIPLE_PROBING.name: MULTIPLE_PROBING
MULTIPLE_PROBING.comment:
Multiple Probing

You may get improved results by probing 2 or more times.
With EXTRA_PROBING the more atypical reading(s) will be disregarded.

A total of 2 does fast/slow probes with a weighted average.
A total of 3 or more adds more slow probes, taking the average.

Z_CLEARANCE_DEPLOY_PROBE.name: Z_CLEARANCE_DEPLOY_PROBE
Z_CLEARANCE_DEPLOY_PROBE.comment:
Z probes require clearance when deploying, stowing, and moving between
probe points to avoid hitting the bed and other hardware.
Servo-mounted probes require extra space for the arm to rotate.
Inductive probes need space to keep from triggering early.

Use these settings to specify the distance (mm) to raise the probe (or
lower the bed). The values set here apply over and above any (negative)
probe Z Offset set with NOZZLE_TO_PROBE_OFFSET, M851, or the LCD.
Only integer values >= 1 are valid here.

Example: 'M851 Z-5' with a CLEARANCE of 4  =>  9mm from bed to nozzle.
    But: 'M851 Z+1' with a CLEARANCE of 2  =>  2mm from bed to nozzle.

Z_AFTER_PROBING.name: Z_AFTER_PROBING
Z_AFTER_PROBING.comment: (mm) Z position after probing is done

Z_PROBE_LOW_POINT.name: Z_PROBE_LOW_POINT
Z_PROBE_LOW_POINT.comment: (mm) Farthest distance below the trigger-point to go before stopping

PROBE_OFFSET_XMIN.name: PROBE_OFFSET_XMIN
PROBE_OFFSET_XMIN.comment: For M851 provide ranges for adjusting the X, Y, and Z probe offsets

Z_MIN_PROBE_REPEATABILITY_TEST.name: Z_MIN_PROBE_REPEATABILITY_TEST
Z_MIN_PROBE_REPEATABILITY_TEST.comment: Enable the M48 repeatability test to test probe accuracy

PAUSE_BEFORE_DEPLOY_STOW.name: PAUSE_BEFORE_DEPLOY_STOW
PAUSE_BEFORE_DEPLOY_STOW.comment: Before deploy/stow pause for user confirmation

PROBING_HEATERS_OFF.name: PROBING_HEATERS_OFF
PROBING_HEATERS_OFF.comment:
Enable one or more of the following if probing seems unreliable.
Heaters and/or fans can be disabled during probing to minimize electrical
noise. A delay can also be added to allow noise and vibration to settle.
These options are most useful for the BLTouch probe, but may also improve
readings with inductive probes and piezo sensors.

WAIT_FOR_BED_HEATER.name: WAIT_FOR_BED_HEATER
WAIT_FOR_BED_HEATER.comment: Wait for bed to heat back up between probes (to improve accuracy)

WAIT_FOR_HOTEND.name: WAIT_FOR_HOTEND
WAIT_FOR_HOTEND.comment: Wait for hotend to heat back up between probes (to improve accuracy & prevent cold extrude)

PROBING_ESTEPPERS_OFF.name: PROBING_ESTEPPERS_OFF
PROBING_ESTEPPERS_OFF.comment: Turn all extruder steppers off when probing

PROBING_STEPPERS_OFF.name: PROBING_STEPPERS_OFF
PROBING_STEPPERS_OFF.comment: Turn all steppers off (unless needed to hold position) when probing (including extruders)

DELAY_BEFORE_PROBING.name: DELAY_BEFORE_PROBING
DELAY_BEFORE_PROBING.comment: (ms) To prevent vibrations from triggering piezo sensors

PREHEAT_BEFORE_PROBING.name: PREHEAT_BEFORE_PROBING
PREHEAT_BEFORE_PROBING.comment: Require minimum nozzle and/or bed temperature for probing

PROBING_NOZZLE_TEMP.name: PROBING_NOZZLE_TEMP
PROBING_NOZZLE_TEMP.comment: (°C) Only applies to E0 at this time

NO_MOTION_BEFORE_HOMING.name: NO_MOTION_BEFORE_HOMING
NO_MOTION_BEFORE_HOMING.comment: Inhibit movement until all axes have been homed. Also enable HOME_AFTER_DEACTIVATE for extra safety.

HOME_AFTER_DEACTIVATE.name: HOME_AFTER_DEACTIVATE
HOME_AFTER_DEACTIVATE.comment: Require rehoming after steppers are deactivated. Also enable NO_MOTION_BEFORE_HOMING for extra safety.

Z_IDLE_HEIGHT.name: Z_IDLE_HEIGHT
Z_IDLE_HEIGHT.comment:
Set Z_IDLE_HEIGHT if the Z-Axis moves on its own when steppers are disabled.
 - Use a low value (i.e., Z_MIN_POS) if the nozzle falls down to the bed.
 - Use a large value (i.e., Z_MAX_POS) if the bed falls down, away from the nozzle.

Z_CLEARANCE_FOR_HOMING.name: Z_CLEARANCE_FOR_HOMING
Z_CLEARANCE_FOR_HOMING.comment: (mm) Minimal Z height before homing (G28) for Z clearance above the bed, clamps, ... You'll need this much clearance above Z_MAX_POS to avoid grinding.

Z_AFTER_HOMING.name: Z_AFTER_HOMING
Z_AFTER_HOMING.comment: (mm) Height to move to after homing (if Z was homed)

XY_AFTER_HOMING.name: XY_AFTER_HOMING
XY_AFTER_HOMING.comment: (mm) Move to an XY position after homing (and raising Z)

EVENT_GCODE_AFTER_HOMING.name: EVENT_GCODE_AFTER_HOMING
EVENT_GCODE_AFTER_HOMING.comment: Commands to run after G28 (and move to XY_AFTER_HOMING)

X_SAFETY_STOP.name: X_SAFETY_STOP
X_SAFETY_STOP.comment:
Safety Stops
If an axis has endstops on both ends the one specified above is used for
homing, while the other can be used for things like SD_ABORT_ON_ENDSTOP_HIT.

BED_CENTER_AT_0_0.name: BED_CENTER_AT_0_0
BED_CENTER_AT_0_0.comment: The center of the bed is at (X=0, Y=0)

MANUAL_X_HOME_POS.name: MANUAL_X_HOME_POS
MANUAL_X_HOME_POS.comment:
Manually set the home position. Leave these undefined for automatic settings.
For DELTA this is the top-center of the Cartesian print volume.

Z_SAFE_HOMING.name: Z_SAFE_HOMING
Z_SAFE_HOMING.comment:
Use "Z Safe Homing" to avoid homing with a Z probe outside the bed area.

- Moves the Z probe (or nozzle) to a defined XY point before Z homing.
- Allows Z homing only when XY positions are known and trusted.
- If stepper drivers sleep, XY homing may be required again before Z homing.

Z_SAFE_HOMING_POINT_ABSOLUTE.name: Z_SAFE_HOMING_POINT_ABSOLUTE
Z_SAFE_HOMING_POINT_ABSOLUTE.comment: Ignore home offsets (M206) for Z homing position

EDITABLE_HOMING_FEEDRATE.name: EDITABLE_HOMING_FEEDRATE
EDITABLE_HOMING_FEEDRATE.comment: Edit homing feedrates with M210 and MarlinUI menu items

VALIDATE_HOMING_ENDSTOPS.name: VALIDATE_HOMING_ENDSTOPS
VALIDATE_HOMING_ENDSTOPS.comment: Validate that endstops are triggered on homing moves

X_BED_SIZE.name: X_BED_SIZE
X_BED_SIZE.comment: The size of the printable area

X_MIN_POS.name: X_MIN_POS
X_MIN_POS.comment: Travel limits (linear=mm, rotational=°) after homing, corresponding to endstop positions.

MIN_SOFTWARE_ENDSTOPS.name: MIN_SOFTWARE_ENDSTOPS
MIN_SOFTWARE_ENDSTOPS.comment: Min software endstops constrain movement within minimum coordinate bounds

MAX_SOFTWARE_ENDSTOPS.name: MAX_SOFTWARE_ENDSTOPS
MAX_SOFTWARE_ENDSTOPS.comment: Max software endstops constrain movement within maximum coordinate bounds

SOFT_ENDSTOPS_MENU_ITEM.name: SOFT_ENDSTOPS_MENU_ITEM
SOFT_ENDSTOPS_MENU_ITEM.comment: Enable/Disable software endstops from the LCD

FILAMENT_RUNOUT_SENSOR.name: FILAMENT_RUNOUT_SENSOR
FILAMENT_RUNOUT_SENSOR.comment:
Filament Runout Sensors
Mechanical or opto endstops are used to check for the presence of filament.

IMPORTANT: Runout will only trigger if Marlin is aware that a print job is running.
Marlin knows a print job is running when:
 1. Running a print job from media started with M24.
 2. The Print Job Timer has been started with M75.
 3. The heaters were turned on and PRINTJOB_TIMER_AUTOSTART is enabled.

RAMPS-based boards use SERVO3_PIN for the first runout sensor.
For other boards you may need to define FIL_RUNOUT_PIN, FIL_RUNOUT2_PIN, etc.

FIL_RUNOUT_ENABLED_DEFAULT.name: FIL_RUNOUT_ENABLED_DEFAULT
FIL_RUNOUT_ENABLED_DEFAULT.comment: Enable the sensor on startup. Override with M412 followed by M500.

NUM_RUNOUT_SENSORS.name: NUM_RUNOUT_SENSORS
NUM_RUNOUT_SENSORS.comment: Number of sensors, up to one per extruder. Define a FIL_RUNOUT#_PIN for each.

FIL_RUNOUT_STATE.name: FIL_RUNOUT_STATE
FIL_RUNOUT_STATE.comment: Pin state indicating that filament is NOT present.

FIL_RUNOUT_PULLUP.name: FIL_RUNOUT_PULLUP
FIL_RUNOUT_PULLUP.comment: Use internal pullup for filament runout pins.

FIL_RUNOUT_PULLDOWN.name: FIL_RUNOUT_PULLDOWN
FIL_RUNOUT_PULLDOWN.comment: Use internal pulldown for filament runout pins.

WATCH_ALL_RUNOUT_SENSORS.name: WATCH_ALL_RUNOUT_SENSORS
WATCH_ALL_RUNOUT_SENSORS.comment: Execute runout script on any triggering sensor, not only for the active extruder. This is automatically enabled for MIXING_EXTRUDERs.

FIL_RUNOUT1_STATE.name: FIL_RUNOUT1_STATE
FIL_RUNOUT1_STATE.comment: Override individually if the runout sensors vary

FILAMENT_RUNOUT_SCRIPT.name: FILAMENT_RUNOUT_SCRIPT
FILAMENT_RUNOUT_SCRIPT.comment:
Commands to execute on filament runout.
With multiple runout sensors use the %c placeholder for the current tool in commands (e.g., "M600 T%c")
NOTE: After 'M412 H1' the host handles filament runout and this script does not apply.

FILAMENT_RUNOUT_DISTANCE_MM.name: FILAMENT_RUNOUT_DISTANCE_MM
FILAMENT_RUNOUT_DISTANCE_MM.comment:
After a runout is detected, continue printing this length of filament
before executing the runout script. Useful for a sensor at the end of
a feed tube. Requires 4 bytes SRAM per sensor, plus 4 bytes overhead.

FILAMENT_MOTION_SENSOR.name: FILAMENT_MOTION_SENSOR
FILAMENT_MOTION_SENSOR.comment:
Enable this option to use an encoder disc that toggles the runout pin
as the filament moves. (Be sure to set FILAMENT_RUNOUT_DISTANCE_MM
large enough to avoid false positives.)

FILAMENT_SWITCH_AND_MOTION.name: FILAMENT_SWITCH_AND_MOTION
FILAMENT_SWITCH_AND_MOTION.comment: Define separate pins below to sense motion

FILAMENT_MOTION_DISTANCE_MM.name: FILAMENT_MOTION_DISTANCE_MM
FILAMENT_MOTION_DISTANCE_MM.comment: (mm) Missing distance required to trigger runout

NUM_MOTION_SENSORS.name: NUM_MOTION_SENSORS
NUM_MOTION_SENSORS.comment: Number of sensors, up to one per extruder. Define a FIL_MOTION#_PIN for each.

FIL_MOTION1_STATE.name: FIL_MOTION1_STATE
FIL_MOTION1_STATE.comment: Override individually if the motion sensors vary

AUTO_BED_LEVELING_3POINT.name: AUTO_BED_LEVELING_3POINT
AUTO_BED_LEVELING_3POINT.comment:
Choose one of the options below to enable G29 Bed Leveling. The parameters
and behavior of G29 will change depending on your selection.

 If using a Probe for Z Homing, enable Z_SAFE_HOMING also!

- AUTO_BED_LEVELING_3POINT
  Probe 3 arbitrary points on the bed (that aren't collinear)
  You specify the XY coordinates of all 3 points.
  The result is a single tilted plane. Best for a flat bed.

- AUTO_BED_LEVELING_LINEAR
  Probe several points in a grid.
  You specify the rectangle and the density of sample points.
  The result is a single tilted plane. Best for a flat bed.

- AUTO_BED_LEVELING_BILINEAR
  Probe several points in a grid.
  You specify the rectangle and the density of sample points.
  The result is a mesh, best for large or uneven beds.

- AUTO_BED_LEVELING_UBL (Unified Bed Leveling)
  A comprehensive bed leveling system combining the features and benefits
  of other systems. UBL also includes integrated Mesh Generation, Mesh
  Validation and Mesh Editing systems.

- MESH_BED_LEVELING
  Probe a grid manually
  The result is a mesh, suitable for large or uneven beds. (See BILINEAR.)
  For machines without a probe, Mesh Bed Leveling provides a method to perform
  leveling in steps so you can manually adjust the Z height at each grid-point.
  With an LCD controller the process is guided step-by-step.

EVENT_GCODE_BEFORE_G29.name: EVENT_GCODE_BEFORE_G29
EVENT_GCODE_BEFORE_G29.comment:
Commands to execute at the start of G29 probing,
after switching to the PROBING_TOOL.

EVENT_GCODE_AFTER_G29.name: EVENT_GCODE_AFTER_G29
EVENT_GCODE_AFTER_G29.comment:
Commands to execute at the end of G29 probing.
Useful to retract or move the Z probe out of the way.

RESTORE_LEVELING_AFTER_G28.name: RESTORE_LEVELING_AFTER_G28
RESTORE_LEVELING_AFTER_G28.comment:
Normally G28 leaves leveling disabled on completion. Enable one of
these options to restore the prior leveling state or to always enable
leveling immediately after G28.

LEVELING_NOZZLE_TEMP.name: LEVELING_NOZZLE_TEMP
LEVELING_NOZZLE_TEMP.comment: (°C) Only applies to E0 at this time

DEBUG_LEVELING_FEATURE.name: DEBUG_LEVELING_FEATURE
DEBUG_LEVELING_FEATURE.comment:
Enable detailed logging of G28, G29, M48, etc.
Turn on with the command 'M111 S32'.
NOTE: Requires a lot of flash!

MANUAL_PROBE_START_Z.name: MANUAL_PROBE_START_Z
MANUAL_PROBE_START_Z.comment: Set a height for the start of manual adjustment
MANUAL_PROBE_START_Z.notes: (mm) Comment out to use the last-measured height

ENABLE_LEVELING_FADE_HEIGHT.name: ENABLE_LEVELING_FADE_HEIGHT
ENABLE_LEVELING_FADE_HEIGHT.comment:
Gradually reduce leveling correction until a set height is reached,
at which point movement will be level to the machine's XY plane.
The height can be set with M420 Z<height>

SEGMENT_LEVELED_MOVES.name: SEGMENT_LEVELED_MOVES
SEGMENT_LEVELED_MOVES.comment:
For Cartesian machines, instead of dividing moves on mesh boundaries,
split up moves into short segments like a Delta. This follows the
contours of the bed more closely than edge-to-edge straight moves.

LEVELED_SEGMENT_LENGTH.name: LEVELED_SEGMENT_LENGTH
LEVELED_SEGMENT_LENGTH.comment: (mm) Length of all segments (except the last one)

G26_MESH_VALIDATION.name: G26_MESH_VALIDATION
G26_MESH_VALIDATION.comment: Enable the G26 Mesh Validation Pattern tool.

G26_XY_FEEDRATE_TRAVEL.name: G26_XY_FEEDRATE_TRAVEL
G26_XY_FEEDRATE_TRAVEL.comment: (mm/s) Feedrate for G26 XY travel moves.

G26_RETRACT_MULTIPLIER.name: G26_RETRACT_MULTIPLIER
G26_RETRACT_MULTIPLIER.comment: G26 Q (retraction) used by default between mesh test elements.

GRID_MAX_POINTS_X.name: GRID_MAX_POINTS_X
GRID_MAX_POINTS_X.comment: Set the number of grid points per dimension.

PROBE_Y_FIRST.name: PROBE_Y_FIRST
PROBE_Y_FIRST.comment: Probe along the Y axis, advancing X after each column

EXTRAPOLATE_BEYOND_GRID.name: EXTRAPOLATE_BEYOND_GRID
EXTRAPOLATE_BEYOND_GRID.comment:
Beyond the probed grid, continue the implied tilt?
Default is to maintain the height of the nearest edge.

ABL_BILINEAR_SUBDIVISION.name: ABL_BILINEAR_SUBDIVISION
ABL_BILINEAR_SUBDIVISION.comment:
Subdivision of the grid by Catmull-Rom method.
Synthesizes intermediate points to produce a more detailed mesh.

BILINEAR_SUBDIVISIONS.name: BILINEAR_SUBDIVISIONS
BILINEAR_SUBDIVISIONS.comment: Number of subdivisions between probe points

MESH_EDIT_GFX_OVERLAY.name: MESH_EDIT_GFX_OVERLAY
MESH_EDIT_GFX_OVERLAY.comment: Display a graphics overlay while editing the mesh

MESH_INSET.name: MESH_INSET
MESH_INSET.comment: Set Mesh bounds as an inset region of the bed

UBL_HILBERT_CURVE.name: UBL_HILBERT_CURVE
UBL_HILBERT_CURVE.comment: Use Hilbert distribution for less travel when probing multiple points

UBL_TILT_ON_MESH_POINTS.name: UBL_TILT_ON_MESH_POINTS
UBL_TILT_ON_MESH_POINTS.comment: Use nearest mesh points with G29 J for better Z reference

UBL_TILT_ON_MESH_POINTS_3POINT.name: UBL_TILT_ON_MESH_POINTS_3POINT
UBL_TILT_ON_MESH_POINTS_3POINT.comment: Use nearest mesh points with G29 J0 (3-point)

UBL_MESH_EDIT_MOVES_Z.name: UBL_MESH_EDIT_MOVES_Z
UBL_MESH_EDIT_MOVES_Z.comment: Sophisticated users prefer no movement of nozzle

UBL_SAVE_ACTIVE_ON_M500.name: UBL_SAVE_ACTIVE_ON_M500
UBL_SAVE_ACTIVE_ON_M500.comment: Save the currently active mesh in the current slot on M500

UBL_Z_RAISE_WHEN_OFF_MESH.name: UBL_Z_RAISE_WHEN_OFF_MESH
UBL_Z_RAISE_WHEN_OFF_MESH.comment: When the nozzle is off the mesh, this value is used as the Z-Height correction value.

UBL_MESH_WIZARD.name: UBL_MESH_WIZARD
UBL_MESH_WIZARD.comment: Run several commands in a row to get a complete mesh

AVOID_OBSTACLES.name: AVOID_OBSTACLES
AVOID_OBSTACLES.comment: Probing not allowed within the position of an obstacle.

CLIP_W.name: CLIP_W
CLIP_W.comment: Bed clip width, should be padded a few mm over its physical size

CLIP_H.name: CLIP_H
CLIP_H.comment: Bed clip height, should be padded a few mm over its physical size

OBSTACLE1.name: OBSTACLE1
OBSTACLE1.comment: Obstacle Rectangles defined as { X1, Y1, X2, Y2 }

G29J_MESH_TILT_MARGIN.name: G29J_MESH_TILT_MARGIN
G29J_MESH_TILT_MARGIN.comment:
The probed grid must be inset for G29 J. This is okay, since it is
only used to compute a linear transformation for the mesh itself.

MESH_G28_REST_ORIGIN.name: MESH_G28_REST_ORIGIN
MESH_G28_REST_ORIGIN.comment: After homing all axes ('G28' or 'G28 XYZ') rest Z at Z_MIN_POS

LCD_BED_LEVELING.name: LCD_BED_LEVELING
LCD_BED_LEVELING.comment:
Add a bed leveling sub-menu for ABL or MBL.
Include a guided procedure if manual probing is enabled.

MESH_EDIT_Z_STEP.name: MESH_EDIT_Z_STEP
MESH_EDIT_Z_STEP.comment: (mm) Step size while manually probing Z axis.

LCD_PROBE_Z_RANGE.name: LCD_PROBE_Z_RANGE
LCD_PROBE_Z_RANGE.comment: (mm) Z Range centered on Z_MIN_POS for LCD Z adjustment

MESH_EDIT_MENU.name: MESH_EDIT_MENU
MESH_EDIT_MENU.comment: Add a menu to edit mesh points

LCD_BED_TRAMMING.name: LCD_BED_TRAMMING
LCD_BED_TRAMMING.comment: Add a menu item to move between bed corners for manual bed adjustment

BED_TRAMMING_HEIGHT.name: BED_TRAMMING_HEIGHT
BED_TRAMMING_HEIGHT.comment: (mm) Z height of nozzle at tramming points

BED_TRAMMING_INCLUDE_CENTER.name: BED_TRAMMING_INCLUDE_CENTER
BED_TRAMMING_INCLUDE_CENTER.comment: Move to the center after the last corner

BED_TRAMMING_VERIFY_RAISED.name: BED_TRAMMING_VERIFY_RAISED
BED_TRAMMING_VERIFY_RAISED.comment: After adjustment triggers the probe, re-probe to verify

BED_TRAMMING_LEVELING_ORDER.name: BED_TRAMMING_LEVELING_ORDER
BED_TRAMMING_LEVELING_ORDER.comment:
Corner Leveling Order

Set 2 or 4 points. When 2 points are given, the 3rd is the center of the opposite edge.

 LF  Left-Front    RF  Right-Front
 LB  Left-Back     RB  Right-Back

Examples:

     Default        {LF,RB,LB,RF}         {LF,RF}           {LB,LF}
 LB --------- RB   LB --------- RB    LB --------- RB   LB --------- RB
 |  4       3  |   | 3         2 |    |     <3>     |   | 1           |
 |             |   |             |    |             |   |          <3>|
 |  1       2  |   | 1         4 |    | 1         2 |   | 2           |
 LF --------- RF   LF --------- RF    LF --------- RF   LF --------- RF

SKEW_CORRECTION.name: SKEW_CORRECTION
SKEW_CORRECTION.comment:
Bed Skew Compensation

This feature corrects for misalignment in the XYZ axes.

Take the following steps to get the bed skew in the XY plane:
 1. Print a test square (e.g., https://www.thingiverse.com/thing:2563185)
 2. For XY_DIAG_AC measure the diagonal A to C
 3. For XY_DIAG_BD measure the diagonal B to D
 4. For XY_SIDE_AD measure the edge A to D

Marlin automatically computes skew factors from these measurements.
Skew factors may also be computed and set manually:

 - Compute AB     : SQRT(2*AC*AC+2*BD*BD-4*AD*AD)/2
 - XY_SKEW_FACTOR : TAN(PI/2-ACOS((AC*AC-AB*AB-AD*AD)/(2*AB*AD)))

If desired, follow the same procedure for XZ and YZ.
Use these diagrams for reference:

   Y                     Z                     Z
   ^     B-------C       ^     B-------C       ^     B-------C
   |    /       /        |    /       /        |    /       /
   |   /       /         |   /       /         |   /       /
   |  A-------D          |  A-------D          |  A-------D
   +-------------->X     +-------------->X     +-------------->Y
    XY_SKEW_FACTOR        XZ_SKEW_FACTOR        YZ_SKEW_FACTOR

XY_SKEW_FACTOR.name: XY_SKEW_FACTOR
XY_SKEW_FACTOR.comment: Or, set the XY skew factor directly:

XZ_SKEW_FACTOR.name: XZ_SKEW_FACTOR
XZ_SKEW_FACTOR.comment: Or, set the Z skew factors directly:

SKEW_CORRECTION_GCODE.name: SKEW_CORRECTION_GCODE
SKEW_CORRECTION_GCODE.comment: Enable this option for M852 to set skew at runtime

EEPROM_SETTINGS.name: EEPROM_SETTINGS
EEPROM_SETTINGS.comment:
EEPROM

Persistent storage to preserve configurable settings across reboots.

  M500 - Store settings to EEPROM.
  M501 - Read settings from EEPROM. (i.e., Throw away unsaved changes)
  M502 - Revert settings to "factory" defaults. (Follow with M500 to init the EEPROM.)
EEPROM_SETTINGS.notes: Persistent storage with M500 and M501

DISABLE_M503.name: DISABLE_M503
DISABLE_M503.comment: Saves ~2700 bytes of flash. Disable for release!

EEPROM_CHITCHAT.name: EEPROM_CHITCHAT
EEPROM_CHITCHAT.comment: Give feedback on EEPROM commands. Disable to save flash.

EEPROM_BOOT_SILENT.name: EEPROM_BOOT_SILENT
EEPROM_BOOT_SILENT.comment: Keep M503 quiet and only give errors during first load

EEPROM_AUTO_INIT.name: EEPROM_AUTO_INIT
EEPROM_AUTO_INIT.comment: Init EEPROM automatically on any errors.

EEPROM_INIT_NOW.name: EEPROM_INIT_NOW
EEPROM_INIT_NOW.comment: Init EEPROM on first boot after a new build.

HOST_KEEPALIVE_FEATURE.name: HOST_KEEPALIVE_FEATURE
HOST_KEEPALIVE_FEATURE.comment:
Host Keepalive

When enabled Marlin will send a busy status message to the host
every couple of seconds when it can't accept commands.
HOST_KEEPALIVE_FEATURE.notes: Disable this if your host doesn't like keepalive messages

DEFAULT_KEEPALIVE_INTERVAL.name: DEFAULT_KEEPALIVE_INTERVAL
DEFAULT_KEEPALIVE_INTERVAL.comment: Number of seconds between "busy" messages. Set with M113.

BUSY_WHILE_HEATING.name: BUSY_WHILE_HEATING
BUSY_WHILE_HEATING.comment: Some hosts require "busy" messages even during heating

PRINTJOB_TIMER_AUTOSTART.name: PRINTJOB_TIMER_AUTOSTART
PRINTJOB_TIMER_AUTOSTART.comment:
Print Job Timer

Automatically start and stop the print job timer on M104/M109/M140/M190/M141/M191.
The print job timer will only be stopped if the bed/chamber target temp is
below BED_MINTEMP/CHAMBER_MINTEMP.

  M104 (hotend, no wait)  - high temp = none,        low temp = stop timer
  M109 (hotend, wait)     - high temp = start timer, low temp = stop timer
  M140 (bed, no wait)     - high temp = none,        low temp = stop timer
  M190 (bed, wait)        - high temp = start timer, low temp = none
  M141 (chamber, no wait) - high temp = none,        low temp = stop timer
  M191 (chamber, wait)    - high temp = start timer, low temp = none

For M104/M109, high temp is anything over EXTRUDE_MINTEMP / 2.
For M140/M190, high temp is anything over BED_MINTEMP.
For M141/M191, high temp is anything over CHAMBER_MINTEMP.

The timer can also be controlled with the following commands:

  M75 - Start the print job timer
  M76 - Pause the print job timer
  M77 - Stop the print job timer

PREHEAT_1_LABEL.name: PREHEAT_1_LABEL
PREHEAT_1_LABEL.comment: Preheat Constants - Up to 10 are supported without changes

NOZZLE_PARK_FEATURE.name: NOZZLE_PARK_FEATURE
NOZZLE_PARK_FEATURE.comment:
Nozzle Park

Park the nozzle at the given XYZ position on idle or G27.

The "P" parameter controls the action applied to the Z axis:

   P0  (Default) If Z is below park Z raise the nozzle.
   P1  Raise the nozzle always to Z-park height.
   P2  Raise the nozzle by Z-park amount, limited to Z_MAX_POS.

NOZZLE_PARK_POINT.name: NOZZLE_PARK_POINT
NOZZLE_PARK_POINT.comment: Specify a park position as { X, Y, Z_raise }

NOZZLE_PARK_MOVE.name: NOZZLE_PARK_MOVE
NOZZLE_PARK_MOVE.comment: Park motion: 0 = XY Move, 1 = X Only, 2 = Y Only, 3 = X before Y, 4 = Y before X

NOZZLE_PARK_Z_RAISE_MIN.name: NOZZLE_PARK_Z_RAISE_MIN
NOZZLE_PARK_Z_RAISE_MIN.comment: (mm) Always raise Z by at least this distance

NOZZLE_PARK_XY_FEEDRATE.name: NOZZLE_PARK_XY_FEEDRATE
NOZZLE_PARK_XY_FEEDRATE.comment: (mm/s) X and Y axes feedrate (also used for delta Z axis)

NOZZLE_PARK_Z_FEEDRATE.name: NOZZLE_PARK_Z_FEEDRATE
NOZZLE_PARK_Z_FEEDRATE.comment: (mm/s) Z axis feedrate (not used for delta printers)

NOZZLE_CLEAN_FEATURE.name: NOZZLE_CLEAN_FEATURE
NOZZLE_CLEAN_FEATURE.comment:
Clean Nozzle Feature

Adds the G12 command to perform a nozzle cleaning process.

Parameters:
  P  Pattern
  S  Strokes / Repetitions
  T  Triangles (P1 only)

Patterns:
  P0  Straight line (default). This process requires a sponge type material
      at a fixed bed location. "S" specifies strokes (i.e. back-forth motions)
      between the start / end points.

  P1  Zig-zag pattern between (X0, Y0) and (X1, Y1), "T" specifies the
      number of zig-zag triangles to do. "S" defines the number of strokes.
      Zig-zags are done in whichever is the narrower dimension.
      For example, "G12 P1 S1 T3" will execute:

         --
        |  (X0, Y1) |     /\        /\        /\     | (X1, Y1)
        |           |    /  \      /  \      /  \    |
      A |           |   /    \    /    \    /    \   |
        |           |  /      \  /      \  /      \  |
        |  (X0, Y0) | /        \/        \/        \ | (X1, Y0)
         --         +--------------------------------+
                      |________|_________|_________|
                          T1        T2        T3

  P2  Circular pattern with middle at NOZZLE_CLEAN_CIRCLE_MIDDLE.
      "R" specifies the radius. "S" specifies the stroke count.
      Before starting, the nozzle moves to NOZZLE_CLEAN_START_POINT.

  Caveats: The ending Z should be the same as starting Z.

NOZZLE_CLEAN_PATTERN_LINE.name: NOZZLE_CLEAN_PATTERN_LINE
NOZZLE_CLEAN_PATTERN_LINE.comment: Provide 'G12 P0' - a simple linear cleaning pattern

NOZZLE_CLEAN_PATTERN_ZIGZAG.name: NOZZLE_CLEAN_PATTERN_ZIGZAG
NOZZLE_CLEAN_PATTERN_ZIGZAG.comment: Provide 'G12 P1' - a zigzag cleaning pattern

NOZZLE_CLEAN_PATTERN_CIRCLE.name: NOZZLE_CLEAN_PATTERN_CIRCLE
NOZZLE_CLEAN_PATTERN_CIRCLE.comment: Provide 'G12 P2' - a circular cleaning pattern

NOZZLE_CLEAN_DEFAULT_PATTERN.name: NOZZLE_CLEAN_DEFAULT_PATTERN
NOZZLE_CLEAN_DEFAULT_PATTERN.comment: Default pattern to use when 'P' is not provided to G12. One of the enabled options above.

NOZZLE_CLEAN_START_POINT.name: NOZZLE_CLEAN_START_POINT
NOZZLE_CLEAN_START_POINT.comment:
Specify positions for each tool as { { X, Y, Z }, { X, Y, Z } }
Dual hotend system may use { {  -20, (Y_BED_SIZE / 2), (Z_MIN_POS + 1) },  {  420, (Y_BED_SIZE / 2), (Z_MIN_POS + 1) }}

NOZZLE_CLEAN_CIRCLE_FN.name: NOZZLE_CLEAN_CIRCLE_FN
NOZZLE_CLEAN_CIRCLE_FN.comment: Circular pattern circle number of segments

NOZZLE_CLEAN_GOBACK.name: NOZZLE_CLEAN_GOBACK
NOZZLE_CLEAN_GOBACK.comment: Move the nozzle to the initial position after cleaning

NOZZLE_CLEAN_NO_Z.name: NOZZLE_CLEAN_NO_Z
NOZZLE_CLEAN_NO_Z.comment: For a purge/clean station that's always at the gantry height (thus no Z move)

NOZZLE_CLEAN_NO_Y.name: NOZZLE_CLEAN_NO_Y
NOZZLE_CLEAN_NO_Y.comment: For a purge/clean station mounted on the X axis

NOZZLE_CLEAN_MIN_TEMP.name: NOZZLE_CLEAN_MIN_TEMP
NOZZLE_CLEAN_MIN_TEMP.comment: Require a minimum hotend temperature for cleaning

NOZZLE_CLEAN_HEATUP.name: NOZZLE_CLEAN_HEATUP
NOZZLE_CLEAN_HEATUP.comment: Heat up the nozzle instead of skipping wipe

WIPE_SEQUENCE_COMMANDS.name: WIPE_SEQUENCE_COMMANDS
WIPE_SEQUENCE_COMMANDS.comment: Explicit wipe G-code script applies to a G12 with no arguments.

PRINTCOUNTER.name: PRINTCOUNTER
PRINTCOUNTER.comment:
Print Counter

Track statistical data such as:

 - Total print jobs
 - Total successful print jobs
 - Total failed print jobs
 - Total time printing

View the current statistics with M78.

PRINTCOUNTER_SAVE_INTERVAL.name: PRINTCOUNTER_SAVE_INTERVAL
PRINTCOUNTER_SAVE_INTERVAL.comment: (minutes) EEPROM save interval during print. A value of 0 will save stats at end of print.

PASSWORD_FEATURE.name: PASSWORD_FEATURE
PASSWORD_FEATURE.comment:
Password

Set a numerical password for the printer which can be requested:

 - When the printer boots up
 - Upon opening the 'Print from Media' Menu
 - When SD printing is completed or aborted

The following G-codes can be used:

 M510 - Lock Printer. Blocks all commands except M511.
 M511 - Unlock Printer.
 M512 - Set, Change and Remove Password.

If you forget the password and get locked out you'll need to re-flash
the firmware with the feature disabled, reset EEPROM, and (optionally)
re-flash the firmware again with this feature enabled.

PASSWORD_LENGTH.name: PASSWORD_LENGTH
PASSWORD_LENGTH.comment: (#) Number of digits (1-9). 3 or 4 is recommended

PASSWORD_UNLOCK_GCODE.name: PASSWORD_UNLOCK_GCODE
PASSWORD_UNLOCK_GCODE.comment: Unlock with the M511 P<password> command. Disable to prevent brute-force attack.

PASSWORD_CHANGE_GCODE.name: PASSWORD_CHANGE_GCODE
PASSWORD_CHANGE_GCODE.comment: Change the password with M512 P<old> S<new>.

PASSWORD_ON_SD_PRINT_MENU.name: PASSWORD_ON_SD_PRINT_MENU
PASSWORD_ON_SD_PRINT_MENU.comment: This does not prevent G-codes from running

SDSUPPORT.name: SDSUPPORT
SDSUPPORT.comment:
SD CARD

SD Card support is disabled by default. If your controller has an SD slot,
you must uncomment the following option or it won't work.

SD_CHECK_AND_RETRY.name: SD_CHECK_AND_RETRY
SD_CHECK_AND_RETRY.comment:
SD CARD: ENABLE CRC

Use CRC checks and retries on the SD communication.

LCD_LANGUAGE.name: LCD_LANGUAGE
LCD_LANGUAGE.comment:
LCD LANGUAGE

Select the language to display on the LCD. These languages are available:

  en, an, bg, ca, cz, da, de, el, el_CY, es, eu, fi, fr, gl, hg, hr, hu, id, it,
  jp_kana, ko_KR, nl, pl, pt, pt_br, ro, ru, sk, sv, tr, uk, vi, zh_CN, zh_TW

DISPLAY_CHARSET_HD44780.name: DISPLAY_CHARSET_HD44780
DISPLAY_CHARSET_HD44780.comment:
LCD Character Set

Note: This option is NOT applicable to Graphical Displays.

All character-based LCDs provide ASCII plus one of these
language extensions:

 - JAPANESE ... the most common
 - WESTERN  ... with more accented characters
 - CYRILLIC ... for the Russian language

To determine the language extension installed on your controller:

 - Compile and upload with LCD_LANGUAGE set to 'test'
 - Click the controller to view the LCD menu
 - The LCD will display Japanese, Western, or Cyrillic text

See https://marlinfw.org/docs/development/lcd_language.html

NO_LCD_MENUS.name: NO_LCD_MENUS
NO_LCD_MENUS.comment:
LCD Menu Items

Disable all menus and only display the Status Screen, or
just remove some extraneous menu items to recover space.

ENCODER_PULSES_PER_STEP.name: ENCODER_PULSES_PER_STEP
ENCODER_PULSES_PER_STEP.comment:
ENCODER SETTINGS

This option overrides the default number of encoder pulses needed to
produce one step. Should be increased for high-resolution encoders.

ENCODER_STEPS_PER_MENU_ITEM.name: ENCODER_STEPS_PER_MENU_ITEM
ENCODER_STEPS_PER_MENU_ITEM.comment:
Use this option to override the number of step signals required to
move between next/prev menu items.

REVERSE_ENCODER_DIRECTION.name: REVERSE_ENCODER_DIRECTION
REVERSE_ENCODER_DIRECTION.comment:
This option reverses the encoder direction everywhere.

Set this option if CLOCKWISE causes values to DECREASE

REVERSE_MENU_DIRECTION.name: REVERSE_MENU_DIRECTION
REVERSE_MENU_DIRECTION.comment:
This option reverses the encoder direction for navigating LCD menus.

If CLOCKWISE normally moves DOWN this makes it go UP.
If CLOCKWISE normally moves UP this makes it go DOWN.

REVERSE_SELECT_DIRECTION.name: REVERSE_SELECT_DIRECTION
REVERSE_SELECT_DIRECTION.comment:
This option reverses the encoder direction for Select Screen.

If CLOCKWISE normally moves LEFT this makes it go RIGHT.
If CLOCKWISE normally moves RIGHT this makes it go LEFT.

ENCODER_NOISE_FILTER.name: ENCODER_NOISE_FILTER
ENCODER_NOISE_FILTER.comment:
Encoder EMI Noise Filter

This option increases encoder samples to filter out phantom encoder clicks caused by EMI noise.

INDIVIDUAL_AXIS_HOMING_MENU.name: INDIVIDUAL_AXIS_HOMING_MENU
INDIVIDUAL_AXIS_HOMING_MENU.comment:
Individual Axis Homing

Add individual axis homing items (Home X, Home Y, and Home Z) to the LCD menu.

SPEAKER.name: SPEAKER
SPEAKER.comment:
SPEAKER/BUZZER

If you have a speaker that can produce tones, enable it here.
By default Marlin assumes you have a buzzer with a fixed frequency.

LCD_FEEDBACK_FREQUENCY_DURATION_MS.name: LCD_FEEDBACK_FREQUENCY_DURATION_MS
LCD_FEEDBACK_FREQUENCY_DURATION_MS.comment:
The duration and frequency for the UI feedback sound.
Set these to 0 to disable audio feedback in the LCD menus.

Note: Test audio output with the G-Code:
M300 S<frequency Hz> P<duration ms>

TONE_QUEUE_LENGTH.name: TONE_QUEUE_LENGTH
TONE_QUEUE_LENGTH.comment:
Tone queue size, used to keep beeps from blocking execution.
Default is 4, or override here. Costs 4 bytes of SRAM per entry.

STARTUP_TUNE.name: STARTUP_TUNE
STARTUP_TUNE.comment:
A sequence of tones to play at startup, in pairs of tone (Hz), duration (ms).
Silence in-between tones.

REPRAP_DISCOUNT_SMART_CONTROLLER.name: REPRAP_DISCOUNT_SMART_CONTROLLER
REPRAP_DISCOUNT_SMART_CONTROLLER.comment:
RepRapDiscount Smart Controller.
https://reprap.org/wiki/RepRapDiscount_Smart_Controller

Note: Usually sold with a white PCB.

YHCB2004.name: YHCB2004
YHCB2004.comment:
GT2560 (YHCB2004) LCD Display

Requires Testato, Koepel softwarewire library and
Andriy Golovnya's LiquidCrystal_AIP31068 library.

PANEL_ONE.name: PANEL_ONE
PANEL_ONE.comment:
PanelOne from T3P3 (via RAMPS 1.4 AUX2/AUX3)
https://reprap.org/wiki/PanelOne

G3D_PANEL.name: G3D_PANEL
G3D_PANEL.comment:
GADGETS3D G3D LCD/SD Controller
https://reprap.org/wiki/RAMPS_1.3/1.4_GADGETS3D_Shield_with_Panel

Note: Usually sold with a blue PCB.

MAKEBOARD_MINI_2_LINE_DISPLAY_1602.name: MAKEBOARD_MINI_2_LINE_DISPLAY_1602
MAKEBOARD_MINI_2_LINE_DISPLAY_1602.comment:
Makeboard 3D Printer Parts 3D Printer Mini Display 1602 Mini Controller
https://www.aliexpress.com/item/32765887917.html

ZONESTAR_LCD.name: ZONESTAR_LCD
ZONESTAR_LCD.comment:
ANET and Tronxy 20x4 Controller
LCD2004 display with 5 analog buttons.

NOTE: Requires ADC_KEYPAD_PIN to be assigned to an analog pin.
This LCD is known to be susceptible to electrical interference which
scrambles the display. Press any button to clear it up.

LCD_SAINSMART_I2C_1602.name: LCD_SAINSMART_I2C_1602
LCD_SAINSMART_I2C_1602.comment:
Sainsmart (YwRobot) LCD Displays

These require LiquidCrystal_I2C library:
https://github.com/MarlinFirmware/New-LiquidCrystal
https://github.com/fmalpartida/New-LiquidCrystal/wiki

LCD_I2C_PANELOLU2.name: LCD_I2C_PANELOLU2
LCD_I2C_PANELOLU2.comment:
PANELOLU2 LCD with status LEDs,
separate encoder and click inputs.

Note: This controller requires Arduino's LiquidTWI2 library v1.2.3 or later.
For more info: https://github.com/lincomatic/LiquidTWI2

Note: The PANELOLU2 encoder click input can either be directly connected to
a pin (if BTN_ENC defined to != -1) or read through I2C (when BTN_ENC == -1).

LCD_I2C_VIKI.name: LCD_I2C_VIKI
LCD_I2C_VIKI.comment:
Panucatt VIKI LCD with status LEDs,
integrated click & L/R/U/D buttons, separate encoder inputs.

SAV_3DLCD.name: SAV_3DLCD
SAV_3DLCD.comment:
2-wire Non-latching LCD SR from https://github.com/fmalpartida/New-LiquidCrystal/wiki/schematics#user-content-ShiftRegister_connection
LCD configuration: https://reprap.org/wiki/SAV_3D_LCD

FF_INTERFACEBOARD.name: FF_INTERFACEBOARD
FF_INTERFACEBOARD.comment:
3-wire SR LCD with strobe using 74HC4094
https://github.com/mikeshub/SailfishLCD
Uses the code directly from Sailfish

MIGHTYBOARD_DEBUG.name: MIGHTYBOARD_DEBUG
MIGHTYBOARD_DEBUG.comment: Lightweight debug output for buttons and encoder

MIGHTYBOARD_DISABLE_ENC_PULLUP.name: MIGHTYBOARD_DISABLE_ENC_PULLUP
MIGHTYBOARD_DISABLE_ENC_PULLUP.comment: Enable if the encoder button is unreliable

MIGHTYBOARD_BUTTON_PULLUPS.name: MIGHTYBOARD_BUTTON_PULLUPS
MIGHTYBOARD_BUTTON_PULLUPS.comment: Enable if other buttons are unreliable

MIGHTYBOARD_BACK_STATUS_BUTTONS.name: MIGHTYBOARD_BACK_STATUS_BUTTONS
MIGHTYBOARD_BACK_STATUS_BUTTONS.comment: Use LEFT/RIGHT buttons for Back / Home. Otherwise they act like encoder down / up.

TFTGLCD_PANEL_SPI.name: TFTGLCD_PANEL_SPI
TFTGLCD_PANEL_SPI.comment:
TFT GLCD Panel with Marlin UI

WYH_L12864.comment: Alfawise Ex8 printer LCD marked as WYH L12864 COG

MKS_MINI_12864.comment:
MKS MINI12864 with graphic controller and SD support
https://reprap.org/wiki/MKS_MINI_12864

MKS_MINI_12864_V3.name: MKS_MINI_12864_V3
MKS_MINI_12864_V3.comment: MKS MINI12864 V3 is an alias for FYSETC_MINI_12864_2_1. Type A/B. NeoPixel RGB Backlight.

MKS_LCD12864A.name: MKS_LCD12864A
MKS_LCD12864A.comment:
MKS LCD12864A/B with graphic controller and SD support. Follows MKS_MINI_12864 pinout.
https://www.aliexpress.com/item/33018110072.html

FYSETC_MINI_12864_X_X.name: FYSETC_MINI_12864_X_X
FYSETC_MINI_12864_X_X.comment:
FYSETC variant of the MINI12864 graphic controller with SD support
https://wiki.fysetc.com/docs/Mini12864Panel
FYSETC_MINI_12864_X_X.notes: Type C/D/E/F. No tunable RGB Backlight by default

FYSETC_MINI_12864_1_2.name: FYSETC_MINI_12864_1_2
FYSETC_MINI_12864_1_2.comment: Type C/D/E/F. Simple RGB Backlight (always on)

FYSETC_GENERIC_12864_1_1.name: FYSETC_GENERIC_12864_1_1
FYSETC_GENERIC_12864_1_1.comment: Larger display with basic ON/OFF backlight.

BTT_MINI_12864.comment:
BigTreeTech Mini 12864 V1.0 / V2.0 is an alias for FYSETC_MINI_12864_2_1. Type A/B. NeoPixel RGB Backlight.

BEEZ_MINI_12864.comment: BEEZ MINI 12864 is an alias for FYSETC_MINI_12864_2_1. Type A/B. NeoPixel RGB Backlight.

CR10_STOCKDISPLAY.comment:
Factory display for Creality CR-10 / CR-7 / Ender-3

Connect to EXP1 on RAMPS and compatible boards.

ENDER2_STOCKDISPLAY.name: ENDER2_STOCKDISPLAY
ENDER2_STOCKDISPLAY.comment: Ender-2 OEM display, a variant of the MKS_MINI_12864

ANET_FULL_GRAPHICS_LCD.name: ANET_FULL_GRAPHICS_LCD
ANET_FULL_GRAPHICS_LCD.comment: ANET and Tronxy 128×64 Full Graphics Controller as used on Anet A6

CTC_A10S_A13.name: CTC_A10S_A13
CTC_A10S_A13.comment: GUCOCO CTC 128×64 Full Graphics Controller as used on GUCOCO CTC A10S

U8GLIB_SSD1306.comment: SSD1306 OLED full graphics generic display

SAV_3DGLCD.comment: SAV OLEd LCD module support using either SSD1306 or SH1106 based LCD modules

MKS OLED 1.3" 128×64 Full Graphics Controller

OVERLORD_OLED.name: OVERLORD_OLED
OVERLORD_OLED.comment: Overlord OLED display/controller with i2c buzzer and LEDs

FYSETC_242_OLED_12864.name: FYSETC_242_OLED_12864
FYSETC_242_OLED_12864.comment:
FYSETC OLED 2.42" 128×64 Full Graphics Controller with WS2812 RGB
