K3D_242_OLED_CONTROLLER.name: K3D_242_OLED_CONTROLLER
K3D_242_OLED_CONTROLLER.comment: K.3D SSD1309 OLED 2.42" 128×64 Full Graphics Controller

DGUS_LCD_UI.name: DGUS_LCD_UI
DGUS_LCD_UI.comment:
DGUS Touch Display with DWIN OS. (Choose one.)

ORIGIN (Marlin DWIN_SET)
 - Download https://github.com/coldtobi/Marlin_DGUS_Resources
 - Copy the downloaded DWIN_SET folder to the SD card.
 - Product: https://www.aliexpress.com/item/32993409517.html

FYSETC (Supplier default)
 - Download https://github.com/FYSETC/FYSTLCD-2.0
 - Copy the downloaded SCREEN folder to the SD card.
 - Product: https://www.aliexpress.com/item/32961471929.html

HIPRECY (Supplier default)
 - Download https://github.com/HiPrecy/Touch-Lcd-LEO
 - Copy the downloaded DWIN_SET folder to the SD card.

MKS (MKS-H43) (Supplier default)
 - Download https://github.com/makerbase-mks/MKS-H43
 - Copy the downloaded DWIN_SET folder to the SD card.
 - Product: https://www.aliexpress.com/item/1005002008179262.html

RELOADED (T5UID1)
 - Download https://github.com/Neo2003/DGUS-reloaded/releases
 - Copy the downloaded DWIN_SET folder to the SD card.

IA_CREALITY (T5UID1)
 - Download https://github.com/InsanityAutomation/Marlin/raw/CrealityDwin_2.0/TM3D_Combined480272_Landscape_V7.7z
 - Copy the downloaded DWIN_SET folder to the SD card.

E3S1PRO (T5L)
 - Download https://github.com/CrealityOfficial/Ender-3S1/archive/3S1_Plus_Screen.zip
 - Copy the downloaded DWIN_SET folder to the SD card.

Flash display with DGUS Displays for Marlin:
 - Format the SD card to FAT32 with an allocation size of 4kb.
 - Download files as specified for your type of display.
 - Plug the microSD card into the back of the display.
 - Boot the display and wait for the update to complete.

TOUCH_UI_FTDI_EVE.name: TOUCH_UI_FTDI_EVE
TOUCH_UI_FTDI_EVE.comment:
Touch UI for FTDI EVE (FT800/FT810) displays
See Configuration_adv.h for all configuration options.

ANYCUBIC_LCD_I3MEGA.name: ANYCUBIC_LCD_I3MEGA
ANYCUBIC_LCD_I3MEGA.comment: Touch-screen LCD for Anycubic i3 Mega

ANYCUBIC_LCD_GCODE_EXT.name: ANYCUBIC_LCD_GCODE_EXT
ANYCUBIC_LCD_GCODE_EXT.comment: Add ".gcode" to menu entries for DGUS clone compatibility

NEXTION_TFT.name: NEXTION_TFT
NEXTION_TFT.comment: 320x240 Nextion 2.8" serial TFT Resistive Touch Screen NX3224T028

Third-party or vendor-customized controller interfaces.
Sources should be installed in 'src/lcd/extui'.

EXTUI_LOCAL_BEEPER.name: EXTUI_LOCAL_BEEPER
EXTUI_LOCAL_BEEPER.comment: Enables use of local Beeper pin with external display

TFT_INTERFACE_FSMC.name: TFT_INTERFACE_FSMC
TFT_INTERFACE_FSMC.comment: Interface. Enable one of the following options:

TFT_RES_320x240.name: TFT_RES_320x240
TFT_RES_320x240.comment: TFT Resolution. Enable one of the following options:

TFT_CLASSIC_UI.name: TFT_CLASSIC_UI
TFT_CLASSIC_UI.comment:
TFT UI - User Interface Selection. Enable one of the following options:

  TFT_CLASSIC_UI - Emulated DOGM - 128x64 Upscaled
  TFT_COLOR_UI   - Marlin Default Menus, Touch Friendly, using full TFT capabilities
  TFT_LVGL_UI    - A Modern UI using LVGL

  For LVGL_UI also copy the 'assets' folder from the build directory to the
  root of your SD card, together with the compiled firmware.

TFT_FONT.name: TFT_FONT
TFT_FONT.comment:
TFT Font for Color UI. Choose one of the following:

NOTOSANS  - Default font with anti-aliasing. Supports Latin Extended and non-Latin characters.
UNIFONT   - Lightweight font, no anti-aliasing. Supports Latin Extended and non-Latin characters.
HELVETICA - Lightweight font, no anti-aliasing. Supports Basic Latin (0x0020-0x007F) and Latin-1 Supplement (0x0080-0x00FF) characters only.

TFT_THEME.name: TFT_THEME
TFT_THEME.comment:
TFT Theme for Color UI. Choose one of the following or add a new one to 'Marlin/src/lcd/tft/themes' directory

BLUE_MARLIN  - Default theme with 'midnight blue' background
BLACK_MARLIN - Theme with 'black' background
ANET_BLACK   - Theme used for Anet ET4/5

TFT_SHARED_IO.name: TFT_SHARED_IO
TFT_SHARED_IO.comment: I/O is shared between TFT display and other devices. Disable async data transfer.

COMPACT_MARLIN_BOOT_LOGO.name: COMPACT_MARLIN_BOOT_LOGO
COMPACT_MARLIN_BOOT_LOGO.comment: Use compressed data to save Flash space

TFT_ROTATION.name: TFT_ROTATION
TFT_ROTATION.comment:
TFT Rotation. Set to one of the following values:

  TFT_ROTATE_90,  TFT_ROTATE_90_MIRROR_X,  TFT_ROTATE_90_MIRROR_Y,
  TFT_ROTATE_180, TFT_ROTATE_180_MIRROR_X, TFT_ROTATE_180_MIRROR_Y,
  TFT_ROTATE_270, TFT_ROTATE_270_MIRROR_X, TFT_ROTATE_270_MIRROR_Y,
  TFT_MIRROR_X, TFT_MIRROR_Y, TFT_NO_ROTATION
TFT_ROTATION.options: { 'TFT_NO_ROTATION':'None', 'TFT_ROTATE_90':'90°', 'TFT_ROTATE_90_MIRROR_X':'90° (Mirror X)', 'TFT_ROTATE_90_MIRROR_Y':'90° (Mirror Y)', 'TFT_ROTATE_180':'180°', 'TFT_ROTATE_180_MIRROR_X':'180° (Mirror X)', 'TFT_ROTATE_180_MIRROR_Y':'180° (Mirror Y)', 'TFT_ROTATE_270':'270°', 'TFT_ROTATE_270_MIRROR_X':'270° (Mirror X)', 'TFT_ROTATE_270_MIRROR_Y':'270° (Mirror Y)', 'TFT_MIRROR_X':'Mirror X', 'TFT_MIRROR_Y':'Mirror Y' }

DWIN_CREALITY_LCD.name: DWIN_CREALITY_LCD
DWIN_CREALITY_LCD.comment: Ender-3 v2 OEM display. A DWIN display with Rotary Encoder.

USE_STRING_HEADINGS.name: USE_STRING_HEADINGS
USE_STRING_HEADINGS.comment: Use string headings for Creality UI instead of images

USE_STRING_TITLES.name: USE_STRING_TITLES
USE_STRING_TITLES.comment: Use string titles for Creality UI instead of images

BUTTON_DELAY_EDIT.name: BUTTON_DELAY_EDIT
BUTTON_DELAY_EDIT.comment: (ms) Button repeat delay for edit screens

NO_BACK_MENU_ITEM.name: NO_BACK_MENU_ITEM
NO_BACK_MENU_ITEM.comment: Don't display a top menu item to go back to the parent menu

TOUCH_CALIBRATION_AUTO_SAVE.name: TOUCH_CALIBRATION_AUTO_SAVE
TOUCH_CALIBRATION_AUTO_SAVE.comment: Auto save successful calibration values to EEPROM

EASYTHREED_UI.name: EASYTHREED_UI
EASYTHREED_UI.comment: EasyThreeD ET-4000+ with button input and status LED

NUM_M106_FANS.name: NUM_M106_FANS
NUM_M106_FANS.comment: Set number of user-controlled fans. Disable to use all board-defined fans.

FAN_SOFT_PWM.name: FAN_SOFT_PWM
FAN_SOFT_PWM.comment:
Use software PWM to drive the fan, as for the heaters. This uses a very low frequency
which is not as annoying as with the hardware PWM. On the other hand, if this frequency
is too low, you should also increment SOFT_PWM_SCALE.

SOFT_PWM_SCALE.name: SOFT_PWM_SCALE
SOFT_PWM_SCALE.comment:
Incrementing this by 1 will double the software PWM frequency, affecting heaters, and
the fan if FAN_SOFT_PWM is enabled. However, control resolution will be halved for each
increment; at zero value, there are 128 effective control positions.

SOFT_PWM_DITHER.name: SOFT_PWM_DITHER
SOFT_PWM_DITHER.comment:
If SOFT_PWM_SCALE is set to a value higher than 0, dithering can be used to mitigate the
associated resolution loss. If enabled, some of the PWM cycles are stretched so on average
the desired duty cycle is attained.

BARICUDA.name: BARICUDA
BARICUDA.comment: Support for the BariCUDA Paste Extruder

TEMP_STAT_LEDS.name: TEMP_STAT_LEDS
TEMP_STAT_LEDS.comment:
Temperature status LEDs that display the hotend and bed temperature.
If all hotends, bed temperature, and target temperature are under 54C
the BLUE led is on. Otherwise the RED led is on. (1C hysteresis)

PCA9632.name: PCA9632
PCA9632.comment: Support for PCA9632 PWM LED driver

PCA9533.name: PCA9533
PCA9533.comment: Support for PCA9533 PWM LED driver

RGB_LED.name: RGB_LED
RGB_LED.comment: LED Type. Enable only one of the following two options:

RGB_STARTUP_TEST.name: RGB_STARTUP_TEST
RGB_STARTUP_TEST.comment: For PWM pins, fade between all colors

NEOPIXEL_LED.name: NEOPIXEL_LED
NEOPIXEL_LED.comment: Support for Adafruit NeoPixel LED driver

NEOPIXEL_TYPE.name: NEOPIXEL_TYPE
NEOPIXEL_TYPE.comment: NEO_GRBW, NEO_RGBW, NEO_GRB, NEO_RBG, etc. See https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_NeoPixel/blob/master/Adafruit_NeoPixel.h

NEOPIXEL_PIXELS.name: NEOPIXEL_PIXELS
NEOPIXEL_PIXELS.comment: Number of LEDs in the strip. (Longest strip when NEOPIXEL2_SEPARATE is disabled.)

NEOPIXEL_IS_SEQUENTIAL.name: NEOPIXEL_IS_SEQUENTIAL
NEOPIXEL_IS_SEQUENTIAL.comment: Sequential display for temperature change - LED by LED. Disable to change all LEDs at once.

NEOPIXEL2_SEPARATE.name: NEOPIXEL2_SEPARATE
NEOPIXEL2_SEPARATE.comment: Support for second Adafruit NeoPixel LED driver controlled with M150 S1 ...

NEOPIXEL2_PIXELS.name: NEOPIXEL2_PIXELS
NEOPIXEL2_PIXELS.comment: Number of LEDs in the second strip

NEOPIXEL_M150_DEFAULT.name: NEOPIXEL_M150_DEFAULT
NEOPIXEL_M150_DEFAULT.comment: Default strip for M150 without 'S'. Use -1 to set all by default.

NEOPIXEL2_INSERIES.name: NEOPIXEL2_INSERIES
NEOPIXEL2_INSERIES.comment: Default behavior is NeoPixel 2 in parallel

NEOPIXEL_BKGD_INDEX_FIRST.name: NEOPIXEL_BKGD_INDEX_FIRST
NEOPIXEL_BKGD_INDEX_FIRST.comment: Use some of the NeoPixel LEDs for static (background) lighting
NEOPIXEL_BKGD_INDEX_FIRST.notes: Index of the first background LED

NEOPIXEL_BKGD_INDEX_LAST.name: NEOPIXEL_BKGD_INDEX_LAST
NEOPIXEL_BKGD_INDEX_LAST.comment: Index of the last background LED

NEOPIXEL_BKGD_ALWAYS_ON.name: NEOPIXEL_BKGD_ALWAYS_ON
NEOPIXEL_BKGD_ALWAYS_ON.comment: Keep the backlight on when other NeoPixels are off

PRINTER_EVENT_LEDS.name: PRINTER_EVENT_LEDS
PRINTER_EVENT_LEDS.comment:
Printer Event LEDs

During printing, the LEDs will reflect the printer status:

 - Gradually change from blue to violet as the heated bed gets to target temp
 - Gradually change from violet to red as the hotend gets to temperature
 - Change to white to illuminate work surface
 - Change to green once print has finished
 - Turn off after the print has finished and the user has pushed a button

NUM_SERVOS.name: NUM_SERVOS
NUM_SERVOS.comment:
Number of servos

For some servo-related options NUM_SERVOS will be set automatically.
Set this manually if there are extra servos needing manual control.
Set to 0 to turn off servo support.
NUM_SERVOS.notes: Note: Servo index starts with 0 for M280-M282 commands

SERVO_DELAY.name: SERVO_DELAY
SERVO_DELAY.comment:
(ms) Delay before the next move will start, to give the servo time to reach its target angle.
300ms is a good value but you can try less delay.
If the servo can't reach the requested position, increase it.

DEACTIVATE_SERVOS_AFTER_MOVE.name: DEACTIVATE_SERVOS_AFTER_MOVE
DEACTIVATE_SERVOS_AFTER_MOVE.comment: Only power servos during movement, otherwise leave off to prevent jitter

EDITABLE_SERVO_ANGLES.name: EDITABLE_SERVO_ANGLES
EDITABLE_SERVO_ANGLES.comment: Edit servo angles with M281 and save to EEPROM with M500

SERVO_DETACH_GCODE.name: SERVO_DETACH_GCODE
SERVO_DETACH_GCODE.comment: Disable servo with M282 to reduce power consumption, noise, and heat when not in use

CONFIGURATION_ADV_H_VERSION.name: CONFIGURATION_ADV_H_VERSION
CONFIGURATION_ADV_H_VERSION.comment:
Configuration_adv.h

Advanced settings.
Only change these if you know exactly what you're doing.
Some of these settings can damage your printer if improperly set!

Basic settings can be found in Configuration.h

CONFIG_EXPORT.name: CONFIG_EXPORT
CONFIG_EXPORT.comment:
Configuration Export

Export the configuration as part of the build. (See signature.py)
Output files are saved with the build (e.g., .pio/build/mega2560).

See `build_all_examples --ini` as an example of config.ini archiving.

 1 = marlin_config.json - Dictionary containing the configuration.
     This file is also generated for CONFIGURATION_EMBEDDING.
 2 = config.ini - File format for PlatformIO preprocessing.
 3 = schema.json - The entire configuration schema. (13 = pattern groups)
 4 = schema.yml - The entire configuration schema.
 5 = Config.h - Minimal configuration by popular demand.

M100_FREE_MEMORY_WATCHER.name: M100_FREE_MEMORY_WATCHER
M100_FREE_MEMORY_WATCHER.comment: M100 Free Memory Watcher to debug memory usage

PINS_DEBUGGING.name: PINS_DEBUGGING
PINS_DEBUGGING.comment: M43 - display pin status, toggle pins, watch pins, watch endstops & toggle LED, test servo probe

MARLIN_TEST_BUILD.name: MARLIN_TEST_BUILD
MARLIN_TEST_BUILD.comment: Enable Tests that will run at startup and produce a report

MARLIN_DEV_MODE.name: MARLIN_DEV_MODE
MARLIN_DEV_MODE.comment: Enable Marlin dev mode which adds some special commands

BUFFER_MONITORING.name: BUFFER_MONITORING
BUFFER_MONITORING.comment:
D576 - Buffer Monitoring
To help diagnose print quality issues stemming from empty command buffers.

POSTMORTEM_DEBUGGING.name: POSTMORTEM_DEBUGGING
POSTMORTEM_DEBUGGING.comment:
Postmortem Debugging captures misbehavior and outputs the CPU status and backtrace to serial.
When running in the debugger it will break for debugging. This is useful to help understand
a crash from a remote location. Requires ~400 bytes of SRAM and 5Kb of flash.

SOFT_RESET_VIA_SERIAL.name: SOFT_RESET_VIA_SERIAL
SOFT_RESET_VIA_SERIAL.notes: 'KILL' and '^X' commands will soft-reset the controller

SOFT_RESET_ON_KILL.name: SOFT_RESET_ON_KILL
SOFT_RESET_ON_KILL.comment: Use a digital button to soft-reset the controller after KILL

OPTIBOOT_RESET_REASON.name: OPTIBOOT_RESET_REASON
OPTIBOOT_RESET_REASON.comment: Report uncleaned reset reason from register r2 instead of MCUSR. Supported by Optiboot on AVR.

MARLIN_SMALL_BUILD.name: MARLIN_SMALL_BUILD
MARLIN_SMALL_BUILD.comment: Shrink the build for smaller boards by sacrificing some serial feedback

THERMOCOUPLE_MAX_ERRORS.name: THERMOCOUPLE_MAX_ERRORS
THERMOCOUPLE_MAX_ERRORS.comment:
Thermocouple sensors are quite sensitive to noise.  Any noise induced in
the sensor wires, such as by stepper motor wires run in parallel to them,
may result in the thermocouple sensor reporting spurious errors.  This
value is the number of errors which can occur in a row before the error
is reported.  This allows us to ignore intermittent error conditions while
still detecting an actual failure, which should result in a continuous
stream of errors from the sensor.

Set this value to 0 to fail on the first error to occur.

TEMP_SENSOR_FORCE_HW_SPI.name: TEMP_SENSOR_FORCE_HW_SPI
TEMP_SENSOR_FORCE_HW_SPI.comment: Thermocouple Options — for MAX6675 (-2), MAX31855 (-3), and MAX31865 (-5).
TEMP_SENSOR_FORCE_HW_SPI.notes: Ignore SCK/MOSI/MISO pins; use CS and the default SPI bus.

MAX31865_SENSOR_WIRES_0.name: MAX31865_SENSOR_WIRES_0
MAX31865_SENSOR_WIRES_0.comment: (2-4) Number of wires for the probe connected to a MAX31865 board.

MAX31865_50HZ_FILTER.name: MAX31865_50HZ_FILTER
MAX31865_50HZ_FILTER.comment: Use a 50Hz filter instead of the default 60Hz.

MAX31865_USE_READ_ERROR_DETECTION.name: MAX31865_USE_READ_ERROR_DETECTION
MAX31865_USE_READ_ERROR_DETECTION.comment: Treat value spikes (20°C delta in under 1s) as read errors.

MAX31865_USE_AUTO_MODE.name: MAX31865_USE_AUTO_MODE
MAX31865_USE_AUTO_MODE.comment: Read faster and more often than 1-shot; bias voltage always on; slight effect on RTD temperature.

MAX31865_MIN_SAMPLING_TIME_MSEC.name: MAX31865_MIN_SAMPLING_TIME_MSEC
MAX31865_MIN_SAMPLING_TIME_MSEC.comment: (ms) 1-shot: minimum read interval. Reduces bias voltage effects by leaving sensor unpowered for longer intervals.

MAX31865_IGNORE_INITIAL_FAULTY_READS.name: MAX31865_IGNORE_INITIAL_FAULTY_READS
MAX31865_IGNORE_INITIAL_FAULTY_READS.comment: Ignore some read faults (keeping the temperature reading) to work around a possible issue (#23439).

MAX31865_WIRE_OHMS_0.name: MAX31865_WIRE_OHMS_0
MAX31865_WIRE_OHMS_0.comment: For 2-wire, set the wire resistances for more accurate readings.

BED_CHECK_INTERVAL.name: BED_CHECK_INTERVAL
BED_CHECK_INTERVAL.notes: (ms) Interval between checks in bang-bang control

BED_HYSTERESIS.name: BED_HYSTERESIS
BED_HYSTERESIS.comment: (°C) Only set the relevant heater state when ABS(T-target) > BED_HYSTERESIS

HEATER_CHAMBER_PIN.name: HEATER_CHAMBER_PIN
HEATER_CHAMBER_PIN.notes: Required heater on/off pin (example: SKR 1.4 Turbo HE1 plug)

FAN1_PIN.name: FAN1_PIN
FAN1_PIN.comment: Remove the fan signal on pin P2_04 (example: SKR 1.4 Turbo HE1 plug)

CHAMBER_CHECK_INTERVAL.name: CHAMBER_CHECK_INTERVAL
CHAMBER_CHECK_INTERVAL.comment: (ms) Interval between checks in bang-bang control

CHAMBER_HYSTERESIS.name: CHAMBER_HYSTERESIS
CHAMBER_HYSTERESIS.comment: (°C) Only set the relevant heater state when ABS(T-target) > CHAMBER_HYSTERESIS

CHAMBER_FAN.name: CHAMBER_FAN
CHAMBER_FAN.comment: Enable a fan on the chamber

CHAMBER_FAN_INDEX.name: CHAMBER_FAN_INDEX
CHAMBER_FAN_INDEX.comment: Index of a fan to repurpose as the chamber fan. (Default: first unused fan)

CHAMBER_FAN_MODE.name: CHAMBER_FAN_MODE
CHAMBER_FAN_MODE.comment: Fan control mode: 0=Static; 1=Linear increase when temp is higher than target; 2=V-shaped curve; 3=similar to 1 but fan is always on.

CHAMBER_VENT.name: CHAMBER_VENT
CHAMBER_VENT.comment: Enable a servo-controlled vent on the chamber

HIGH_EXCESS_HEAT_LIMIT.name: HIGH_EXCESS_HEAT_LIMIT
HIGH_EXCESS_HEAT_LIMIT.comment: How much above target temp to consider there is excess heat in the chamber

TEMP_COOLER_HYSTERESIS.name: TEMP_COOLER_HYSTERESIS
TEMP_COOLER_HYSTERESIS.comment: (°C) Temperature proximity considered "close enough" to the target

COOLER_PIN.name: COOLER_PIN
COOLER_PIN.comment: Laser cooler on/off pin used to control power to the cooling element (e.g., TEC, External chiller via relay)

TEMP_COOLER_PIN.name: TEMP_COOLER_PIN
TEMP_COOLER_PIN.comment: Laser/Cooler temperature sensor pin. ADC is required.

COOLER_FAN.name: COOLER_FAN
COOLER_FAN.comment: Enable a fan on the cooler, Fan# 0,1,2,3 etc.

COOLER_FAN_BASE.name: COOLER_FAN_BASE
COOLER_FAN_BASE.comment: Base Cooler fan PWM (0-255); turns on when Cooler temperature is above the target

THERMAL_PROTECTION_BOARD.name: THERMAL_PROTECTION_BOARD
THERMAL_PROTECTION_BOARD.notes: Halt the printer if the board sensor leaves the temp range below.

THERMAL_PROTECTION_SOC.name: THERMAL_PROTECTION_SOC
THERMAL_PROTECTION_SOC.notes: Halt the printer if the SoC sensor leaves the temp range below.

THERMAL_PROTECTION_PERIOD.name: THERMAL_PROTECTION_PERIOD
THERMAL_PROTECTION_PERIOD.comment:
Thermal Protection provides additional protection to your printer from damage
and fire. Marlin always includes safe min and max temperature ranges which
protect against a broken or disconnected thermistor wire.

The issue: If a thermistor falls out, it will report the much lower
temperature of the air in the room, and the firmware will keep
the heater on.

The solution: Once the temperature reaches the target, start observing.
If the temperature stays too far below the target (hysteresis) for too
long (period), the firmware will halt the machine as a safety precaution.

If you get false positives for "Thermal Runaway", increase
THERMAL_PROTECTION_HYSTERESIS and/or THERMAL_PROTECTION_PERIOD

ADAPTIVE_FAN_SLOWING.name: ADAPTIVE_FAN_SLOWING
ADAPTIVE_FAN_SLOWING.comment: Slow down the part-cooling fan if the temperature drops

REPORT_ADAPTIVE_FAN_SLOWING.name: REPORT_ADAPTIVE_FAN_SLOWING
REPORT_ADAPTIVE_FAN_SLOWING.comment: Report fan slowing activity to the console

TEMP_TUNING_MAINTAIN_FAN.name: TEMP_TUNING_MAINTAIN_FAN
TEMP_TUNING_MAINTAIN_FAN.comment: Don't slow down the fan speed during M303 or M306 T

WATCH_TEMP_PERIOD.name: WATCH_TEMP_PERIOD
WATCH_TEMP_PERIOD.comment:
Whenever an M104, M109, or M303 increases the target temperature, the
firmware will wait for the WATCH_TEMP_PERIOD to expire. If the temperature
hasn't increased by WATCH_TEMP_INCREASE degrees, the machine is halted and
requires a hard reset. This test restarts with any M104/M109/M303, but only
if the current temperature is far enough below the target for a reliable
test.

If you get false positives for "Heating failed", increase WATCH_TEMP_PERIOD
and/or decrease WATCH_TEMP_INCREASE. WATCH_TEMP_INCREASE should not be set
below 2.

THERMAL_PROTECTION_BED_PERIOD.name: THERMAL_PROTECTION_BED_PERIOD
THERMAL_PROTECTION_BED_PERIOD.comment: Thermal Protection parameters for the bed are just as above for hotends.

WATCH_BED_TEMP_PERIOD.name: WATCH_BED_TEMP_PERIOD
WATCH_BED_TEMP_PERIOD.comment: As described above, except for the bed (M140/M190/M303).

THERMAL_PROTECTION_CHAMBER_PERIOD.name: THERMAL_PROTECTION_CHAMBER_PERIOD
THERMAL_PROTECTION_CHAMBER_PERIOD.comment: Thermal Protection parameters for the heated chamber.

THERMAL_PROTECTION_COOLER_PERIOD.name: THERMAL_PROTECTION_COOLER_PERIOD
THERMAL_PROTECTION_COOLER_PERIOD.comment: Thermal Protection parameters for the laser cooler.

THERMAL_PROTECTION_VARIANCE_MONITOR.name: THERMAL_PROTECTION_VARIANCE_MONITOR
THERMAL_PROTECTION_VARIANCE_MONITOR.comment:
Thermal Protection Variance Monitor - EXPERIMENTAL
Kill the machine on a stuck temperature sensor.

This feature may cause some thermally-stable systems to halt. Be sure to test it thoroughly under
a variety of conditions. Disable if you get false positives.

This feature ensures that temperature sensors are updating regularly. If sensors die or get "stuck",
or if Marlin stops reading them, temperatures will remain constant while heaters may still be powered!
This feature only monitors temperature changes so it should catch any issue, hardware or software.

By default it uses the THERMAL_PROTECTION_*_PERIOD constants (above) for the time window, within which
at least one temperature change must occur, to indicate that sensor polling is working. If any monitored
heater's temperature remains totally constant (without even a fractional change) during this period, a
thermal malfunction error occurs and the printer is halted.

A very stable heater might produce a false positive and halt the printer. In this case, try increasing
the corresponding THERMAL_PROTECTION_*_PERIOD constant a bit. Keep in mind that uncontrolled heating
shouldn't be allowed to persist for more than a minute or two.

Be careful to distinguish false positives from real sensor issues before disabling this feature. If the
heater's temperature appears even slightly higher than expected after restarting, you may have a real
thermal malfunction. Check the temperature graph in your host for any unusual bumps.

THERMAL_PROTECTION_VARIANCE_MONITOR_PERI.name: THERMAL_PROTECTION_VARIANCE_MONITOR_PERIOD
THERMAL_PROTECTION_VARIANCE_MONITOR_PERI.comment:
Variance detection window to override the THERMAL_PROTECTION...PERIOD settings above.
Keep in mind that some heaters heat up faster than others.

PID_EXTRUSION_SCALING.name: PID_EXTRUSION_SCALING
PID_EXTRUSION_SCALING.comment:
Add an additional term to the heater power, proportional to the extrusion speed.
A well-chosen Kc value should add just enough power to melt the increased material volume.

DEFAULT_KC_LIST.name: DEFAULT_KC_LIST
DEFAULT_KC_LIST.comment:
Specify up to one value per hotend here, according to your setup.
If there are fewer values, the last one applies to the remaining hotends.

PID_FAN_SCALING.name: PID_FAN_SCALING
PID_FAN_SCALING.comment:
Add an additional term to the heater power, proportional to the fan speed.
A well-chosen Kf value should add just enough power to compensate for power-loss from the cooling fan.
You can either just add a constant compensation with the DEFAULT_KF value
or follow the instruction below to get speed-dependent compensation.

Constant compensation (use only with fan speeds of 0% and 100%)
---------------------------------------------------------------------
A good starting point for the Kf-value comes from the calculation:
  kf = (power_fan * eff_fan) / power_heater * 255
where eff_fan is between 0.0 and 1.0, based on fan-efficiency and airflow to the nozzle / heater.

Example:
  Heater: 40W, Fan: 0.1A * 24V = 2.4W, eff_fan = 0.8
  Kf = (2.4W * 0.8) / 40W * 255 = 12.24

Fan-speed dependent compensation
--------------------------------
1. To find a good Kf value, set the hotend temperature, wait for it to settle, and enable the fan (100%).
   Make sure PID_FAN_SCALING_LIN_FACTOR is 0 and PID_FAN_SCALING_ALTERNATIVE_DEFINITION is not enabled.
   If you see the temperature drop repeat the test, increasing the Kf value slowly, until the temperature
   drop goes away. If the temperature overshoots after enabling the fan, the Kf value is too big.
2. Note the Kf-value for fan-speed at 100%
3. Determine a good value for PID_FAN_SCALING_MIN_SPEED, which is around the speed, where the fan starts moving.
4. Repeat step 1. and 2. for this fan speed.
5. Enable PID_FAN_SCALING_ALTERNATIVE_DEFINITION and enter the two identified Kf-values in
   PID_FAN_SCALING_AT_FULL_SPEED and PID_FAN_SCALING_AT_MIN_SPEED. Enter the minimum speed in PID_FAN_SCALING_MIN_SPEED

PID_FAN_SCALING_AT_FULL_SPEED.name: PID_FAN_SCALING_AT_FULL_SPEED
PID_FAN_SCALING_AT_FULL_SPEED.comment:
The alternative definition is used for an easier configuration.
Just figure out Kf at full speed (255) and PID_FAN_SCALING_MIN_SPEED.
DEFAULT_KF and PID_FAN_SCALING_LIN_FACTOR are calculated accordingly.

PID_FAN_SCALING_MIN_SPEED.name: PID_FAN_SCALING_MIN_SPEED
PID_FAN_SCALING_MIN_SPEED.comment: Minimum fan speed at which to enable PID_FAN_SCALING

DEFAULT_KF_LIST.name: DEFAULT_KF_LIST
DEFAULT_KF_LIST.comment:
Specify up to one value per hotend here, according to your setup.
If there are fewer values, the last one applies to the remaining hotends.

AUTOTEMP.name: AUTOTEMP
AUTOTEMP.comment:
Automatic Temperature Mode

Dynamically adjust the hotend target temperature based on planned E moves.

(Contrast with PID_EXTRUSION_SCALING, which tracks E movement and adjusts PID
 behavior using an additional kC value.)

Autotemp is calculated by (mintemp + factor * mm_per_sec), capped to maxtemp.

Enable Autotemp Mode with M104/M109 F<factor> S<mintemp> B<maxtemp>.
Disable by sending M104/M109 with no F parameter (or F0 with AUTOTEMP_PROPORTIONAL).

AUTOTEMP_OLDWEIGHT.name: AUTOTEMP_OLDWEIGHT
AUTOTEMP_OLDWEIGHT.comment: Factor used to weight previous readings (0.0 < value < 1.0)

AUTOTEMP_PROPORTIONAL.name: AUTOTEMP_PROPORTIONAL
AUTOTEMP_PROPORTIONAL.comment: Turn on AUTOTEMP on M104/M109 by default using proportions set here

AUTOTEMP_FACTOR_P.name: AUTOTEMP_FACTOR_P
AUTOTEMP_FACTOR_P.comment: Apply this F parameter by default (overridden by M104/M109 F)

SHOW_TEMP_ADC_VALUES.name: SHOW_TEMP_ADC_VALUES
SHOW_TEMP_ADC_VALUES.comment:
Show Temperature ADC value
Enable for M105 to include ADC values read from temperature sensors.

MAX_CONSECUTIVE_LOW_TEMPERATURE_ERROR_AL.name: MAX_CONSECUTIVE_LOW_TEMPERATURE_ERROR_ALLOWED
MAX_CONSECUTIVE_LOW_TEMPERATURE_ERROR_AL.comment:
The number of consecutive low temperature errors that can occur
before a MINTEMP error is triggered. (Shouldn't be more than 10.)

PREHEAT_TIME_HOTEND_MS.name: PREHEAT_TIME_HOTEND_MS
PREHEAT_TIME_HOTEND_MS.comment:
The number of milliseconds a hotend will preheat before starting to check
the temperature. This value should NOT be set to the time it takes the
hot end to reach the target temperature, but the time it takes to reach
the minimum temperature your thermistor can read. The lower the better/safer.
This shouldn't need to be more than 30 seconds (30000)

TEMP_SENSOR_AD595_OFFSET.name: TEMP_SENSOR_AD595_OFFSET
TEMP_SENSOR_AD595_OFFSET.comment:
Calibration for AD595 / AD8495 sensor to adjust temperature measurements.
The final temperature is calculated as (measuredTemp * GAIN) + OFFSET.

HEATERS_PARALLEL.name: HEATERS_PARALLEL
HEATERS_PARALLEL.comment: Control heater 0 and heater 1 in parallel.

EXTRUDER_RUNOUT_PREVENT.name: EXTRUDER_RUNOUT_PREVENT
EXTRUDER_RUNOUT_PREVENT.comment:
Extruder runout prevention.
If the machine is idle and the temperature over MINTEMP
then extrude some filament every couple of SECONDS.

HOTEND_IDLE_TIMEOUT.name: HOTEND_IDLE_TIMEOUT
HOTEND_IDLE_TIMEOUT.comment:
Hotend Idle Timeout
Prevent filament in the nozzle from charring and causing a critical jam.

HOTEND_IDLE_TIMEOUT_SEC.name: HOTEND_IDLE_TIMEOUT_SEC
HOTEND_IDLE_TIMEOUT_SEC.comment: (seconds) Time without extruder movement to trigger protection

HOTEND_IDLE_MIN_TRIGGER.name: HOTEND_IDLE_MIN_TRIGGER
HOTEND_IDLE_MIN_TRIGGER.comment: (°C) Minimum temperature to enable hotend protection

HOTEND_IDLE_NOZZLE_TARGET.name: HOTEND_IDLE_NOZZLE_TARGET
HOTEND_IDLE_NOZZLE_TARGET.comment: (°C) Safe temperature for the nozzle after timeout

HOTEND_IDLE_BED_TARGET.name: HOTEND_IDLE_BED_TARGET
HOTEND_IDLE_BED_TARGET.comment: (°C) Safe temperature for the bed after timeout

EXT_SOLENOID.name: EXT_SOLENOID
EXT_SOLENOID.comment:
Activate a solenoid on the active extruder with M380. Disable all with M381.
Define SOL0_PIN, SOL1_PIN, etc., for each extruder that has a solenoid.

LIN_ADVANCE.name: LIN_ADVANCE
LIN_ADVANCE.comment:
Linear Pressure Control v1.5

Assumption: advance [steps] = k * (delta velocity [steps/s])
K=0 means advance disabled.

NOTE: K values for LIN_ADVANCE 1.5 differ from earlier versions!

Set K around 0.22 for 3mm PLA Direct Drive with ~6.5cm between the drive gear and heatbreak.
Larger K values will be needed for flexible filament and greater distances.
If this algorithm produces a higher speed offset than the extruder can handle (compared to E jerk)
print acceleration will be reduced during the affected moves to keep within the limit.

See https://marlinfw.org/docs/features/lin_advance.html for full instructions.

ADVANCE_K.name: ADVANCE_K
ADVANCE_K.comment: (mm) Compression length per 1mm/s extruder speed, per extruder. Override with 'M900 T<tool> K<mm>'.

ADVANCE_K_EXTRA.name: ADVANCE_K_EXTRA
ADVANCE_K_EXTRA.comment: Add a second linear advance constant, configurable with 'M900 L'.

LA_DEBUG.name: LA_DEBUG
LA_DEBUG.comment: Print debug information to serial during operation. Disable for production use.

EXPERIMENTAL_I2S_LA.name: EXPERIMENTAL_I2S_LA
EXPERIMENTAL_I2S_LA.comment: Allow I2S_STEPPER_STREAM to be used with LA. Performance degrades as the LA step rate reaches ~20kHz.

SMOOTH_LIN_ADVANCE.name: SMOOTH_LIN_ADVANCE
SMOOTH_LIN_ADVANCE.comment: Remove limits on acceleration by gradual increase of nozzle pressure

ADVANCE_TAU.name: ADVANCE_TAU
ADVANCE_TAU.comment:
ADVANCE_TAU is also the time ahead that the smoother needs to look
into the planner, so the planner needs to have enough blocks loaded.
For k=0.04 at 10k acceleration and an "Orbiter 2" extruder it can be as low as 0.0075.
Adjust by lowering the value until you observe the extruder skipping, then raise slightly.
Higher k and higher XY acceleration may require larger ADVANCE_TAU to avoid skipping steps.
ADVANCE_TAU.notes: (s) Smoothing time to reduce extruder acceleration, per extruder

SMOOTH_LIN_ADV_HZ.name: SMOOTH_LIN_ADV_HZ
SMOOTH_LIN_ADV_HZ.comment: (Hz) How often to update extruder speed

INPUT_SHAPING_E_SYNC.name: INPUT_SHAPING_E_SYNC
INPUT_SHAPING_E_SYNC.comment: Synchronize the extruder-shaped XY axes (to increase precision)

NONLINEAR_EXTRUSION.name: NONLINEAR_EXTRUSION
NONLINEAR_EXTRUSION.comment:
Nonlinear Extrusion Control

Control extrusion rate based on instantaneous extruder velocity. Can be used to correct for
underextrusion at high extruder speeds that are otherwise well-behaved (i.e., not skipping).
For better results also enable ADAPTIVE_STEP_SMOOTHING.

NONLINEAR_EXTRUSION_DEFAULT_ON.name: NONLINEAR_EXTRUSION_DEFAULT_ON
NONLINEAR_EXTRUSION_DEFAULT_ON.comment: Enable if NLE should be ON by default

USE_CONTROLLER_FAN.name: USE_CONTROLLER_FAN
USE_CONTROLLER_FAN.comment:
Controller Fan
To cool down the stepper drivers and MOSFETs.

The fan turns on automatically whenever any driver is enabled and turns
off (or reduces to idle speed) shortly after drivers are turned off.

CONTROLLER_FAN_PIN.name: CONTROLLER_FAN_PIN
CONTROLLER_FAN_PIN.comment: Set a custom pin for the controller fan

CONTROLLER_FAN2_PIN.name: CONTROLLER_FAN2_PIN
CONTROLLER_FAN2_PIN.comment: Set a custom pin for second controller fan

CONTROLLER_FAN_USE_Z_ONLY.name: CONTROLLER_FAN_USE_Z_ONLY
CONTROLLER_FAN_USE_Z_ONLY.comment: With this option only the Z axis is considered

CONTROLLER_FAN_IGNORE_Z.name: CONTROLLER_FAN_IGNORE_Z
CONTROLLER_FAN_IGNORE_Z.comment: Ignore Z stepper. Useful when stepper timeout is disabled.

CONTROLLERFAN_SPEED_MIN.name: CONTROLLERFAN_SPEED_MIN
CONTROLLERFAN_SPEED_MIN.comment: (0-255) Minimum speed. (If set below this value the fan is turned off.)

CONTROLLERFAN_SPEED_ACTIVE.name: CONTROLLERFAN_SPEED_ACTIVE
CONTROLLERFAN_SPEED_ACTIVE.comment: (0-255) Active speed, used when any motor is enabled

CONTROLLERFAN_SPEED_IDLE.name: CONTROLLERFAN_SPEED_IDLE
CONTROLLERFAN_SPEED_IDLE.comment: (0-255) Idle speed, used when motors are disabled

CONTROLLERFAN_IDLE_TIME.name: CONTROLLERFAN_IDLE_TIME
CONTROLLERFAN_IDLE_TIME.comment: (seconds) Extra time to keep the fan running after disabling motors

CONTROLLER_FAN_MIN_BOARD_TEMP.name: CONTROLLER_FAN_MIN_BOARD_TEMP
CONTROLLER_FAN_MIN_BOARD_TEMP.comment: Use TEMP_SENSOR_BOARD as a trigger for enabling the controller fan
CONTROLLER_FAN_MIN_BOARD_TEMP.notes: (°C) Turn on the fan if the board reaches this temperature

CONTROLLER_FAN_MIN_SOC_TEMP.name: CONTROLLER_FAN_MIN_SOC_TEMP
CONTROLLER_FAN_MIN_SOC_TEMP.comment: Use TEMP_SENSOR_SOC as a trigger for enabling the controller fan
CONTROLLER_FAN_MIN_SOC_TEMP.notes: (°C) Turn on the fan if the SoC reaches this temperature

CONTROLLER_FAN_BED_HEATING.name: CONTROLLER_FAN_BED_HEATING
CONTROLLER_FAN_BED_HEATING.comment: Turn on the fan when heating the bed

FAN_KICKSTART_TIME.name: FAN_KICKSTART_TIME
FAN_KICKSTART_TIME.comment:
Fan Kickstart
When part cooling or controller fans first start, run at a speed that
gets it spinning reliably for a short time before setting the requested speed.
(Does not work on Sanguinololu with FAN_SOFT_PWM.)

FAN_KICKSTART_LINEAR.name: FAN_KICKSTART_LINEAR
FAN_KICKSTART_LINEAR.comment: Set kickstart time linearly based on the speed, e.g., for 20% (51) it will be FAN_KICKSTART_TIME * 0.2. Useful for quick speed up to low speed. Kickstart power must be set to 255.

FAN_OFF_PWM.name: FAN_OFF_PWM
FAN_OFF_PWM.comment: Some coolers may require a non-zero "off" state.

FAN_MIN_PWM.name: FAN_MIN_PWM
FAN_MIN_PWM.comment:
PWM Fan Scaling

Define the min/max speeds for PWM fans (as set with M106).

With these options the M106 0-255 value range is scaled to a subset
to ensure that the fan has enough power to spin, or to run lower
current fans with higher current. (e.g., 5V/12V fans with 12V/24V)
Value 0 always turns off the fan.

Define one or both of these to override the default 0-255 range.

FAST_PWM_FAN.name: FAST_PWM_FAN
FAST_PWM_FAN.comment:
Fan Fast PWM

Combinations of PWM Modes, prescale values and TOP resolutions are used internally
to produce a frequency as close as possible to the desired frequency.

FAST_PWM_FAN_FREQUENCY
  Set this to your desired frequency.
  For AVR, if left undefined this defaults to F = F_CPU/(2*255*1)
           i.e., F = 31.4kHz on 16MHz micro-controllers or F = 39.2kHz on 20MHz micro-controllers.
  For non AVR, if left undefined this defaults to F = 1Khz.
  This F value is only to protect the hardware from an absence of configuration
  and not to complete it when users are not aware that the frequency must be specifically set to support the target board.

  NOTE: Setting very low frequencies (< 10 Hz) may result in unexpected timer behavior.
        Setting very high frequencies can damage your hardware.

USE_OCR2A_AS_TOP [undefined by default]
  Boards that use TIMER2 for PWM have limitations resulting in only a few possible frequencies on TIMER2:
  16MHz MCUs: [62.5kHz, 31.4kHz (default), 7.8kHz, 3.92kHz, 1.95kHz, 977Hz, 488Hz, 244Hz, 60Hz, 122Hz, 30Hz]
  20MHz MCUs: [78.1kHz, 39.2kHz (default), 9.77kHz, 4.9kHz, 2.44kHz, 1.22kHz, 610Hz, 305Hz, 153Hz, 76Hz, 38Hz]
  A greater range can be achieved by enabling USE_OCR2A_AS_TOP. But note that this option blocks the use of
  PWM on pin OC2A. Only use this option if you don't need PWM on 0C2A. (Check your schematic.)
  USE_OCR2A_AS_TOP sacrifices duty cycle control resolution to achieve this broader range of frequencies.
FAST_PWM_FAN.notes: Increase the fan PWM frequency. Removes the PWM noise but increases heating in the FET/Arduino

FAST_PWM_FAN_FREQUENCY.name: FAST_PWM_FAN_FREQUENCY
FAST_PWM_FAN_FREQUENCY.comment: Define here to override the defaults below

REDUNDANT_PART_COOLING_FAN.name: REDUNDANT_PART_COOLING_FAN
REDUNDANT_PART_COOLING_FAN.comment: Assign more PWM fans for part cooling, synchronized with Fan 0
REDUNDANT_PART_COOLING_FAN.notes: Index of the first fan to synchronize with Fan 0

NUM_REDUNDANT_FANS.name: NUM_REDUNDANT_FANS
NUM_REDUNDANT_FANS.comment: Number of sequential fans to synchronize with Fan 0

E0_AUTO_FAN_PIN.name: E0_AUTO_FAN_PIN
E0_AUTO_FAN_PIN.comment:
Extruder cooling fans

Extruder auto fans automatically turn on when their extruders'
temperatures go above EXTRUDER_AUTO_FAN_TEMPERATURE.

Your board's pins file specifies the recommended pins. Override those here
or set to -1 to disable completely.

Multiple extruders can be assigned to the same pin in which case
the fan will turn on when any selected extruder is above the threshold.

FOURWIRES_FANS.name: FOURWIRES_FANS
FOURWIRES_FANS.comment:
Hotend Cooling Fans tachometers

Define one or more tachometer pins to enable fan speed
monitoring, and reporting of fan speeds with M123.

NOTE: Only works with fans up to 7000 RPM.
FOURWIRES_FANS.notes: Needed with AUTO_FAN when 4-wire PWM fans are installed

FANMUX0_PIN.name: FANMUX0_PIN
FANMUX0_PIN.comment:
Part-Cooling Fan Multiplexer

This feature allows you to digitally multiplex the fan output.
The multiplexer is automatically switched at tool-change.
Set FANMUX[012]_PINs below for up to 2, 4, or 8 multiplexed fans.

CASE_LIGHT_PIN.name: CASE_LIGHT_PIN
CASE_LIGHT_PIN.comment: Override the default pin if needed

INVERT_CASE_LIGHT.name: INVERT_CASE_LIGHT
INVERT_CASE_LIGHT.comment: Set true if Case Light is ON when pin is LOW

CASE_LIGHT_DEFAULT_BRIGHTNESS.name: CASE_LIGHT_DEFAULT_BRIGHTNESS
CASE_LIGHT_DEFAULT_BRIGHTNESS.comment: Set default power-up brightness (0-255, requires PWM pin)

CASE_LIGHT_NO_BRIGHTNESS.name: CASE_LIGHT_NO_BRIGHTNESS
CASE_LIGHT_NO_BRIGHTNESS.comment: Disable brightness control. Enable for non-PWM lighting.

CASE_LIGHT_MENU.name: CASE_LIGHT_MENU
CASE_LIGHT_MENU.comment: Add Case Light options to the LCD menu

CASE_LIGHT_USE_NEOPIXEL.name: CASE_LIGHT_USE_NEOPIXEL
CASE_LIGHT_USE_NEOPIXEL.comment: Use NeoPixel LED as case light

CASE_LIGHT_USE_RGB_LED.name: CASE_LIGHT_USE_RGB_LED
CASE_LIGHT_USE_RGB_LED.comment: Use RGB / RGBW LED as case light

ENDSTOPS_ALWAYS_ON_DEFAULT.name: ENDSTOPS_ALWAYS_ON_DEFAULT
ENDSTOPS_ALWAYS_ON_DEFAULT.comment:
If you want endstops to stay on (by default) even when not homing,
enable this option. Override at any time with M120, M121.

Z_LATE_ENABLE.name: Z_LATE_ENABLE
Z_LATE_ENABLE.comment: Enable Z the last moment. Needed if your Z driver overheats.

EXTERNAL_CLOSED_LOOP_CONTROLLER.name: EXTERNAL_CLOSED_LOOP_CONTROLLER
EXTERNAL_CLOSED_LOOP_CONTROLLER.comment: Employ an external closed loop controller. Override pins here if needed.

EXTRA_FAN_SPEED.name: EXTRA_FAN_SPEED
EXTRA_FAN_SPEED.comment:
Extra Fan Speed
Adds a secondary fan speed for each print-cooling fan.
  'M106 P<fan> T3-255' : Set a secondary speed for <fan>
  'M106 P<fan> T2'     : Use the set secondary speed
  'M106 P<fan> T1'     : Restore the previous fan speed

CANCEL_OBJECTS.name: CANCEL_OBJECTS
CANCEL_OBJECTS.comment:
Cancel Objects

Implement M486 to allow Marlin to skip objects

CANCEL_OBJECTS_REPORTING.name: CANCEL_OBJECTS_REPORTING
CANCEL_OBJECTS_REPORTING.comment: Emit the current object as a status message

DUAL_X_CARRIAGE.name: DUAL_X_CARRIAGE
DUAL_X_CARRIAGE.comment:
Dual X Carriage

This setup has two X carriages that can move independently, each with its own hotend.
The carriages can be used to print an object with two colors or materials, or in
"duplication mode" it can print two identical or X-mirrored objects simultaneously.
The inactive carriage is parked automatically to prevent oozing.
X1 is the left carriage, X2 the right. They park and home at opposite ends of the X axis.
By default the X2 stepper is assigned to the first unused E plug on the board.

The following Dual X Carriage modes can be selected with M605 S<mode>:

  0 : (FULL_CONTROL) The slicer has full control over both X-carriages and can achieve optimal travel
      results as long as it supports dual X-carriages. (M605 S0)

  1 : (AUTO_PARK) The firmware automatically parks and unparks the X-carriages on tool-change so
      that additional slicer support is not required. (M605 S1)

  2 : (DUPLICATION) The firmware moves the second X-carriage and extruder in synchronization with
      the first X-carriage and extruder, to print 2 copies of the same object at the same time.
      Set the constant X-offset and temperature differential with M605 S2 X[offs] R[deg] and
      follow with M605 S2 to initiate duplicated movement.

  3 : (MIRRORED) Formbot/Vivedino-inspired mirrored mode in which the second extruder duplicates
      the movement of the first except the second extruder is reversed in the X axis.
      Set the initial X offset and temperature differential with M605 S2 X[offs] R[deg] and
      follow with M605 S3 to initiate mirrored movement.

X1_MAX_POS.name: X1_MAX_POS
X1_MAX_POS.comment: A max coordinate so the X1 carriage can't hit the parked X2 carriage

X2_MIN_POS.name: X2_MIN_POS
X2_MIN_POS.comment: A min coordinate so the X2 carriage can't hit the parked X1 carriage

X2_MAX_POS.name: X2_MAX_POS
X2_MAX_POS.comment: The max position of the X2 carriage, typically also the home position

X2_HOME_POS.name: X2_HOME_POS
X2_HOME_POS.comment: Default X2 home position. Set to X2_MAX_POS. NOTE: For Dual X Carriage use M218 T1 Xn to override the X2_HOME_POS. This allows recalibration of endstops distance without a rebuild. Remember to set the second extruder's X-offset to 0 in your slicer.

DEFAULT_DUAL_X_CARRIAGE_MODE.name: DEFAULT_DUAL_X_CARRIAGE_MODE
DEFAULT_DUAL_X_CARRIAGE_MODE.comment: This is the default power-up mode which can be changed later using M605 S<mode>.

DEFAULT_DUPLICATION_X_OFFSET.name: DEFAULT_DUPLICATION_X_OFFSET
DEFAULT_DUPLICATION_X_OFFSET.comment: Default x offset in duplication mode (typically set to half print bed width)

EVENT_GCODE_IDEX_AFTER_MODECHANGE.name: EVENT_GCODE_IDEX_AFTER_MODECHANGE
EVENT_GCODE_IDEX_AFTER_MODECHANGE.comment: Default action to execute following M605 mode change commands. Typically G28X to apply new mode.

INVERT_X2_VS_X_DIR.name: INVERT_X2_VS_X_DIR
INVERT_X2_VS_X_DIR.comment:
Multi-Stepper / Multi-Endstop

When X2_DRIVER_TYPE is defined, this indicates that the X and X2 motors work in tandem.
The following explanations for X also apply to Y and Z multi-stepper setups.
Endstop offsets may be changed by 'M666 X<offset> Y<offset> Z<offset>' and stored to EEPROM.

- Enable INVERT_X2_VS_X_DIR if the X2 motor requires an opposite DIR signal from X.

- Enable X_DUAL_ENDSTOPS if the second motor has its own endstop, with adjustable offset.

  - Extra endstops are included in the output of 'M119'.

  - Set X_DUAL_ENDSTOP_ADJUSTMENT to the known error in the X2 endstop.
    Applied to the X2 motor on 'G28' / 'G28 X'.
    Get the offset by homing X and measuring the error.
    Also set with 'M666 X<offset>' and stored to EEPROM with 'M500'.

  - Define the extra endstop pins here to override defaults. No auto-assignment.
INVERT_X2_VS_X_DIR.notes: X2 direction signal is the opposite of X

X2_ENDSTOP_ADJUSTMENT.name: X2_ENDSTOP_ADJUSTMENT
X2_ENDSTOP_ADJUSTMENT.comment: X2 offset relative to X endstop

INVERT_Y2_VS_Y_DIR.name: INVERT_Y2_VS_Y_DIR
INVERT_Y2_VS_Y_DIR.comment: Y2 direction signal is the opposite of Y

Y2_ENDSTOP_ADJUSTMENT.name: Y2_ENDSTOP_ADJUSTMENT
Y2_ENDSTOP_ADJUSTMENT.comment: Y2 offset relative to Y endstop

INVERT_Z2_VS_Z_DIR.name: INVERT_Z2_VS_Z_DIR
INVERT_Z2_VS_Z_DIR.notes: Z2 direction signal is the opposite of Z

Z_MULTI_ENDSTOPS.name: Z_MULTI_ENDSTOPS
Z_MULTI_ENDSTOPS.comment: Other Z axes have their own endstops

Z2_ENDSTOP_ADJUSTMENT.name: Z2_ENDSTOP_ADJUSTMENT
Z2_ENDSTOP_ADJUSTMENT.comment: Z2 offset relative to Z endstop

INVERT_Z3_VS_Z_DIR.name: INVERT_Z3_VS_Z_DIR
INVERT_Z3_VS_Z_DIR.comment: Z3 direction signal is the opposite of Z

Z3_ENDSTOP_ADJUSTMENT.name: Z3_ENDSTOP_ADJUSTMENT
Z3_ENDSTOP_ADJUSTMENT.comment: Z3 offset relative to Z endstop

INVERT_Z4_VS_Z_DIR.name: INVERT_Z4_VS_Z_DIR
INVERT_Z4_VS_Z_DIR.comment: Z4 direction signal is the opposite of Z

Z4_ENDSTOP_ADJUSTMENT.name: Z4_ENDSTOP_ADJUSTMENT
Z4_ENDSTOP_ADJUSTMENT.comment: Z4 offset relative to Z endstop

E_DUAL_STEPPER_DRIVERS.name: E_DUAL_STEPPER_DRIVERS
E_DUAL_STEPPER_DRIVERS.comment: Drive the E axis with two synchronized steppers

SENSORLESS_BACKOFF_MM.name: SENSORLESS_BACKOFF_MM
SENSORLESS_BACKOFF_MM.comment:
Homing Procedure
Homing (G28) does an indefinite move towards the endstops to establish
the position of the toolhead relative to the workspace.
SENSORLESS_BACKOFF_MM.notes: (linear=mm, rotational=°) Backoff from endstops before sensorless homing

HOMING_BUMP_MM.name: HOMING_BUMP_MM
HOMING_BUMP_MM.comment: (linear=mm, rotational=°) Backoff from endstops after first bump

HOMING_BUMP_DIVISOR.name: HOMING_BUMP_DIVISOR
HOMING_BUMP_DIVISOR.comment: Re-Bump Speed Divisor (Divides the Homing Feedrate)

HOMING_BACKOFF_POST_MM.name: HOMING_BACKOFF_POST_MM
HOMING_BACKOFF_POST_MM.comment: (linear=mm, rotational=°) Backoff from endstops after homing

XY_COUNTERPART_BACKOFF_MM.name: XY_COUNTERPART_BACKOFF_MM
XY_COUNTERPART_BACKOFF_MM.comment: (mm) Backoff X after homing Y, and vice-versa

QUICK_HOME.name: QUICK_HOME
QUICK_HOME.comment: If G28 contains XY do a diagonal move first

HOME_Y_BEFORE_X.name: HOME_Y_BEFORE_X
HOME_Y_BEFORE_X.comment: If G28 contains XY home Y before X

HOME_Z_FIRST.name: HOME_Z_FIRST
HOME_Z_FIRST.comment: Home Z first. Requires a real endstop (not a probe).

CODEPENDENT_XY_HOMING.name: CODEPENDENT_XY_HOMING
CODEPENDENT_XY_HOMING.comment: If X/Y can't home without homing Y/X first

BLTOUCH_DELAY.name: BLTOUCH_DELAY
BLTOUCH_DELAY.comment:
Safety: The probe needs time to recognize the command.
Minimum command delay (ms). Enable and increase if needed.

BLTOUCH_FORCE_SW_MODE.name: BLTOUCH_FORCE_SW_MODE
BLTOUCH_FORCE_SW_MODE.comment:
Feature: Switch into SW mode after a deploy. It makes the output pulse longer. Can be useful
in special cases, like noisy or filtered input configurations.

BLTOUCH_SET_5V_MODE.name: BLTOUCH_SET_5V_MODE
BLTOUCH_SET_5V_MODE.comment:
Danger: Don't activate 5V mode unless attached to a 5V-tolerant controller!
V3.0 or 3.1: Set default mode to 5V mode at Marlin startup.
If disabled, OD mode is the hard-coded default on 3.0
On startup, Marlin will compare its EEPROM to this value. If the selected mode
differs, a mode set EEPROM write will be completed at initialization.
Use the option below to force an EEPROM write to a V3.1 probe regardless.

BLTOUCH_LCD_VOLTAGE_MENU.name: BLTOUCH_LCD_VOLTAGE_MENU
BLTOUCH_LCD_VOLTAGE_MENU.comment: Safety: Enable voltage mode settings in the LCD menu.

BLTOUCH_FORCE_MODE_SET.name: BLTOUCH_FORCE_MODE_SET
BLTOUCH_FORCE_MODE_SET.comment:
Safety: Activate if connecting a probe with an unknown voltage mode.
V3.0: Set a probe into mode selected above at Marlin startup. Required for 5V mode on 3.0
V3.1: Force a probe with unknown mode into selected mode at Marlin startup ( = Probe EEPROM write )
To preserve the life of the probe, use this once then turn it off and re-flash.

BLTOUCH_HS_MODE.name: BLTOUCH_HS_MODE
BLTOUCH_HS_MODE.comment:
Enable "HIGH SPEED" option for probing.
Danger: Disable if your probe sometimes fails. Only suitable for stable well-adjusted systems.
This feature was designed for Deltabots with very fast Z moves; however, higher speed Cartesians
might be able to use it. If the machine can't raise Z fast enough the BLTouch may go into ALARM.

Set the default state here, change with 'M401 S' or UI, use M500 to save, M502 to reset.

BLTOUCH_HS_EXTRA_CLEARANCE.name: BLTOUCH_HS_EXTRA_CLEARANCE
BLTOUCH_HS_EXTRA_CLEARANCE.comment:
The probe Z offset (M851 Z) is the height at which the probe triggers.
This must be large enough to keep the probe pin off the bed and prevent
it from snagging on the bed clips.

Z_STEPPER_AUTO_ALIGN.name: Z_STEPPER_AUTO_ALIGN
Z_STEPPER_AUTO_ALIGN.comment:
Z Steppers Auto-Alignment
Add the G34 command to align multiple Z steppers using a bed probe.

Z_STEPPER_ALIGN_XY.name: Z_STEPPER_ALIGN_XY
Z_STEPPER_ALIGN_XY.comment:
Define probe X and Y positions for Z1, Z2 [, Z3 [, Z4]]
These positions are machine-relative and do not shift with the M206 home offset!
If not defined, probe limits will be used.
Override with 'M422 S<index> X<pos> Y<pos>'.

Z_STEPPERS_ORIENTATION.name: Z_STEPPERS_ORIENTATION
Z_STEPPERS_ORIENTATION.comment:
Orientation for the automatically-calculated probe positions.
Override Z stepper align points with 'M422 S<index> X<pos> Y<pos>'

2 Steppers:  (0)     (1)
              |       |   2   |
              | 1   2 |       |
              |       |   1   |

3 Steppers:  (0)     (1)     (2)     (3)
              |   3   | 1     | 2   1 |     2 |
              |       |     3 |       | 3     |
              | 1   2 | 2     |   3   |     1 |

4 Steppers:  (0)     (1)     (2)     (3)
              | 4   3 | 1   4 | 2   1 | 3   2 |
              |       |       |       |       |
              | 1   2 | 2   3 | 3   4 | 4   1 |

Z_STEPPER_ALIGN_STEPPER_XY.name: Z_STEPPER_ALIGN_STEPPER_XY
Z_STEPPER_ALIGN_STEPPER_XY.comment:
Z Stepper positions for more rapid convergence in bed alignment.
Requires 3 or 4 Z steppers.

Define Stepper XY positions for Z1, Z2, Z3... corresponding to the screw
positions in the bed carriage, with one position per Z stepper in stepper
driver order.

Z_STEPPER_ALIGN_AMP.name: Z_STEPPER_ALIGN_AMP
Z_STEPPER_ALIGN_AMP.comment:
Amplification factor. Used to scale the correction step up or down in case
the stepper (spindle) position is farther out than the test point.
Z_STEPPER_ALIGN_AMP.notes: Use a value > 1.0 NOTE: This may cause instability!

G34_MAX_GRADE.name: G34_MAX_GRADE
G34_MAX_GRADE.comment: On a 300mm bed a 5% grade would give a misalignment of ~1.5cm
G34_MAX_GRADE.notes: (%) Maximum incline that G34 will handle

Z_STEPPER_ALIGN_ITERATIONS.name: Z_STEPPER_ALIGN_ITERATIONS
Z_STEPPER_ALIGN_ITERATIONS.comment: Number of iterations to apply during alignment

Z_STEPPER_ALIGN_ACC.name: Z_STEPPER_ALIGN_ACC
Z_STEPPER_ALIGN_ACC.comment: Stop iterating early if the accuracy is better than this

RESTORE_LEVELING_AFTER_G34.name: RESTORE_LEVELING_AFTER_G34
RESTORE_LEVELING_AFTER_G34.comment: Restore leveling after G34 is done?

HOME_AFTER_G34.name: HOME_AFTER_G34
HOME_AFTER_G34.comment:
After G34, re-home Z (G28 Z) or just calculate it from the last probe heights?
Re-homing might be more precise in reproducing the actual 'G28 Z' homing height, especially on an uneven bed.

EVENT_GCODE_BEFORE_G34.name: EVENT_GCODE_BEFORE_G34
EVENT_GCODE_BEFORE_G34.comment:
Commands to execute at the start of G34 probing,
after switching to the PROBING_TOOL.

EVENT_GCODE_AFTER_G34.name: EVENT_GCODE_AFTER_G34
EVENT_GCODE_AFTER_G34.comment:
Commands to execute at the end of G34 probing.
Useful to retract or move the Z probe out of the way.

ASSISTED_TRAMMING.name: ASSISTED_TRAMMING
ASSISTED_TRAMMING.comment:
Assisted Tramming

Add the G35 command to measure bed corners and help adjust screws. Requires a bed probe.

TRAMMING_POINT_XY.name: TRAMMING_POINT_XY
TRAMMING_POINT_XY.comment: Define from 3 to 9 points to probe.

TRAMMING_POINT_NAME_1.name: TRAMMING_POINT_NAME_1
TRAMMING_POINT_NAME_1.comment: Define position names for probe points.

RESTORE_LEVELING_AFTER_G35.name: RESTORE_LEVELING_AFTER_G35
RESTORE_LEVELING_AFTER_G35.comment: Enable to restore leveling setup after operation

REPORT_TRAMMING_MM.name: REPORT_TRAMMING_MM
REPORT_TRAMMING_MM.comment: Report Z deviation (mm) for each point relative to the first

ASSISTED_TRAMMING_WIZARD.name: ASSISTED_TRAMMING_WIZARD
ASSISTED_TRAMMING_WIZARD.comment: Add a Tramming Wizard to the LCD menu

ASSISTED_TRAMMING_WAIT_POSITION.name: ASSISTED_TRAMMING_WAIT_POSITION
ASSISTED_TRAMMING_WAIT_POSITION.comment: Move the nozzle out of the way for adjustment

TRAMMING_SCREW_THREAD.name: TRAMMING_SCREW_THREAD
TRAMMING_SCREW_THREAD.comment:
Screw Thread. Use one of the following defines:

  M3_CW = M3 Clockwise, M3_CCW = M3 Counter-Clockwise
  M4_CW = M4 Clockwise, M4_CCW = M4 Counter-Clockwise
  M5_CW = M5 Clockwise, M5_CCW = M5 Counter-Clockwise
TRAMMING_SCREW_THREAD.options: {'M3_CW':'M3 Clockwise','M3_CCW':'M3 Counter-Clockwise','M4_CW':'M4 Clockwise','M4_CCW':'M4 Counter-Clockwise','M5_CW':'M5 Clockwise','M5_CCW':'M5 Counter-Clockwise'}

G38_PROBE_TARGET.name: G38_PROBE_TARGET
G38_PROBE_TARGET.comment:
G38 Probe Target

This option adds G38.2 and G38.3 (probe towards target)
and optionally G38.4 and G38.5 (probe away from target).
Set MULTIPLE_PROBING for G38 to probe more than once.

G38_PROBE_AWAY.name: G38_PROBE_AWAY
G38_PROBE_AWAY.comment: Include G38.4 and G38.5 to probe away from target

G38_MINIMUM_MOVE.name: G38_MINIMUM_MOVE
G38_MINIMUM_MOVE.comment: (mm) Minimum distance that will produce a move.

MECHANICAL_GANTRY_CALIBRATION.name: MECHANICAL_GANTRY_CALIBRATION
MECHANICAL_GANTRY_CALIBRATION.comment:
Mechanical Gantry Calibration
Modern replacement for the Průša TMC_Z_CALIBRATION.
Adds capability to work with any adjustable current drivers.
Implemented as G34 because M915 is deprecated.

GANTRY_CALIBRATION_EXTRA_HEIGHT.name: GANTRY_CALIBRATION_EXTRA_HEIGHT
GANTRY_CALIBRATION_EXTRA_HEIGHT.comment: Extra distance in mm past Z_###_POS to move

GANTRY_CALIBRATION_TO_MIN.name: GANTRY_CALIBRATION_TO_MIN
GANTRY_CALIBRATION_TO_MIN.comment: Enable to calibrate Z in the MIN direction

GANTRY_CALIBRATION_COMMANDS_POST.name: GANTRY_CALIBRATION_COMMANDS_POST
GANTRY_CALIBRATION_COMMANDS_POST.comment: G28 highly recommended to ensure an accurate position

FT_MOTION.name: FT_MOTION
FT_MOTION.comment:
Fixed-time-based Motion Control -- BETA FEATURE
Enable/disable and set parameters with G-code M493 and M494.
See ft_types.h for named values used by FTM options.

FTM_IS_DEFAULT_MOTION.name: FTM_IS_DEFAULT_MOTION
FTM_IS_DEFAULT_MOTION.comment: Use FT Motion as the factory default?

FT_MOTION_MENU.name: FT_MOTION_MENU
FT_MOTION_MENU.comment: Provide a MarlinUI menu to set M493 and M494 parameters

NO_STANDARD_MOTION.name: NO_STANDARD_MOTION
NO_STANDARD_MOTION.comment: Disable the standard motion system entirely to save Flash and RAM

FTM_HOME_AND_PROBE.name: FTM_HOME_AND_PROBE
FTM_HOME_AND_PROBE.comment: Use FT Motion for homing / probing. Disable if FT Motion breaks these functions.

FTM_DYNAMIC_FREQ.name: FTM_DYNAMIC_FREQ
FTM_DYNAMIC_FREQ.comment: Enable for linear adjustment of XY shaping frequency according to Z or E

FTM_DEFAULT_DYNFREQ_MODE.name: FTM_DEFAULT_DYNFREQ_MODE
FTM_DEFAULT_DYNFREQ_MODE.comment: Default mode of dynamic frequency calculation. (DISABLED, Z_BASED, MASS_BASED)

FTM_SHAPER_ZV.name: FTM_SHAPER_ZV
FTM_SHAPER_ZV.comment: Disable unused shapers if you need more free space

FTM_DEFAULT_SHAPER_X.name: FTM_DEFAULT_SHAPER_X
FTM_DEFAULT_SHAPER_X.comment: Default shaper mode on X axis (NONE, ZV, ZVD, ZVDD, ZVDDD, EI, 2HEI, 3HEI, MZV)

FTM_SHAPING_DEFAULT_FREQ_X.name: FTM_SHAPING_DEFAULT_FREQ_X
FTM_SHAPING_DEFAULT_FREQ_X.comment: (Hz) Default peak frequency used by input shapers

FTM_SHAPING_ZETA_X.name: FTM_SHAPING_ZETA_X
FTM_SHAPING_ZETA_X.comment: Zeta used by input shapers for X axis

FTM_SHAPING_V_TOL_X.name: FTM_SHAPING_V_TOL_X
FTM_SHAPING_V_TOL_X.comment: Vibration tolerance used by EI input shapers for X axis

FTM_DEFAULT_SHAPER_Y.name: FTM_DEFAULT_SHAPER_Y
FTM_DEFAULT_SHAPER_Y.comment: Default shaper mode on Y axis

FTM_SHAPING_DEFAULT_FREQ_Y.name: FTM_SHAPING_DEFAULT_FREQ_Y
FTM_SHAPING_DEFAULT_FREQ_Y.comment: (Hz) Default peak frequency used by input shapers

FTM_SHAPING_ZETA_Y.name: FTM_SHAPING_ZETA_Y
FTM_SHAPING_ZETA_Y.comment: Zeta used by input shapers for Y axis

FTM_SHAPING_V_TOL_Y.name: FTM_SHAPING_V_TOL_Y
FTM_SHAPING_V_TOL_Y.comment: Vibration tolerance used by EI input shapers for Y axis

FTM_DEFAULT_SHAPER_Z.name: FTM_DEFAULT_SHAPER_Z
FTM_DEFAULT_SHAPER_Z.comment: Default shaper mode on Z axis

FTM_SHAPING_DEFAULT_FREQ_Z.name: FTM_SHAPING_DEFAULT_FREQ_Z
FTM_SHAPING_DEFAULT_FREQ_Z.comment: (Hz) Default peak frequency used by input shapers

FTM_SHAPING_ZETA_Z.name: FTM_SHAPING_ZETA_Z
FTM_SHAPING_ZETA_Z.comment: Zeta used by input shapers for Z axis

FTM_SHAPING_V_TOL_Z.name: FTM_SHAPING_V_TOL_Z
FTM_SHAPING_V_TOL_Z.comment: Vibration tolerance used by EI input shapers for Z axis

FTM_SHAPER_E.name: FTM_SHAPER_E
FTM_SHAPER_E.comment: Include E shaping support Required to synchronize extruder with XYZ (better quality)

FTM_DEFAULT_SHAPER_E.name: FTM_DEFAULT_SHAPER_E
FTM_DEFAULT_SHAPER_E.comment: Default shaper mode on Extruder axis

FTM_SHAPING_DEFAULT_FREQ_E.name: FTM_SHAPING_DEFAULT_FREQ_E
FTM_SHAPING_DEFAULT_FREQ_E.comment: (Hz) Default peak frequency used by input shapers

FTM_SHAPING_ZETA_E.name: FTM_SHAPING_ZETA_E
FTM_SHAPING_ZETA_E.comment: Zeta used by input shapers for E axis

FTM_SHAPING_V_TOL_E.name: FTM_SHAPING_V_TOL_E
FTM_SHAPING_V_TOL_E.comment: Vibration tolerance used by EI input shapers for E axis

FTM_RESONANCE_TEST.name: FTM_RESONANCE_TEST
FTM_RESONANCE_TEST.comment: Sine sweep motion for resonance study

FTM_SMOOTHING.name: FTM_SMOOTHING
FTM_SMOOTHING.comment: Smoothing can reduce artifacts and make steppers quieter on sharp corners, but too much will round corners.

FTM_MAX_SMOOTHING_TIME.name: FTM_MAX_SMOOTHING_TIME
FTM_MAX_SMOOTHING_TIME.comment: (s) Maximum smoothing time. Higher values consume more RAM. Increase smoothing time to reduce jerky motion, ghosting and noises.

FTM_SMOOTHING_TIME_X.name: FTM_SMOOTHING_TIME_X
FTM_SMOOTHING_TIME_X.comment: (s) Smoothing time for X axis. Zero means disabled.

FTM_SMOOTHING_TIME_E.name: FTM_SMOOTHING_TIME_E
FTM_SMOOTHING_TIME_E.comment: (s) Smoothing time for E axis. Prevents noise/skipping from LA by smoothing acceleration peaks, which may also smooth curved surfaces.

FTM_POLYS.name: FTM_POLYS
FTM_POLYS.comment: Disable POLY5/6 to save ~3k of Flash. Preserves TRAPEZOIDAL.

FTM_TRAJECTORY_TYPE.name: FTM_TRAJECTORY_TYPE
FTM_TRAJECTORY_TYPE.comment: Block acceleration profile (TRAPEZOIDAL, POLY5, POLY6) TRAPEZOIDAL: Continuous Velocity. Max acceleration is respected. POLY5:       Like POLY6 with 1.5x but uses less CPU. POLY6:       Continuous Acceleration (aka S_CURVE). POLY trajectories not only reduce resonances without rounding corners, but also reduce extruder strain due to linear advance.

FTM_POLY6_ACCELERATION_OVERSHOOT.name: FTM_POLY6_ACCELERATION_OVERSHOOT
FTM_POLY6_ACCELERATION_OVERSHOOT.comment: Max acceleration overshoot factor for POLY6 (1.25 to 1.875)

FTM_BUFFER_SIZE.name: FTM_BUFFER_SIZE
FTM_BUFFER_SIZE.notes: Window size for trajectory generation, must be a power of 2 (e.g 64, 128, 256, ...) The total buffered time in seconds is (FTM_BUFFER_SIZE/FTM_FS)

FTM_MIN_SHAPE_FREQ.name: FTM_MIN_SHAPE_FREQ
FTM_MIN_SHAPE_FREQ.comment: (Hz) Minimum shaping frequency, lower consumes more RAM

FTM_DIR_CHANGE_HOLD_X.name: FTM_DIR_CHANGE_HOLD_X
FTM_DIR_CHANGE_HOLD_X.comment:
TMC2208 / TMC2208_STANDALONE drivers require a brief pause after a DIR change
to prevent a standstill shutdown when using StealthChop (the standalone default).
These options cause FT Motion to delay for > 750µs after a DIR change on a given axis.
Disable only if you are certain that this can never happen with your TMC2208s.

INPUT_SHAPING_X.name: INPUT_SHAPING_X
INPUT_SHAPING_X.comment:
Input Shaping

Zero Vibration (ZV) Input Shaping for X and/or Y movements.

This option uses a lot of SRAM for the step buffer. The buffer size is
calculated automatically from SHAPING_FREQ_[XYZ], DEFAULT_AXIS_STEPS_PER_UNIT,
DEFAULT_MAX_FEEDRATE and ADAPTIVE_STEP_SMOOTHING. The default calculation can
be overridden by setting SHAPING_MIN_FREQ and/or SHAPING_MAX_FEEDRATE.
The higher the frequency and the lower the feedrate, the smaller the buffer.
If the buffer is too small at runtime, input shaping will have reduced
effectiveness during high speed movements.

Tune with M593 D<factor> F<frequency>

SHAPING_FREQ_X.name: SHAPING_FREQ_X
SHAPING_FREQ_X.comment: (Hz) The default dominant resonant frequency on the X axis.

SHAPING_ZETA_X.name: SHAPING_ZETA_X
SHAPING_ZETA_X.comment: Damping ratio of the X axis (range: 0.0 = no damping to 1.0 = critical damping).

SHAPING_FREQ_Y.name: SHAPING_FREQ_Y
SHAPING_FREQ_Y.comment: (Hz) The default dominant resonant frequency on the Y axis.

SHAPING_ZETA_Y.name: SHAPING_ZETA_Y
SHAPING_ZETA_Y.comment: Damping ratio of the Y axis (range: 0.0 = no damping to 1.0 = critical damping).

SHAPING_FREQ_Z.name: SHAPING_FREQ_Z
SHAPING_FREQ_Z.comment: (Hz) The default dominant resonant frequency on the Z axis.

SHAPING_ZETA_Z.name: SHAPING_ZETA_Z
SHAPING_ZETA_Z.comment: Damping ratio of the Z axis (range: 0.0 = no damping to 1.0 = critical damping).

SHAPING_MIN_FREQ.name: SHAPING_MIN_FREQ
SHAPING_MIN_FREQ.comment: (Hz) By default the minimum of the shaping frequencies. Override to affect SRAM usage.

SHAPING_MAX_STEPRATE.name: SHAPING_MAX_STEPRATE
SHAPING_MAX_STEPRATE.comment: By default the maximum total step rate of the shaped axes. Override to affect SRAM usage.

SHAPING_MENU.name: SHAPING_MENU
SHAPING_MENU.comment: Add a menu to the LCD to set shaping parameters.

MULTI_NOZZLE_DUPLICATION.name: MULTI_NOZZLE_DUPLICATION
MULTI_NOZZLE_DUPLICATION.comment: Add a Duplicate option for well-separated conjoined nozzles

STEP_STATE_X.name: STEP_STATE_X
STEP_STATE_X.comment: By default stepper drivers require an active-HIGH signal but some high-power drivers require an active-LOW signal to step.

DEFAULT_STEPPER_TIMEOUT_SEC.name: DEFAULT_STEPPER_TIMEOUT_SEC
DEFAULT_STEPPER_TIMEOUT_SEC.comment:
Idle Stepper Shutdown
Enable DISABLE_IDLE_* to shut down axis steppers after an idle period.
The default timeout duration can be overridden with M18 and M84. Set to 0 for No Timeout.

DISABLE_IDLE_Z.name: DISABLE_IDLE_Z
DISABLE_IDLE_Z.comment: Disable if the nozzle could fall onto your printed part!

DEFAULT_MINIMUMFEEDRATE.name: DEFAULT_MINIMUMFEEDRATE
DEFAULT_MINIMUMFEEDRATE.comment: Default Minimum Feedrates for printing and travel moves
DEFAULT_MINIMUMFEEDRATE.notes: (mm/s) Minimum feedrate. Set with M205 S.

DEFAULT_MINTRAVELFEEDRATE.name: DEFAULT_MINTRAVELFEEDRATE
DEFAULT_MINTRAVELFEEDRATE.comment: (mm/s) Minimum travel feedrate. Set with M205 T.

DEFAULT_MINSEGMENTTIME.name: DEFAULT_MINSEGMENTTIME
DEFAULT_MINSEGMENTTIME.comment: Minimum time that a segment needs to take as the buffer gets emptied

SLOWDOWN.name: SLOWDOWN
SLOWDOWN.comment:
Slow down the machine if the lookahead buffer is (by default) half full.
Increase the slowdown divisor for larger buffer sizes.

XY_FREQUENCY_LIMIT.name: XY_FREQUENCY_LIMIT
XY_FREQUENCY_LIMIT.comment:
XY Frequency limit
Reduce resonance by limiting the frequency of small zigzag infill moves.
See https://hydraraptor.blogspot.com/2010/12/frequency-limit.html
Use M201 F<freq> S<min%> to change limits at runtime.
XY_FREQUENCY_LIMIT.notes: (Hz) Maximum frequency of small zigzag infill moves. Set with M201 F<hertz>.

XY_FREQUENCY_MIN_PERCENT.name: XY_FREQUENCY_MIN_PERCENT
XY_FREQUENCY_MIN_PERCENT.comment: (%) Minimum FR percentage to apply. Set with M201 S<min%>.

BACKLASH_COMPENSATION.name: BACKLASH_COMPENSATION
BACKLASH_COMPENSATION.comment:
Backlash Compensation
Adds extra movement to axes on direction-changes to account for backlash.

BACKLASH_DISTANCE_MM.name: BACKLASH_DISTANCE_MM
BACKLASH_DISTANCE_MM.comment:
Define values for backlash distance and correction.
If BACKLASH_GCODE is enabled these values are the defaults.
BACKLASH_DISTANCE_MM.notes: (linear=mm, rotational=°) One value for each linear axis

CORE_BACKLASH.name: CORE_BACKLASH
CORE_BACKLASH.comment: Add steps for motor direction changes on CORE kinematics

BACKLASH_SMOOTHING_MM.name: BACKLASH_SMOOTHING_MM
BACKLASH_SMOOTHING_MM.comment:
Set BACKLASH_SMOOTHING_MM to spread backlash correction over multiple segments
to reduce print artifacts. (Enabling this is costly in memory and computation!)

BACKLASH_GCODE.name: BACKLASH_GCODE
BACKLASH_GCODE.comment: Add runtime configuration and tuning of backlash values (M425)

MEASURE_BACKLASH_WHEN_PROBING.name: MEASURE_BACKLASH_WHEN_PROBING
MEASURE_BACKLASH_WHEN_PROBING.comment: Measure the Z backlash when probing (G29) and set with "M425 Z"

BACKLASH_MEASUREMENT_LIMIT.name: BACKLASH_MEASUREMENT_LIMIT
BACKLASH_MEASUREMENT_LIMIT.comment:
When measuring, the probe will move up to BACKLASH_MEASUREMENT_LIMIT
mm away from point of contact in BACKLASH_MEASUREMENT_RESOLUTION
increments while checking for the contact to be broken.

CALIBRATION_GCODE.name: CALIBRATION_GCODE
CALIBRATION_GCODE.comment:
Automatic backlash, position, and hotend offset calibration

Enable G425 to run automatic calibration using an electrically-
conductive cube, bolt, or washer mounted on the bed.

G425 uses the probe to touch the top and sides of the calibration object
on the bed and measures and/or correct positional offsets, axis backlash
and hotend offsets.

Note: HOTEND_OFFSET and CALIBRATION_OBJECT_CENTER must be set to within
      ±5mm of true values for G425 to succeed.

CALIBRATION_NOZZLE_TIP_HEIGHT.name: CALIBRATION_NOZZLE_TIP_HEIGHT
CALIBRATION_NOZZLE_TIP_HEIGHT.comment: The following parameters refer to the conical section of the nozzle tip.

CALIBRATION_REPORTING.name: CALIBRATION_REPORTING
CALIBRATION_REPORTING.comment: Uncomment to enable reporting (required for "G425 V", but consumes flash).

CALIBRATION_OBJECT_CENTER.name: CALIBRATION_OBJECT_CENTER
CALIBRATION_OBJECT_CENTER.comment: The true location and dimension the cube/bolt/washer on the bed.

CALIBRATION_MEASURE_RIGHT.name: CALIBRATION_MEASURE_RIGHT
CALIBRATION_MEASURE_RIGHT.comment:
Comment out any sides which are unreachable by the probe. For best
auto-calibration results, all sides must be reachable.

CALIBRATION_MEASURE_AT_TOP_EDGES.name: CALIBRATION_MEASURE_AT_TOP_EDGES
CALIBRATION_MEASURE_AT_TOP_EDGES.comment:
Probing at the exact top center only works if the center is flat. If
probing on a screw head or hollow washer, probe near the edges.

CALIBRATION_PIN.name: CALIBRATION_PIN
CALIBRATION_PIN.comment: Define the pin to read during calibration
CALIBRATION_PIN.notes: Define here to override the default pin

CALIBRATION_PIN_INVERTING.name: CALIBRATION_PIN_INVERTING
CALIBRATION_PIN_INVERTING.comment: Set to true to invert the custom pin

MULTISTEPPING_LIMIT.name: MULTISTEPPING_LIMIT
MULTISTEPPING_LIMIT.comment:
Multi-stepping sends steps in bursts to reduce MCU usage for high step-rates.
This allows higher feedrates than the MCU could otherwise support.

ADAPTIVE_STEP_SMOOTHING.name: ADAPTIVE_STEP_SMOOTHING
ADAPTIVE_STEP_SMOOTHING.comment:
Adaptive Step Smoothing increases the resolution of multi-axis moves, particularly at step frequencies
below 1kHz (for AVR) or 10kHz (for ARM), where aliasing between axes in multi-axis moves causes audible
vibration and surface artifacts. The algorithm adapts to provide the best possible step smoothing at the
lowest stepping frequencies.

MICROSTEP1.name: MICROSTEP1
MICROSTEP1.comment:
Custom Microstepping
Override as-needed for your setup. Up to 3 MS pins are supported.

MICROSTEP_MODES.name: MICROSTEP_MODES
MICROSTEP_MODES.comment: Microstep settings (Requires a board with pins named X_MS1, X_MS2, etc.)

MIN_ARC_SEGMENT_MM.name: MIN_ARC_SEGMENT_MM
MIN_ARC_SEGMENT_MM.comment: (mm) Minimum length of each arc segment

MAX_ARC_SEGMENT_MM.name: MAX_ARC_SEGMENT_MM
MAX_ARC_SEGMENT_MM.comment: (mm) Maximum length of each arc segment

MIN_CIRCLE_SEGMENTS.name: MIN_CIRCLE_SEGMENTS
MIN_CIRCLE_SEGMENTS.comment: Minimum number of segments in a complete circle

ARC_SEGMENTS_PER_SEC.name: ARC_SEGMENTS_PER_SEC
ARC_SEGMENTS_PER_SEC.comment: Use the feedrate to choose the segment length

N_ARC_CORRECTION.name: N_ARC_CORRECTION
N_ARC_CORRECTION.comment: Number of interpolated segments between corrections

ARC_P_CIRCLES.name: ARC_P_CIRCLES
ARC_P_CIRCLES.comment: Enable the 'P' parameter to specify complete circles

BEZIER_CURVE_SUPPORT.name: BEZIER_CURVE_SUPPORT
BEZIER_CURVE_SUPPORT.comment: G5 Bézier Curve Support with XYZE destination and IJPQ offsets

CNC_WORKSPACE_PLANES.name: CNC_WORKSPACE_PLANES
CNC_WORKSPACE_PLANES.comment: Allow G2/G3/G5 to operate in XY, ZX, or YZ planes

DIRECT_STEPPING.name: DIRECT_STEPPING
DIRECT_STEPPING.comment:
Direct Stepping

Comparable to the method used by Klipper, G6 direct stepping significantly
reduces motion calculations, increases top printing speeds, and results in
less step aliasing by calculating all motions in advance.
Preparing your G-code: https://github.com/colinrgodsey/step-daemon

MIN_STEPS_PER_SEGMENT.name: MIN_STEPS_PER_SEGMENT
MIN_STEPS_PER_SEGMENT.comment: Moves (or segments) with fewer steps than this will be joined with the next move

MINIMUM_STEPPER_POST_DIR_DELAY.name: MINIMUM_STEPPER_POST_DIR_DELAY
MINIMUM_STEPPER_POST_DIR_DELAY.comment:
Minimum delay before and after setting the stepper DIR (in ns)
    0 : No delay (Expect at least 10µS since one Stepper ISR must transpire)
   20 : Minimum for TMC2xxx drivers
  200 : Minimum for A4988 drivers
  400 : Minimum for A5984 drivers
  500 : Minimum for LV8729 drivers (guess, no info in datasheet)
  650 : Minimum for DRV8825 drivers
 1500 : Minimum for TB6600 drivers (guess, no info in datasheet)
15000 : Minimum for TB6560 drivers (guess, no info in datasheet)

Override the default value based on the driver type set in Configuration.h.

MINIMUM_STEPPER_PULSE_NS.name: MINIMUM_STEPPER_PULSE_NS
MINIMUM_STEPPER_PULSE_NS.comment:
Minimum stepper driver pulse width (in ns)
If undefined, these defaults (from Conditionals-4-adv.h) apply:
    100 : Minimum for TMC2xxx stepper drivers
    500 : Minimum for LV8729
   1000 : Minimum for A4988 and A5984 stepper drivers
   2000 : Minimum for DRV8825 stepper drivers
   3000 : Minimum for TB6600 stepper drivers
  30000 : Minimum for TB6560 stepper drivers

Override the default value based on the driver type set in Configuration.h.

MAXIMUM_STEPPER_RATE.name: MAXIMUM_STEPPER_RATE
MAXIMUM_STEPPER_RATE.comment:
Maximum stepping rate (in Hz) the stepper driver allows
If undefined, these defaults (from Conditionals-4-adv.h) apply:
 5000000 : Maximum for TMC2xxx stepper drivers
 1000000 : Maximum for LV8729 stepper driver
  500000 : Maximum for A4988 stepper driver
  250000 : Maximum for DRV8825 stepper driver
  150000 : Maximum for TB6600 stepper driver
   15000 : Maximum for TB6560 stepper driver

Override the default value based on the driver type set in Configuration.h.

PWM_MOTOR_CURRENT.name: PWM_MOTOR_CURRENT
PWM_MOTOR_CURRENT.comment:
Some boards have a means of setting the stepper motor current via firmware.

The power on motor currents are set by:
  PWM_MOTOR_CURRENT - used by MINIRAMBO & ULTIMAIN_2
                       known compatible chips: A4982
  DIGIPOT_MOTOR_CURRENT - used by BQ_ZUM_MEGA_3D, RAMBO & SCOOVO_X9H
                       known compatible chips: AD5206
  DAC_MOTOR_CURRENT_DEFAULT - used by PRINTRBOARD_REVF & RIGIDBOARD_V2
                       known compatible chips: MCP4728
  DIGIPOT_I2C_MOTOR_CURRENTS - used by 5DPRINT, AZTEEG_X3_PRO, AZTEEG_X5_MINI_WIFI, MIGHTYBOARD_REVE
                       known compatible chips: MCP4451, MCP4018

Motor currents can also be set by M907 - M910 and by the LCD.
  M907 - applies to all.
  M908 - BQ_ZUM_MEGA_3D, RAMBO, PRINTRBOARD_REVF, RIGIDBOARD_V2 & SCOOVO_X9H
  M909, M910 & LCD - only PRINTRBOARD_REVF & RIGIDBOARD_V2

DAC_MOTOR_CURRENT_DEFAULT.name: DAC_MOTOR_CURRENT_DEFAULT
DAC_MOTOR_CURRENT_DEFAULT.comment: Default drive percent - X, Y, Z, E axis

DIGIPOT_I2C_MOTOR_CURRENTS.name: DIGIPOT_I2C_MOTOR_CURRENTS
DIGIPOT_I2C_MOTOR_CURRENTS.comment:
Actual motor currents in Amps. The number of entries must match DIGIPOT_I2C_NUM_CHANNELS.
These correspond to the physical drivers, so be mindful if the order is changed.

DIGIPOT_USE_RAW_VALUES.name: DIGIPOT_USE_RAW_VALUES
DIGIPOT_USE_RAW_VALUES.comment: Use DIGIPOT_MOTOR_CURRENT raw wiper values (instead of A4988 motor currents)

DIGIPOT_I2C_ADDRESS_A.name: DIGIPOT_I2C_ADDRESS_A
DIGIPOT_I2C_ADDRESS_A.comment:
Common slave addresses:

                       A   (A shifted)   B   (B shifted)  IC
Smoothie              0x2C (0x58)       0x2D (0x5A)       MCP4451
AZTEEG_X3_PRO         0x2C (0x58)       0x2E (0x5C)       MCP4451
AZTEEG_X5_MINI        0x2C (0x58)       0x2E (0x5C)       MCP4451
AZTEEG_X5_MINI_WIFI         0x58              0x5C        MCP4451
MIGHTYBOARD_REVE      0x2F (0x5E)                         MCP4018
DIGIPOT_I2C_ADDRESS_A.notes: Unshifted slave address for first DIGIPOT

DIGIPOT_I2C_ADDRESS_B.name: DIGIPOT_I2C_ADDRESS_B
DIGIPOT_I2C_ADDRESS_B.comment: Unshifted slave address for second DIGIPOT

DISABLE_REDUCED_ACCURACY_WARNING.name: DISABLE_REDUCED_ACCURACY_WARNING
DISABLE_REDUCED_ACCURACY_WARNING.comment: Turn off the display blinking that warns about possible accuracy reduction

MANUAL_FEEDRATE.name: MANUAL_FEEDRATE
MANUAL_FEEDRATE.comment: (mm/min) Feedrates for manual moves along X, Y, Z, E from panel

FINE_MANUAL_MOVE.name: FINE_MANUAL_MOVE
FINE_MANUAL_MOVE.comment: (mm) Smallest manual move (< 0.1mm) applying to Z on most machines

MANUAL_E_MOVES_RELATIVE.name: MANUAL_E_MOVES_RELATIVE
MANUAL_E_MOVES_RELATIVE.comment: Display extruder move distance rather than "position"

ULTIPANEL_FEEDMULTIPLY.name: ULTIPANEL_FEEDMULTIPLY
ULTIPANEL_FEEDMULTIPLY.comment: Encoder sets the feedrate multiplier on the Status Screen

ULTIPANEL_FLOWPERCENT.name: ULTIPANEL_FLOWPERCENT
ULTIPANEL_FLOWPERCENT.comment: Encoder sets the flow percentage on the Status Screen

ENCODER_RATE_MULTIPLIER.name: ENCODER_RATE_MULTIPLIER
ENCODER_RATE_MULTIPLIER.comment: Change values more rapidly when the encoder is rotated faster

BEEP_ON_FEEDRATE_CHANGE.name: BEEP_ON_FEEDRATE_CHANGE
BEEP_ON_FEEDRATE_CHANGE.comment: Play a beep when the feedrate is changed from the Status Screen

PROBE_OFFSET_WIZARD.name: PROBE_OFFSET_WIZARD
PROBE_OFFSET_WIZARD.comment:
Probe Offset Wizard
Add a Probe Z Offset calibration option to the LCD menu.
Use this helper to get a perfect 'M851 Z' probe offset.
When launched this powerful wizard:
 - Measures the bed height at the configured position with the probe.
 - Moves the nozzle to the same position for a "paper" measurement.
 - The difference is used to set the probe Z offset.

PROBE_OFFSET_WIZARD_START_Z.name: PROBE_OFFSET_WIZARD_START_Z
PROBE_OFFSET_WIZARD_START_Z.comment:
Enable to init the Probe Z-Offset when starting the Wizard.
Use a height slightly above the estimated nozzle-to-probe Z offset.
For example, with an offset of -5, consider a starting height of -4.

PROBE_OFFSET_WIZARD_XY_POS.name: PROBE_OFFSET_WIZARD_XY_POS
PROBE_OFFSET_WIZARD_XY_POS.comment: Set a convenient position to do the calibration (probing point and nozzle/bed-distance)

PROBE_DEPLOY_STOW_MENU.name: PROBE_DEPLOY_STOW_MENU
PROBE_DEPLOY_STOW_MENU.comment: Show Deploy / Stow Probe options in the Motion menu.

X_AXIS_TWIST_COMPENSATION.name: X_AXIS_TWIST_COMPENSATION
X_AXIS_TWIST_COMPENSATION.comment: Add calibration in the Probe Offsets menu to compensate for X-axis twist.

XATC_START_Z.name: XATC_START_Z
XATC_START_Z.comment:
Enable to init the Probe Z-Offset when starting the Wizard.
Use a height slightly above the estimated nozzle-to-probe Z offset.
For example, with an offset of -5, consider a starting height of -4.

XATC_MAX_POINTS.name: XATC_MAX_POINTS
XATC_MAX_POINTS.comment: Number of points to probe in the wizard

XATC_Z_OFFSETS.name: XATC_Z_OFFSETS
XATC_Z_OFFSETS.comment: Z offsets for X axis sample points

LCD_INFO_MENU.name: LCD_INFO_MENU
LCD_INFO_MENU.comment: Include a page of printer information in the LCD Main Menu

LCD_PRINTER_INFO_IS_BOOTSCREEN.name: LCD_PRINTER_INFO_IS_BOOTSCREEN
LCD_PRINTER_INFO_IS_BOOTSCREEN.comment: Show bootscreen(s) instead of Printer Info pages

BUILD_INFO_MENU_ITEM.name: BUILD_INFO_MENU_ITEM
BUILD_INFO_MENU_ITEM.comment: Add a menu item to display the build date and time

MANUAL_MOVE_DISTANCE_MM.name: MANUAL_MOVE_DISTANCE_MM
MANUAL_MOVE_DISTANCE_MM.comment:
MarlinUI "Move Axis" menu distances. Comma-separated list.
Values are displayed as-defined, so always use plain numbers here.
Axis moves <= 1/2 the axis length and Extruder moves <= EXTRUDE_MAXLENGTH
will be shown in the move submenus.

MANUAL_MOVE_DISTANCE_DEG.name: MANUAL_MOVE_DISTANCE_DEG
MANUAL_MOVE_DISTANCE_DEG.comment: Manual move distances for rotational axes

TURBO_BACK_MENU_ITEM.name: TURBO_BACK_MENU_ITEM
TURBO_BACK_MENU_ITEM.comment: BACK menu items keep the highlight at the top

GENERIC_BACK_MENU_ITEM.name: GENERIC_BACK_MENU_ITEM
GENERIC_BACK_MENU_ITEM.comment: BACK menu items show "Back" instead of the previous menu name

PREHEAT_SHORTCUT_MENU_ITEM.name: PREHEAT_SHORTCUT_MENU_ITEM
PREHEAT_SHORTCUT_MENU_ITEM.comment: Insert a menu for preheating at the top level to allow for quick access

LCD_ENDSTOP_TEST.name: LCD_ENDSTOP_TEST
LCD_ENDSTOP_TEST.comment: Add Configuration > Debug Menu > Endstop Test for endstop/probe/runout testing

SHOW_BOOTSCREEN.name: SHOW_BOOTSCREEN
SHOW_BOOTSCREEN.comment:
*** VENDORS PLEASE READ ***

Marlin allows you to add a custom boot image for Graphical LCDs.
With this option Marlin will first show your custom screen followed
by the standard Marlin logo with version number and web URL.

We encourage you to take advantage of this new feature and we also
respectfully request that you retain the unmodified Marlin boot screen.
SHOW_BOOTSCREEN.notes: Show the Marlin bootscreen on startup. ** ENABLE FOR PRODUCTION **

BOOTSCREEN_TIMEOUT.name: BOOTSCREEN_TIMEOUT
BOOTSCREEN_TIMEOUT.comment: (ms) Total Duration to display the boot screen(s)

BOOT_MARLIN_LOGO_SMALL.name: BOOT_MARLIN_LOGO_SMALL
BOOT_MARLIN_LOGO_SMALL.comment: Show a smaller Marlin logo on the Boot Screen (saving lots of flash)

BOOT_MARLIN_LOGO_ANIMATED.name: BOOT_MARLIN_LOGO_ANIMATED
BOOT_MARLIN_LOGO_ANIMATED.comment: Animated Marlin logo. Costs ~3260 (or ~940) bytes of flash.

SHOW_CUSTOM_BOOTSCREEN.name: SHOW_CUSTOM_BOOTSCREEN
SHOW_CUSTOM_BOOTSCREEN.comment: Show the bitmap in Marlin/_Bootscreen.h on startup.

CUSTOM_STATUS_SCREEN_IMAGE.name: CUSTOM_STATUS_SCREEN_IMAGE
CUSTOM_STATUS_SCREEN_IMAGE.comment: Show the bitmap in Marlin/_Statusscreen.h on the status screen.

SOUND_MENU_ITEM.name: SOUND_MENU_ITEM
SOUND_MENU_ITEM.comment: Add a mute option to the LCD menu

LCD_DOUBLE_BUFFER.name: LCD_DOUBLE_BUFFER
LCD_DOUBLE_BUFFER.comment: Optimize display updates. Costs ~1K of SRAM.

LCD_TIMEOUT_TO_STATUS.name: LCD_TIMEOUT_TO_STATUS
LCD_TIMEOUT_TO_STATUS.comment: The timeout to return to the status screen from sub-menus

STATUS_MESSAGE_SCROLLING.name: STATUS_MESSAGE_SCROLLING
STATUS_MESSAGE_SCROLLING.comment: Scroll a longer status message into view

STATUS_MESSAGE_TIMEOUT_SEC.name: STATUS_MESSAGE_TIMEOUT_SEC
STATUS_MESSAGE_TIMEOUT_SEC.comment: Apply a timeout to low-priority status messages

LCD_DECIMAL_SMALL_XY.name: LCD_DECIMAL_SMALL_XY
LCD_DECIMAL_SMALL_XY.comment: On the Info Screen, display XY with one decimal place when possible

LCD_SHOW_E_TOTAL.name: LCD_SHOW_E_TOTAL
LCD_SHOW_E_TOTAL.comment: Show the E position (filament used) during printing

SHOW_TEMPERATURE_BELOW_ZERO.name: SHOW_TEMPERATURE_BELOW_ZERO
SHOW_TEMPERATURE_BELOW_ZERO.comment: Display a negative temperature instead of "err"

LED_CONTROL_MENU.name: LED_CONTROL_MENU
LED_CONTROL_MENU.comment:
LED Control Menu
Add LED Control to the LCD menu

LED_COLOR_PRESETS.name: LED_COLOR_PRESETS
LED_COLOR_PRESETS.comment: Enable the Preset Color menu option

NEO2_COLOR_PRESETS.name: NEO2_COLOR_PRESETS
NEO2_COLOR_PRESETS.comment: Enable a second NeoPixel Preset Color menu option

LED_USER_PRESET_STARTUP.name: LED_USER_PRESET_STARTUP
LED_USER_PRESET_STARTUP.comment: Have the printer display the user preset color on startup

NEO2_USER_PRESET_STARTUP.name: NEO2_USER_PRESET_STARTUP
