NEO2_USER_PRESET_STARTUP.comment: Have the printer display the user preset color on startup for the second strip

SPEED_EDIT_MIN.name: SPEED_EDIT_MIN
SPEED_EDIT_MIN.comment: Some displays offer Feedrate / Flow editing.

SET_PROGRESS_MANUALLY.name: SET_PROGRESS_MANUALLY
SET_PROGRESS_MANUALLY.comment: Add 'M73' to set print job progress, overrides Marlin's built-in estimate

SET_PROGRESS_PERCENT.name: SET_PROGRESS_PERCENT
SET_PROGRESS_PERCENT.comment: Add 'P' parameter to set percentage done

SET_REMAINING_TIME.name: SET_REMAINING_TIME
SET_REMAINING_TIME.comment: Add 'R' parameter to set remaining time

SET_INTERACTION_TIME.name: SET_INTERACTION_TIME
SET_INTERACTION_TIME.comment: Add 'C' parameter to set time until next filament change or other user interaction

M73_REPORT_SD_ONLY.name: M73_REPORT_SD_ONLY
M73_REPORT_SD_ONLY.comment: Report only when printing from SD

SHOW_PROGRESS_PERCENT.name: SHOW_PROGRESS_PERCENT
SHOW_PROGRESS_PERCENT.comment: LCD Print Progress options. Multiple times may be displayed in turn.
SHOW_PROGRESS_PERCENT.notes: Show print progress percentage (doesn't affect progress bar)

SHOW_INTERACTION_TIME.name: SHOW_INTERACTION_TIME
SHOW_INTERACTION_TIME.comment: Display time until next user interaction ('C' = filament change)

PRINT_PROGRESS_SHOW_DECIMALS.name: PRINT_PROGRESS_SHOW_DECIMALS
PRINT_PROGRESS_SHOW_DECIMALS.comment: Show/report progress with decimal digits, not all UIs support this

LCD_PROGRESS_BAR.name: LCD_PROGRESS_BAR
LCD_PROGRESS_BAR.comment: Show a progress bar on HD44780 LCDs for SD printing

PROGRESS_BAR_BAR_TIME.name: PROGRESS_BAR_BAR_TIME
PROGRESS_BAR_BAR_TIME.comment: (ms) Amount of time to show the bar

PROGRESS_BAR_MSG_TIME.name: PROGRESS_BAR_MSG_TIME
PROGRESS_BAR_MSG_TIME.comment: (ms) Amount of time to show the status message

PROGRESS_MSG_EXPIRE.name: PROGRESS_MSG_EXPIRE
PROGRESS_MSG_EXPIRE.comment: (ms) Amount of time to retain the status message (0=forever)

PROGRESS_MSG_ONCE.name: PROGRESS_MSG_ONCE
PROGRESS_MSG_ONCE.comment: Show the message for MSG_TIME then clear it

LCD_PROGRESS_BAR_TEST.name: LCD_PROGRESS_BAR_TEST
LCD_PROGRESS_BAR_TEST.comment: Add a menu item to test the progress bar

SD_SPI_SPEED.name: SD_SPI_SPEED
SD_SPI_SPEED.comment:
SD Card SPI Speed
May be required to resolve "volume init" errors.

Enable and set to SPI_HALF_SPEED, SPI_QUARTER_SPEED, or SPI_EIGHTH_SPEED
 otherwise full speed will be applied.

REINIT_NOISY_LCD.name: REINIT_NOISY_LCD
REINIT_NOISY_LCD.comment:
Reinit the LCD after SD Card insert/remove or when entering the menu.
Required for some LCDs that use shared SPI with an external SD Card reader.

SD_DETECT_STATE.name: SD_DETECT_STATE
SD_DETECT_STATE.comment:
The standard SD detect circuit reads LOW when media is inserted and HIGH when empty.
Enable this option and set to HIGH if your SD cards are incorrectly detected.

SD_IGNORE_AT_STARTUP.name: SD_IGNORE_AT_STARTUP
SD_IGNORE_AT_STARTUP.comment: Don't mount the SD card when starting up

SDCARD_READONLY.name: SDCARD_READONLY
SDCARD_READONLY.comment: Read-only SD card (to save over 2K of flash)

GCODE_REPEAT_MARKERS.name: GCODE_REPEAT_MARKERS
GCODE_REPEAT_MARKERS.comment: Enable G-code M808 to set repeat markers and do looping

SD_PROCEDURE_DEPTH.name: SD_PROCEDURE_DEPTH
SD_PROCEDURE_DEPTH.comment: Increase if you need more nested M32 calls

SD_FINISHED_STEPPERRELEASE.name: SD_FINISHED_STEPPERRELEASE
SD_FINISHED_STEPPERRELEASE.comment: Disable steppers when SD Print is finished

SD_FINISHED_RELEASECOMMAND.name: SD_FINISHED_RELEASECOMMAND
SD_FINISHED_RELEASECOMMAND.comment: Use "M84XYE" to keep Z enabled so your bed stays in place

SDCARD_RATHERRECENTFIRST.name: SDCARD_RATHERRECENTFIRST
SDCARD_RATHERRECENTFIRST.comment:
Reverse SD sort to show "more recent" files first, according to the card's FAT.
Since the FAT gets out of order with usage, SDCARD_SORT_ALPHA is recommended.

SD_MENU_CONFIRM_START.name: SD_MENU_CONFIRM_START
SD_MENU_CONFIRM_START.comment: Confirm the selected SD file before printing

NO_SD_AUTOSTART.name: NO_SD_AUTOSTART
NO_SD_AUTOSTART.comment: Remove auto#.g file support completely to save some Flash, SRAM

MENU_ADDAUTOSTART.name: MENU_ADDAUTOSTART
MENU_ADDAUTOSTART.comment: Add a menu option to run auto#.g files

ONE_CLICK_PRINT.name: ONE_CLICK_PRINT
ONE_CLICK_PRINT.comment: Prompt to print the newest file on inserted media

BROWSE_MEDIA_ON_INSERT.name: BROWSE_MEDIA_ON_INSERT
BROWSE_MEDIA_ON_INSERT.comment: Open the file browser when media is inserted

MEDIA_MENU_AT_TOP.name: MEDIA_MENU_AT_TOP
MEDIA_MENU_AT_TOP.comment: Force the media menu to be listed on the top of the main menu

EVENT_GCODE_SD_ABORT.name: EVENT_GCODE_SD_ABORT
EVENT_GCODE_SD_ABORT.comment: G-code to run on SD Abort Print (e.g., "G28XY" or "G27")

PE_LEDS_COMPLETED_TIME.name: PE_LEDS_COMPLETED_TIME
PE_LEDS_COMPLETED_TIME.comment: (seconds) Time to keep the LED "done" color before restoring normal illumination

REMAINING_TIME_PRIME.name: REMAINING_TIME_PRIME
REMAINING_TIME_PRIME.comment:
Priming for the Remaining Time estimate
Long processes at the start of a G-code file can skew the Remaining Time estimate.
Enable these options to start this estimation at a later point in the G-code file.
REMAINING_TIME_PRIME.notes: Provide G-code 'M75 R' to prime the Remaining Time estimate

REMAINING_TIME_AUTOPRIME.name: REMAINING_TIME_AUTOPRIME
REMAINING_TIME_AUTOPRIME.comment: Prime the Remaining Time estimate later (e.g., at the end of 'M109')

POWER_LOSS_RECOVERY.name: POWER_LOSS_RECOVERY
POWER_LOSS_RECOVERY.comment:
Continue after Power-Loss (Creality3D)

Store the current state to the SD Card at the start of each layer
during SD printing. If the recovery file is found at boot time, present
an option on the LCD screen to continue the print from the last-known
point in the file.

PLR_ENABLED_DEFAULT.name: PLR_ENABLED_DEFAULT
PLR_ENABLED_DEFAULT.comment: Power-Loss Recovery enabled by default. (Set with 'M413 Sn' & M500)

PLR_HEAT_BED_ON_REBOOT.name: PLR_HEAT_BED_ON_REBOOT
PLR_HEAT_BED_ON_REBOOT.comment: Heat up bed immediately on reboot to mitigate object detaching/warping.

PLR_HEAT_BED_EXTRA.name: PLR_HEAT_BED_EXTRA
PLR_HEAT_BED_EXTRA.comment: (°C) Relative increase of bed temperature for better adhesion (limited by max temp).

PLR_BED_THRESHOLD.name: PLR_BED_THRESHOLD
PLR_BED_THRESHOLD.comment: (°C) Skip user confirmation at or above this bed temperature (0 to disable)

POWER_LOSS_PIN.name: POWER_LOSS_PIN
POWER_LOSS_PIN.comment: Pin to detect power-loss. Set to -1 to disable default pin on boards without module, or comment to use board default.

POWER_LOSS_PULLUP.name: POWER_LOSS_PULLUP
POWER_LOSS_PULLUP.comment: Set pullup / pulldown as appropriate for your sensor

POWER_LOSS_ZRAISE.name: POWER_LOSS_ZRAISE
POWER_LOSS_ZRAISE.comment: (mm) Z axis raise on resume (on power-loss with UPS)

POWER_LOSS_PURGE_LEN.name: POWER_LOSS_PURGE_LEN
POWER_LOSS_PURGE_LEN.comment: (mm) Length of filament to purge on resume

POWER_LOSS_MIN_Z_CHANGE.name: POWER_LOSS_MIN_Z_CHANGE
POWER_LOSS_MIN_Z_CHANGE.comment:
Without a POWER_LOSS_PIN the following option helps reduce wear on the SD card,
especially with "vase mode" printing. Set too high and vases cannot be continued.
POWER_LOSS_MIN_Z_CHANGE.notes: (mm) Minimum Z change before saving power-loss data

BACKUP_POWER_SUPPLY.name: BACKUP_POWER_SUPPLY
BACKUP_POWER_SUPPLY.comment: Backup power / UPS to move the steppers on power-loss

POWER_LOSS_RETRACT_LEN.name: POWER_LOSS_RETRACT_LEN
POWER_LOSS_RETRACT_LEN.comment: (mm) Length of filament to retract on fail

POWER_LOSS_RECOVER_ZHOME.name: POWER_LOSS_RECOVER_ZHOME
POWER_LOSS_RECOVER_ZHOME.comment: Enable if Z homing is needed for proper recovery. 99.9% of the time this should be disabled!

POWER_LOSS_ZHOME_POS.name: POWER_LOSS_ZHOME_POS
POWER_LOSS_ZHOME_POS.comment: Safe XY position to home Z while avoiding objects on the bed

SDCARD_SORT_ALPHA.name: SDCARD_SORT_ALPHA
SDCARD_SORT_ALPHA.comment:
Sort SD file listings in alphabetical order.

With this option enabled, items on SD cards will be sorted
by name for easier navigation.

By default...

 - Use the slowest -but safest- method for sorting.
 - Folders are sorted to the top.
 - The sort key is statically allocated.
 - No added G-code (M34) support.
 - 40 item sorting limit. (Items after the first 40 are unsorted.)

SD sorting uses static allocation (as set by SDSORT_LIMIT), allowing the
compiler to calculate the worst-case usage and throw an error if the SRAM
limit is exceeded.

 - SDSORT_USES_RAM provides faster sorting via a static directory buffer.
 - SDSORT_USES_STACK does the same, but uses a local stack-based buffer.
 - SDSORT_CACHE_NAMES will retain the sorted file listing in RAM. (Expensive!)
 - SDSORT_DYNAMIC_RAM only uses RAM when the SD menu is visible. (Use with caution!)

SDSORT_QUICK.name: SDSORT_QUICK
SDSORT_QUICK.notes: Use Quick Sort as a sorting algorithm. Otherwise use Bubble Sort.

SDSORT_REVERSE.name: SDSORT_REVERSE
SDSORT_REVERSE.comment: Default to sorting file names in reverse order.

SDSORT_LIMIT.name: SDSORT_LIMIT
SDSORT_LIMIT.comment: Maximum number of sorted items (10-256). Costs 27 bytes each.

SDSORT_GCODE.name: SDSORT_GCODE
SDSORT_GCODE.comment: Enable G-code M34 to set sorting behaviors: M34 S<-1|0|1> F<-1|0|1>

SDSORT_USES_STACK.name: SDSORT_USES_STACK
SDSORT_USES_STACK.comment: Prefer the stack for pre-sorting to give back some SRAM. (Negated by next 2 options.)

SDSORT_USES_RAM.name: SDSORT_USES_RAM
SDSORT_USES_RAM.comment: Pre-allocate a static array for faster pre-sorting.

SDSORT_CACHE_NAMES.name: SDSORT_CACHE_NAMES
SDSORT_CACHE_NAMES.comment: Keep sorted items in RAM longer for speedy performance. Most expensive option.

SDSORT_DYNAMIC_RAM.name: SDSORT_DYNAMIC_RAM
SDSORT_DYNAMIC_RAM.comment: Use dynamic allocation (within SD menus). Least expensive option. Set SDSORT_LIMIT before use!

SDSORT_CACHE_VFATS.name: SDSORT_CACHE_VFATS
SDSORT_CACHE_VFATS.comment: Maximum number of 13-byte VFAT entries to use for sorting. Note: Only affects SCROLL_LONG_FILENAMES with SDSORT_CACHE_NAMES but not SDSORT_DYNAMIC_RAM.

UTF_FILENAME_SUPPORT.name: UTF_FILENAME_SUPPORT
UTF_FILENAME_SUPPORT.comment:
Allow international symbols in long filenames. To display correctly, the
LCD's font must contain the characters. Check your selected LCD language.

LONG_FILENAME_HOST_SUPPORT.name: LONG_FILENAME_HOST_SUPPORT
LONG_FILENAME_HOST_SUPPORT.comment: Get the long filename of a file/folder with 'M33 <dosname>' and list long filenames with 'M20 L'

LONG_FILENAME_WRITE_SUPPORT.name: LONG_FILENAME_WRITE_SUPPORT
LONG_FILENAME_WRITE_SUPPORT.comment: Create / delete files with long filenames via M28, M30, and Binary Transfer Protocol

M20_TIMESTAMP_SUPPORT.name: M20_TIMESTAMP_SUPPORT
M20_TIMESTAMP_SUPPORT.comment: Include timestamps by adding the 'T' flag to M20 commands

SCROLL_LONG_FILENAMES.name: SCROLL_LONG_FILENAMES
SCROLL_LONG_FILENAMES.comment: Scroll long filenames in the SD card menu

SD_ABORT_NO_COOLDOWN.name: SD_ABORT_NO_COOLDOWN
SD_ABORT_NO_COOLDOWN.comment: Leave the heaters on after Stop Print (not recommended!)

SD_ABORT_ON_ENDSTOP_HIT.name: SD_ABORT_ON_ENDSTOP_HIT
SD_ABORT_ON_ENDSTOP_HIT.comment:
Abort SD printing when any endstop is triggered.
This feature is enabled with 'M540 S1' or from the LCD menu.
Endstops must be activated for this option to work.

SD_ABORT_ON_ENDSTOP_HIT_GCODE.name: SD_ABORT_ON_ENDSTOP_HIT_GCODE
SD_ABORT_ON_ENDSTOP_HIT_GCODE.comment: G-code to run on endstop hit (e.g., "G28XY" or "G27")

SD_REPRINT_LAST_SELECTED_FILE.name: SD_REPRINT_LAST_SELECTED_FILE
SD_REPRINT_LAST_SELECTED_FILE.comment: On print completion open the LCD Menu and select the same file

USB_FLASH_DRIVE_SUPPORT.name: USB_FLASH_DRIVE_SUPPORT
USB_FLASH_DRIVE_SUPPORT.comment:
Support for USB thumb drives using an Arduino USB Host Shield or
equivalent MAX3421E breakout board. The USB thumb drive will appear
to Marlin as an SD card.

The MAX3421E can be assigned the same pins as the SD card reader, with
the following pin mapping:

   SCLK, MOSI, MISO --> SCLK, MOSI, MISO
   INT              --> SD_DETECT_PIN [1]
   SS               --> SD_SS_PIN

[1] On AVR an interrupt-capable pin is best for UHS3 compatibility.

USE_UHS2_USB.name: USE_UHS2_USB
USE_UHS2_USB.comment:
USB Host Shield Library

- UHS2 uses no interrupts and has been production-tested
  on a LulzBot TAZ Pro with a 32-bit Archim board.

- UHS3 is newer code with better USB compatibility. But it
  is less tested and is known to interfere with Servos.
  [1] This requires USB_INTR_PIN to be interrupt-capable.

DISABLE_DUE_SD_MMC.name: DISABLE_DUE_SD_MMC
DISABLE_DUE_SD_MMC.comment: Disable USB Host access to USB Drive to prevent hangs on block access for DUE platform

USE_OTG_USB_HOST.name: USE_OTG_USB_HOST
USE_OTG_USB_HOST.comment: Native USB Host supported by some boards (USB OTG)

SD_FIRMWARE_UPDATE.name: SD_FIRMWARE_UPDATE
SD_FIRMWARE_UPDATE.comment:
When using a bootloader that supports SD-Firmware-Flashing,
add a menu item to activate SD-FW-Update on the next reboot.

Requires ATMEGA2560 (Arduino Mega)

Tested with this bootloader:
  https://github.com/FleetProbe/MicroBridge-Arduino-ATMega2560

CONFIGURATION_EMBEDDING.name: CONFIGURATION_EMBEDDING
CONFIGURATION_EMBEDDING.comment:
Enable this option if you have more than ~3K of unused flash space.
Marlin will embed all settings in the firmware binary as compressed data.
Use 'M503 C' to write the settings out to the SD Card as 'mc.zip'.
See docs/ConfigEmbedding.md for details on how to use 'mc-apply.py'.

BINARY_FILE_TRANSFER.name: BINARY_FILE_TRANSFER
BINARY_FILE_TRANSFER.comment: Add an optimized binary file transfer mode, initiated with 'M28 B1'

CUSTOM_FIRMWARE_UPLOAD.name: CUSTOM_FIRMWARE_UPLOAD
CUSTOM_FIRMWARE_UPLOAD.comment: Include extra facilities (e.g., 'M20 F') supporting firmware upload via BINARY_FILE_TRANSFER

OTA_FIRMWARE_UPDATE.name: OTA_FIRMWARE_UPDATE
OTA_FIRMWARE_UPDATE.comment: "Over-the-air" Firmware Update with M936 - Required to set EEPROM flag

SDCARD_CONNECTION.name: SDCARD_CONNECTION
SDCARD_CONNECTION.comment:
Set this option to one of the following (or the board's defaults apply):

          LCD - Use the SD drive in the external LCD controller.
      ONBOARD - Use the SD drive on the control board.
 CUSTOM_CABLE - Use a custom cable to access the SD (as defined in a pins file).

NO_SD_DETECT.name: NO_SD_DETECT
NO_SD_DETECT.comment: Enable if SD detect is rendered useless (e.g., by using an SD extender)

MULTI_VOLUME.name: MULTI_VOLUME
MULTI_VOLUME.comment:
Multiple volume support - EXPERIMENTAL.
Adds 'M21 Pm' / 'M21 S' / 'M21 U' to mount SD Card / USB Drive.

NO_SD_HOST_DRIVE.name: NO_SD_HOST_DRIVE
NO_SD_HOST_DRIVE.comment:
By default an onboard SD card reader may be shared as a USB mass-
storage device. This option hides the SD card from the host PC.
NO_SD_HOST_DRIVE.notes: Disable SD Card access over USB (for security).

XYZ_NO_FRAME.name: XYZ_NO_FRAME
XYZ_NO_FRAME.comment: Save many cycles by drawing a hollow frame or no frame on the Info Screen

USE_BIG_EDIT_FONT.name: USE_BIG_EDIT_FONT
USE_BIG_EDIT_FONT.comment:
A bigger font is available for edit items. Costs 3120 bytes of flash.
Western only. Not available for Cyrillic, Kana, Turkish, Greek, or Chinese.

USE_SMALL_INFOFONT.name: USE_SMALL_INFOFONT
USE_SMALL_INFOFONT.comment:
A smaller font may be used on the Info Screen. Costs 2434 bytes of flash.
Western only. Not available for Cyrillic, Kana, Turkish, Greek, or Chinese.

DOGM_SPI_DELAY_US.name: DOGM_SPI_DELAY_US
DOGM_SPI_DELAY_US.comment:
Enable this option and reduce the value to optimize screen updates.
The normal delay is 10µs. Use the lowest value that still gives a reliable display.

STATUS_COMBINE_HEATERS.name: STATUS_COMBINE_HEATERS
STATUS_COMBINE_HEATERS.comment:
Status (Info) Screen customization
These options may affect code size and screen render time.
Custom status screens can forcibly override these settings.
STATUS_COMBINE_HEATERS.notes: Use combined heater images instead of separate ones

STATUS_HOTEND_NUMBERLESS.name: STATUS_HOTEND_NUMBERLESS
STATUS_HOTEND_NUMBERLESS.comment: Use plain hotend icons instead of numbered ones (with 2+ hotends)

STATUS_HOTEND_INVERTED.name: STATUS_HOTEND_INVERTED
STATUS_HOTEND_INVERTED.comment: Show solid nozzle bitmaps when heating (Requires STATUS_HOTEND_ANIM for numbered hotends)

STATUS_HOTEND_ANIM.name: STATUS_HOTEND_ANIM
STATUS_HOTEND_ANIM.comment: Use a second bitmap to indicate hotend heating

STATUS_BED_ANIM.name: STATUS_BED_ANIM
STATUS_BED_ANIM.comment: Use a second bitmap to indicate bed heating

STATUS_CHAMBER_ANIM.name: STATUS_CHAMBER_ANIM
STATUS_CHAMBER_ANIM.comment: Use a second bitmap to indicate chamber heating

STATUS_CUTTER_ANIM.name: STATUS_CUTTER_ANIM
STATUS_CUTTER_ANIM.comment: Use a second bitmap to indicate spindle / laser active

STATUS_COOLER_ANIM.name: STATUS_COOLER_ANIM
STATUS_COOLER_ANIM.comment: Use a second bitmap to indicate laser cooling

STATUS_FLOWMETER_ANIM.name: STATUS_FLOWMETER_ANIM
STATUS_FLOWMETER_ANIM.comment: Use multiple bitmaps to indicate coolant flow

STATUS_HEAT_PERCENT.name: STATUS_HEAT_PERCENT
STATUS_HEAT_PERCENT.comment: Only one STATUS_HEAT_* option can be enabled
STATUS_HEAT_PERCENT.notes: Show heating in a progress bar

STATUS_HEAT_POWER.name: STATUS_HEAT_POWER
STATUS_HEAT_POWER.comment: Show heater output power as a vertical bar

MENU_HOLLOW_FRAME.name: MENU_HOLLOW_FRAME
MENU_HOLLOW_FRAME.notes: Enable to save many cycles by drawing a hollow frame on Menu Screens

OVERLAY_GFX_REVERSE.name: OVERLAY_GFX_REVERSE
OVERLAY_GFX_REVERSE.comment: Swap the CW/CCW indicators in the graphics overlay

GAMES_EASTER_EGG.name: GAMES_EASTER_EGG
GAMES_EASTER_EGG.comment: Add extra blank lines above the "Games" sub-menu

LCD_BAUDRATE.name: LCD_BAUDRATE
LCD_BAUDRATE.comment: Additional options for DGUS / DWIN displays

SERIAL_STATS_RX_BUFFER_OVERRUNS.name: SERIAL_STATS_RX_BUFFER_OVERRUNS
SERIAL_STATS_RX_BUFFER_OVERRUNS.comment: Fix Rx overrun situation (Currently only for AVR)

DGUS_PREHEAT_UI.name: DGUS_PREHEAT_UI
DGUS_PREHEAT_UI.comment: Display a preheat screen during heatup

DGUS_UI_MOVE_DIS_OPTION.name: DGUS_UI_MOVE_DIS_OPTION
DGUS_UI_MOVE_DIS_OPTION.comment: Disabled by default for FYSETC and MKS

DGUS_UI_WAITING.name: DGUS_UI_WAITING
DGUS_UI_WAITING.comment: Show a "waiting" screen between some screens

DGUS_UI_WAITING_STATUS_PERIOD.name: DGUS_UI_WAITING_STATUS_PERIOD
DGUS_UI_WAITING_STATUS_PERIOD.comment: Increase to slower waiting status looping

DGUS_SOFTWARE_AUTOSCROLL.name: DGUS_SOFTWARE_AUTOSCROLL
DGUS_SOFTWARE_AUTOSCROLL.comment:
The stock Ender-3 S1 Pro/Plus display firmware has rather poor SD file handling.

The autoscroll is mainly useful for status messages, filenames, and the "About" page.

NOTE: The Advanced SD Card option is affected by the stock touchscreen firmware, so
      pages 5 and up will display "4/4". This may get fixed in a screen firmware update.

DGUS_AUTOSCROLL_START_CYCLES.name: DGUS_AUTOSCROLL_START_CYCLES
DGUS_AUTOSCROLL_START_CYCLES.comment: Refresh cycles without scrolling at the beginning of text strings

DGUS_AUTOSCROLL_END_CYCLES.name: DGUS_AUTOSCROLL_END_CYCLES
DGUS_AUTOSCROLL_END_CYCLES.comment: ... at the end of text strings

DGUS_ADVANCED_SDCARD.name: DGUS_ADVANCED_SDCARD
DGUS_ADVANCED_SDCARD.comment: Allow more than 20 files and navigating directories

DGUS_USERCONFIRM.name: DGUS_USERCONFIRM
DGUS_USERCONFIRM.comment: Reuse the SD Card page to show various messages

CHIRON_TFT_STANDARD.name: CHIRON_TFT_STANDARD
CHIRON_TFT_STANDARD.comment:
By default the type of panel is automatically detected.
Enable one of these options if you know the panel type.

AC_DEFAULT_STARTUP_TUNE.name: AC_DEFAULT_STARTUP_TUNE
AC_DEFAULT_STARTUP_TUNE.comment: Enable the longer Anycubic powerup startup tune

AC_SD_FOLDER_VIEW.name: AC_SD_FOLDER_VIEW
AC_SD_FOLDER_VIEW.comment:
Display Folders
By default the file browser lists all G-code files (including those in subfolders) in a flat list.
Enable this option to display a hierarchical file browser.

NOTES:
- Without this option it helps to enable SDCARD_SORT_ALPHA so files are sorted before/after folders.
- When used with the "new" panel, folder names will also have '.gcode' appended to their names.
  This hack is currently required to force the panel to show folders.

LCD_LANGUAGE_2.name: LCD_LANGUAGE_2
LCD_LANGUAGE_2.comment: Specify additional languages for the UI. Default specified by LCD_LANGUAGE.

LCD_LANGUAGE_AUTO_SAVE.name: LCD_LANGUAGE_AUTO_SAVE
LCD_LANGUAGE_AUTO_SAVE.comment: Automatically save language to EEPROM on change

TOUCH_UI_320x240.name: TOUCH_UI_320x240
TOUCH_UI_320x240.comment: Correct the resolution if not using the stock TFT panel.

AO_EXP1_PINMAP.name: AO_EXP1_PINMAP
AO_EXP1_PINMAP.comment: Mappings for boards with a standard RepRapDiscount Display connector

CLCD_MOD_RESET.name: CLCD_MOD_RESET
CLCD_MOD_RESET.comment: Pins for CS and MOD_RESET (PD) must be chosen

CLCD_USE_SOFT_SPI.name: CLCD_USE_SOFT_SPI
CLCD_USE_SOFT_SPI.comment: If using software SPI, specify pins for SCLK, MOSI, MISO

TOUCH_UI_INVERTED.name: TOUCH_UI_INVERTED
TOUCH_UI_INVERTED.comment:
Display Orientation. An inverted (i.e. upside-down) display
is supported on the FT800. The FT810 and beyond also support
portrait and mirrored orientations.

TOUCH_UI_USE_UTF8.name: TOUCH_UI_USE_UTF8
TOUCH_UI_USE_UTF8.comment:
UTF8 processing and rendering.
Unsupported characters are shown as '?'.

TOUCH_UI_UTF8_WESTERN_CHARSET.name: TOUCH_UI_UTF8_WESTERN_CHARSET
TOUCH_UI_UTF8_WESTERN_CHARSET.comment:
Western accents support. These accented characters use
combined bitmaps and require relatively little storage.

TOUCH_UI_UTF8_SUPERSCRIPTS.name: TOUCH_UI_UTF8_SUPERSCRIPTS
TOUCH_UI_UTF8_SUPERSCRIPTS.comment:
Additional character groups. These characters require
full bitmaps and take up considerable storage:

TOUCH_UI_UTF8_SCANDINAVIAN.name: TOUCH_UI_UTF8_SCANDINAVIAN
TOUCH_UI_UTF8_SCANDINAVIAN.comment: Æ Ð Ø Þ æ ð ø þ

TOUCH_UI_UTF8_CYRILLIC_CHARSET.name: TOUCH_UI_UTF8_CYRILLIC_CHARSET
TOUCH_UI_UTF8_CYRILLIC_CHARSET.comment: Cyrillic character set, costs about 27KiB of flash

TOUCH_UI_FIT_TEXT.name: TOUCH_UI_FIT_TEXT
TOUCH_UI_FIT_TEXT.comment: Use a smaller font when labels don't fit buttons

TOUCH_UI_PASSCODE.name: TOUCH_UI_PASSCODE
TOUCH_UI_PASSCODE.comment:
Use a numeric passcode for "Screen lock" keypad.
(recommended for smaller displays)

TOUCH_UI_DEBUG.name: TOUCH_UI_DEBUG
TOUCH_UI_DEBUG.comment: Output extra debug info for Touch UI events

TOUCH_UI_DEVELOPER_MENU.name: TOUCH_UI_DEVELOPER_MENU
TOUCH_UI_DEVELOPER_MENU.comment: Developer menu (accessed by touching "About Printer" copyright text)

DISPLAY_SLEEP_MINUTES.name: DISPLAY_SLEEP_MINUTES
DISPLAY_SLEEP_MINUTES.comment:
Display Sleep
Enable this option to save energy and prevent OLED pixel burn-in.
DISPLAY_SLEEP_MINUTES.notes: (minutes) Timeout before turning off the screen

LCD_BACKLIGHT_TIMEOUT_MINS.name: LCD_BACKLIGHT_TIMEOUT_MINS
LCD_BACKLIGHT_TIMEOUT_MINS.comment:
LCD Backlight Timeout
Requires a display with a controllable backlight
LCD_BACKLIGHT_TIMEOUT_MINS.notes: (minutes) Timeout before turning off the backlight

EDITABLE_DISPLAY_TIMEOUT.name: EDITABLE_DISPLAY_TIMEOUT
EDITABLE_DISPLAY_TIMEOUT.comment: Edit sleep / backlight timeout with M255 S<minutes> and a menu item

ADC_BUTTON_DEBOUNCE_DELAY.name: ADC_BUTTON_DEBOUNCE_DELAY
ADC_BUTTON_DEBOUNCE_DELAY.notes: (count) Increase if buttons bounce or repeat too fast

FAST_BUTTON_POLLING.name: FAST_BUTTON_POLLING
FAST_BUTTON_POLLING.comment: Poll buttons at ~1kHz on 8-bit AVR. Set to 'false' for slow polling on 32-bit.

USE_WATCHDOG.name: USE_WATCHDOG
USE_WATCHDOG.comment:
The watchdog hardware timer will do a reset and disable all outputs
if the firmware gets too overloaded to read the temperature sensors.

If you find that watchdog reboot causes your AVR board to hang forever,
enable WATCHDOG_RESET_MANUAL to use a custom timer instead of WDTO.
NOTE: This method is less reliable as it can only catch hangups while
interrupts are enabled.

DISABLE_DRIVER_SAFE_POWER_PROTECT.name: DISABLE_DRIVER_SAFE_POWER_PROTECT
DISABLE_DRIVER_SAFE_POWER_PROTECT.comment:
Stepper Driver Anti-SNAFU Protection

If the SAFE_POWER_PIN is defined for your board, Marlin will check
that stepper drivers are properly plugged in before applying power.
Disable protection if your stepper drivers don't support the feature.

BABYSTEPPING.name: BABYSTEPPING
BABYSTEPPING.comment:
Babystepping enables movement of the axes by tiny increments without changing
the current position values. This feature is used primarily to adjust the Z
axis in the first layer of a print in real-time.

Warning: Does not respect endstops!

BABYSTEP_ALWAYS_AVAILABLE.name: BABYSTEP_ALWAYS_AVAILABLE
BABYSTEP_ALWAYS_AVAILABLE.comment: Allow babystepping at all times (not just during movement)

BABYSTEP_XY.name: BABYSTEP_XY
BABYSTEP_XY.comment: Also enable X/Y Babystepping. Not supported on DELTA!

BABYSTEP_INVERT_Z.name: BABYSTEP_INVERT_Z
BABYSTEP_INVERT_Z.comment: Enable if Z babysteps should go the other way

BABYSTEP_MILLIMETER_UNITS.name: BABYSTEP_MILLIMETER_UNITS
BABYSTEP_MILLIMETER_UNITS.comment: Specify BABYSTEP_MULTIPLICATOR_(XY|Z) in mm instead of micro-steps

BABYSTEP_MULTIPLICATOR_Z.name: BABYSTEP_MULTIPLICATOR_Z
BABYSTEP_MULTIPLICATOR_Z.comment: (steps or mm) Steps or millimeter distance for each Z babystep

BABYSTEP_MULTIPLICATOR_XY.name: BABYSTEP_MULTIPLICATOR_XY
BABYSTEP_MULTIPLICATOR_XY.comment: (steps or mm) Steps or millimeter distance for each XY babystep

DOUBLECLICK_FOR_Z_BABYSTEPPING.name: DOUBLECLICK_FOR_Z_BABYSTEPPING
DOUBLECLICK_FOR_Z_BABYSTEPPING.comment: Double-click on the Status Screen for Z Babystepping.

DOUBLECLICK_MAX_INTERVAL.name: DOUBLECLICK_MAX_INTERVAL
DOUBLECLICK_MAX_INTERVAL.comment: (ms) Maximum interval between clicks. Note: Extra time may be added to mitigate controller latency.

MOVE_Z_WHEN_IDLE.name: MOVE_Z_WHEN_IDLE
MOVE_Z_WHEN_IDLE.comment: Jump to the move Z menu on double-click when printer is idle.

MOVE_Z_IDLE_MULTIPLICATOR.name: MOVE_Z_IDLE_MULTIPLICATOR
MOVE_Z_IDLE_MULTIPLICATOR.comment: Multiply 1mm by this factor for the move step size.

BABYSTEP_DISPLAY_TOTAL.name: BABYSTEP_DISPLAY_TOTAL
BABYSTEP_DISPLAY_TOTAL.comment: Display total babysteps since last G28

BABYSTEP_HOTEND_Z_OFFSET.name: BABYSTEP_HOTEND_Z_OFFSET
BABYSTEP_HOTEND_Z_OFFSET.comment: For multiple hotends, babystep relative Z offsets

BABYSTEP_GFX_OVERLAY.name: BABYSTEP_GFX_OVERLAY
BABYSTEP_GFX_OVERLAY.comment: Enable graphical overlay on Z-offset editor

SAFE_BED_LEVELING_START_X.name: SAFE_BED_LEVELING_START_X
SAFE_BED_LEVELING_START_X.comment:
Use Safe Bed Leveling coordinates to move axes to a useful position before bed probing.
For example, after homing a rotational axis the Z probe might not be perpendicular to the bed.
Choose values the orient the bed horizontally and the Z-probe vertically.

PROBE_PT_1.name: PROBE_PT_1
PROBE_PT_1.comment:
Points to probe for all 3-point Leveling procedures.
Override if the automatically selected points are inadequate.

PROBING_MARGIN_LEFT.name: PROBING_MARGIN_LEFT
PROBING_MARGIN_LEFT.comment:
Probing Margins

Override PROBING_MARGIN for each side of the build plate
Useful to get probe points to exact positions on targets or
to allow leveling to avoid plate clamps on only specific
sides of the bed. With NOZZLE_AS_PROBE negative values are
allowed, to permit probing outside the bed.

If you are replacing the prior *_PROBE_BED_POSITION options,
LEFT and FRONT values in most cases will map directly over
RIGHT and REAR would be the inverse such as
(X/Y_BED_SIZE - RIGHT/BACK_PROBE_BED_POSITION)

This will allow all positions to match at compilation, however
should the probe position be modified with M851XY then the
probe points will follow. This prevents any change from causing
the probe to be unable to reach any points.

MESH_MIN_X.name: MESH_MIN_X
MESH_MIN_X.comment: Override the mesh area if the automatic (max) area is too large

OPTIMIZED_MESH_STORAGE.name: OPTIMIZED_MESH_STORAGE
OPTIMIZED_MESH_STORAGE.comment: Store mesh with less precision to save EEPROM space

G29_RETRY_AND_RECOVER.name: G29_RETRY_AND_RECOVER
G29_RETRY_AND_RECOVER.comment:
Repeatedly attempt G29 leveling until it succeeds.
Stop after G29_MAX_RETRIES attempts.

G29_SUCCESS_COMMANDS.name: G29_SUCCESS_COMMANDS
G29_SUCCESS_COMMANDS.comment:
Specify the GCODE commands that will be executed when leveling succeeds,
between attempts, and after the maximum number of retries have been tried.

G29_RECOVER_COMMANDS.name: G29_RECOVER_COMMANDS
G29_RECOVER_COMMANDS.value: "M117 Probe failed. Rewiping.\nG28\nG12 P0 S12 T0"

G29_FAILURE_COMMANDS.name: G29_FAILURE_COMMANDS
G29_FAILURE_COMMANDS.value:
"M117 Bed leveling failed.
G0 Z10
M300 P25 S880
M300 P50 S0
M300 P25 S880
M300 P50 S0
M300 P25 S880
M300 P50 S0
G4 S1"

PTC_PROBE.name: PTC_PROBE
PTC_PROBE.comment:
Thermal Probe Compensation

Adjust probe measurements to compensate for distortion associated with the temperature
of the probe, bed, and/or hotend.
Use G76 to automatically calibrate this feature for probe and bed temperatures.
(Extruder temperature/offset values must be calibrated manually.)
Use M871 to set temperature/offset values manually.
For more details see https://marlinfw.org/docs/features/probe_temp_compensation.html

PTC_LINEAR_EXTRAPOLATION.name: PTC_LINEAR_EXTRAPOLATION
PTC_LINEAR_EXTRAPOLATION.comment:
If the probe is outside the defined range, use linear extrapolation with the closest
point and the point with index PTC_LINEAR_EXTRAPOLATION. e.g., If set to 4 it will use the
linear extrapolation between data[0] and data[4] for values below PTC_PROBE_START.

PTC_PROBE_START.name: PTC_PROBE_START
PTC_PROBE_START.comment:
Probe temperature calibration generates a table of values starting at PTC_PROBE_START
(e.g., 30), in steps of PTC_PROBE_RES (e.g., 5) with PTC_PROBE_COUNT (e.g., 10) samples.

PTC_BED_START.name: PTC_BED_START
PTC_BED_START.comment: Bed temperature calibration builds a similar table.

PTC_HOTEND_START.name: PTC_HOTEND_START
PTC_HOTEND_START.comment: Note: There is no automatic calibration for the hotend. Use M871.

PTC_PARK_POS.name: PTC_PARK_POS
PTC_PARK_POS.comment: Park position to wait for probe cooldown

PTC_PROBE_POS.name: PTC_PROBE_POS
PTC_PROBE_POS.comment: Probe position to probe and wait for probe to reach target temperature

PTC_PROBE_TEMP.name: PTC_PROBE_TEMP
PTC_PROBE_TEMP.comment:
The temperature the probe should be at while taking measurements during
bed temperature calibration.

PTC_PROBE_HEATING_OFFSET.name: PTC_PROBE_HEATING_OFFSET
PTC_PROBE_HEATING_OFFSET.comment:
Height above Z=0.0 to raise the nozzle. Lowering this can help the probe to heat faster.
Note: The Z=0.0 offset is determined by the probe Z offset (e.g., as set with M851 Z).

SAVED_POSITIONS.name: SAVED_POSITIONS
SAVED_POSITIONS.notes: Each saved position slot costs 12 bytes

BLOCK_BUFFER_SIZE.name: BLOCK_BUFFER_SIZE
BLOCK_BUFFER_SIZE.comment: The number of linear moves that can be in the planner at once.

FASTER_GCODE_PARSER.name: FASTER_GCODE_PARSER
FASTER_GCODE_PARSER.comment: Spend 28 bytes of SRAM to optimize the G-code parser

GCODE_CASE_INSENSITIVE.name: GCODE_CASE_INSENSITIVE
GCODE_CASE_INSENSITIVE.comment: Accept G-code sent to the firmware in lowercase

REPETIER_GCODE_M360.name: REPETIER_GCODE_M360
REPETIER_GCODE_M360.comment: Add commands originally from Repetier FW

DEBUG_FLAGS_GCODE.name: DEBUG_FLAGS_GCODE
DEBUG_FLAGS_GCODE.comment:
Enable M111 debug flags 1=ECHO, 2=INFO, 4=ERRORS (unimplemented).
Disable to save some flash. Some hosts (Repetier Host) may rely on this feature.

NO_WORKSPACE_OFFSETS.name: NO_WORKSPACE_OFFSETS
NO_WORKSPACE_OFFSETS.comment:
Enable this option for a leaner build of Marlin that removes
workspace offsets to slightly optimize performance.
G92 will revert to its behavior from Marlin 1.0.

NO_HOME_OFFSETS.name: NO_HOME_OFFSETS
NO_HOME_OFFSETS.comment: Disable M206 and M428 if you don't need home offsets.

PAREN_COMMENTS.name: PAREN_COMMENTS
PAREN_COMMENTS.comment:
CNC G-code options
Support CNC-style G-code dialects used by laser cutters, drawing machine cams, etc.
Note that G0 feedrates should be used with care for 3D printing (if used at all).
High feedrates may cause ringing and harm print quality.

GCODE_MOTION_MODES.name: GCODE_MOTION_MODES
GCODE_MOTION_MODES.comment: Remember the motion mode (G0 G1 G2 G3 G5 G38.X) and apply for X Y Z E F, etc.

G0_FEEDRATE.name: G0_FEEDRATE
G0_FEEDRATE.comment: Enable and set a (default) feedrate for all G0 moves

VARIABLE_G0_FEEDRATE.name: VARIABLE_G0_FEEDRATE
VARIABLE_G0_FEEDRATE.comment: The G0 feedrate is set by F in G0 motion mode

STARTUP_COMMANDS.name: STARTUP_COMMANDS
STARTUP_COMMANDS.comment:
Startup commands

Execute certain G-code commands immediately after power-on.

GCODE_MACROS.name: GCODE_MACROS
GCODE_MACROS.comment:
G-code Macros

Add G-codes M810-M819 to define and run G-code macros
and M820 to report the current set of macros.
Macros are not saved to EEPROM unless enabled below.

GCODE_MACROS_SLOT_SIZE.name: GCODE_MACROS_SLOT_SIZE
GCODE_MACROS_SLOT_SIZE.comment: Maximum length of a single macro

MAX_CMD_SIZE.name: MAX_CMD_SIZE
MAX_CMD_SIZE.comment: The ASCII buffer for serial input

TX_BUFFER_SIZE.name: TX_BUFFER_SIZE
TX_BUFFER_SIZE.comment:
Host Transmit Buffer Size
 - Costs 386 bytes of flash and TX_BUFFER_SIZE+3 bytes of SRAM (if not 0).
 - 4 bytes required to buffer a simple "ok".
 - 32 bytes for ADVANCED_OK (M105).
 - 128 bytes for the optimal speed of 'debug-echo:'
 - Other output doesn't need to be that speedy.

RX_BUFFER_SIZE.name: RX_BUFFER_SIZE
RX_BUFFER_SIZE.comment:
Host Receive Buffer Size
Without XON/XOFF flow control (see SERIAL_XON_XOFF below) 32 bytes should be enough.
To use flow control, set this buffer size to at least 1024 bytes.

SERIAL_XON_XOFF.name: SERIAL_XON_XOFF
SERIAL_XON_XOFF.comment:
Enable to have the controller send XON/XOFF control characters to
the host to signal the RX buffer is becoming full.

SERIAL_STATS_MAX_RX_QUEUED.name: SERIAL_STATS_MAX_RX_QUEUED
SERIAL_STATS_MAX_RX_QUEUED.comment:
Enable this option to collect and display the maximum
RX queue usage after transferring a file to SD.

SERIAL_STATS_DROPPED_RX.name: SERIAL_STATS_DROPPED_RX
SERIAL_STATS_DROPPED_RX.comment:
Enable this option to collect and display the number
of dropped bytes after a file transfer to SD.

SERIAL_STATS_RX_FRAMING_ERRORS.name: SERIAL_STATS_RX_FRAMING_ERRORS
SERIAL_STATS_RX_FRAMING_ERRORS.comment:
Enable this option to collect and display framing errors.
Framing errors occur when invalid start/stop bits or other
serial protocol violations are detected.

RX_BUFFER_MONITOR.name: RX_BUFFER_MONITOR
RX_BUFFER_MONITOR.comment:
Monitor RX buffer usage
Dump an error to the serial port if the serial receive buffer overflows.
If you see these errors, increase the RX_BUFFER_SIZE value.
Not supported on all platforms.

EMERGENCY_PARSER.name: EMERGENCY_PARSER
EMERGENCY_PARSER.comment:
Emergency Command Parser

Add a low-level parser to intercept certain commands as they
enter the serial receive buffer, so they cannot be blocked.
Currently handles M108, M112, M410, M876
NOTE: Not yet implemented for all platforms.

REALTIME_REPORTING_COMMANDS.name: REALTIME_REPORTING_COMMANDS
REALTIME_REPORTING_COMMANDS.comment:
Realtime Reporting (requires EMERGENCY_PARSER)

- Report position and state of the machine (like Grbl).
- Auto-report position during long moves.
- Useful for CNC/LASER.

Adds support for commands:
 S000 : Report State and Position while moving.
 P000 : Instant Pause / Hold while moving. Enable SOFT_FEED_HOLD for soft deceleration.
 R000 : Resume from Pause / Hold. Enable SOFT_FEED_HOLD for soft acceleration.

- During Hold all Emergency Parser commands are available, as usual.
- Enable NANODLP_Z_SYNC and NANODLP_ALL_AXIS for move command end-state reports.

FULL_REPORT_TO_HOST_FEATURE.name: FULL_REPORT_TO_HOST_FEATURE
FULL_REPORT_TO_HOST_FEATURE.comment: Auto-report the machine status like Grbl CNC

NO_TIMEOUTS.name: NO_TIMEOUTS
NO_TIMEOUTS.comment:
Bad Serial-connections can miss a received command by sending an 'ok'
Therefore some clients abort after 30 seconds in a timeout.
Some other clients start sending commands while receiving a 'wait'.
This "wait" is only sent when the buffer is empty. 1 second is a good value here.

ADVANCED_OK.name: ADVANCED_OK
ADVANCED_OK.comment: Some clients will have this feature soon. This could make the NO_TIMEOUTS unnecessary.

SERIAL_OVERRUN_PROTECTION.name: SERIAL_OVERRUN_PROTECTION
SERIAL_OVERRUN_PROTECTION.comment:
Printrun may have trouble receiving long strings all at once.
This option inserts short delays between lines of serial output.

SERIAL_FLOAT_PRECISION.name: SERIAL_FLOAT_PRECISION
SERIAL_FLOAT_PRECISION.comment: For serial echo, the number of digits after the decimal point

SERIAL_DMA.name: SERIAL_DMA
SERIAL_DMA.comment:
This feature is EXPERIMENTAL so use with caution and test thoroughly.
Enable this option to receive data on the serial ports via the onboard DMA
controller for more stable and reliable high-speed serial communication.
Support is currently limited to some STM32 MCUs and all HC32 MCUs.
Note: This has no effect on emulated USB serial ports.

PROPORTIONAL_FONT_RATIO.name: PROPORTIONAL_FONT_RATIO
PROPORTIONAL_FONT_RATIO.comment:
Set the number of proportional font spaces required to fill up a typical character space.
This can help to better align the output of commands like 'G29 O' Mesh Output.

For clients that use a fixed-width font (like OctoPrint), leave this set to 1.0.
Otherwise, adjust according to your client and font.

FWRETRACT.name: FWRETRACT
FWRETRACT.comment:
Firmware-based and LCD-controlled retract

Add G10 / G11 commands for automatic firmware-based retract / recover.
Use M207 and M208 to define parameters for retract / recover.

Use M209 to enable or disable auto-retract.
With auto-retract enabled, all G1 E moves within the set range
will be converted to firmware-based retract/recover moves.

Be sure to turn off auto-retract during filament change.

Note that M207 / M208 / M209 settings are saved to EEPROM.

MIN_AUTORETRACT.name: MIN_AUTORETRACT
MIN_AUTORETRACT.comment: (mm) Don't convert E moves under this length

MAX_AUTORETRACT.name: MAX_AUTORETRACT
MAX_AUTORETRACT.comment: (mm) Don't convert E moves over this length

RETRACT_RECOVER_LENGTH.name: RETRACT_RECOVER_LENGTH
RETRACT_RECOVER_LENGTH.comment: (mm) Default additional recover length (added to retract length on recover)

RETRACT_RECOVER_LENGTH_SWAP.name: RETRACT_RECOVER_LENGTH_SWAP
RETRACT_RECOVER_LENGTH_SWAP.comment: (mm) Default additional swap recover length (added to retract length on recover from toolchange)

RETRACT_RECOVER_FEEDRATE.name: RETRACT_RECOVER_FEEDRATE
RETRACT_RECOVER_FEEDRATE.comment: (mm/s) Default feedrate for recovering from retraction

RETRACT_RECOVER_FEEDRATE_SWAP.name: RETRACT_RECOVER_FEEDRATE_SWAP
RETRACT_RECOVER_FEEDRATE_SWAP.comment: (mm/s) Default feedrate for recovering from swap retraction

RETRACT_SYNC_MIXING.name: RETRACT_SYNC_MIXING
RETRACT_SYNC_MIXING.comment: Retract and restore all mixing steppers simultaneously

TOOLCHANGE_ZRAISE.name: TOOLCHANGE_ZRAISE
TOOLCHANGE_ZRAISE.comment: Z raise distance for tool-change, as needed for some extruders

TOOLCHANGE_ZRAISE_BEFORE_RETRACT.name: TOOLCHANGE_ZRAISE_BEFORE_RETRACT
TOOLCHANGE_ZRAISE_BEFORE_RETRACT.comment: Apply raise before swap retraction (if enabled)

TOOLCHANGE_NO_RETURN.name: TOOLCHANGE_NO_RETURN
TOOLCHANGE_NO_RETURN.comment: Never return to previous position on tool-change

EVENT_GCODE_AFTER_TOOLCHANGE.name: EVENT_GCODE_AFTER_TOOLCHANGE
EVENT_GCODE_AFTER_TOOLCHANGE.comment: Extra G-code to run after tool-change

EVENT_GCODE_TOOLCHANGE_T0.name: EVENT_GCODE_TOOLCHANGE_T0
EVENT_GCODE_TOOLCHANGE_T0.comment:
Extra G-code to run while executing tool-change commands. Can be used to use an additional
stepper motor (e.g., I axis in Configuration.h) to drive the tool-changer.
EVENT_GCODE_TOOLCHANGE_T0.notes: Extra G-code to run while executing tool-change command T0

EVENT_GCODE_TOOLCHANGE_T1.name: EVENT_GCODE_TOOLCHANGE_T1
EVENT_GCODE_TOOLCHANGE_T1.comment: Extra G-code to run while executing tool-change command T1

EVENT_GCODE_TOOLCHANGE_ALWAYS_RUN.name: EVENT_GCODE_TOOLCHANGE_ALWAYS_RUN
EVENT_GCODE_TOOLCHANGE_ALWAYS_RUN.comment: Always execute above G-code sequences. Use with caution!

TC_GCODE_USE_GLOBAL_X.name: TC_GCODE_USE_GLOBAL_X
TC_GCODE_USE_GLOBAL_X.comment:
Consider coordinates for EVENT_GCODE_TOOLCHANGE_Tx as relative to T0
so that moves in the specified axes are the same for all tools.
TC_GCODE_USE_GLOBAL_X.notes: Use X position relative to Tool 0

TC_GCODE_USE_GLOBAL_Y.name: TC_GCODE_USE_GLOBAL_Y
TC_GCODE_USE_GLOBAL_Y.comment: Use Y position relative to Tool 0

TC_GCODE_USE_GLOBAL_Z.name: TC_GCODE_USE_GLOBAL_Z
TC_GCODE_USE_GLOBAL_Z.comment: Use Z position relative to Tool 0

TOOL_SENSOR.name: TOOL_SENSOR
TOOL_SENSOR.comment:
Tool Sensors detect when tools have been picked up or dropped.
Requires the pins TOOL_SENSOR1_PIN, TOOL_SENSOR2_PIN, etc.

TOOLCHANGE_FILAMENT_SWAP.name: TOOLCHANGE_FILAMENT_SWAP
TOOLCHANGE_FILAMENT_SWAP.comment:
Retract and prime filament on tool-change to reduce
ooze and stringing and to get cleaner transitions.

TOOLCHANGE_FS_EXTRA_RESUME_LENGTH.name: TOOLCHANGE_FS_EXTRA_RESUME_LENGTH
TOOLCHANGE_FS_EXTRA_RESUME_LENGTH.comment: (mm) Extra length for better restart. Adjust with LCD or M217 B.

TOOLCHANGE_FS_UNRETRACT_SPEED.name: TOOLCHANGE_FS_UNRETRACT_SPEED
TOOLCHANGE_FS_UNRETRACT_SPEED.comment: (mm/min) (On SINGLENOZZLE or Bowden loading must be slowed down)

TOOLCHANGE_FS_EXTRA_PRIME.name: TOOLCHANGE_FS_EXTRA_PRIME
TOOLCHANGE_FS_EXTRA_PRIME.comment: Longer prime to clean out a SINGLENOZZLE

TOOLCHANGE_FS_WIPE_RETRACT.name: TOOLCHANGE_FS_WIPE_RETRACT
TOOLCHANGE_FS_WIPE_RETRACT.comment: (mm) Cutting retraction out of park, for less stringing, better wipe, etc. Adjust with LCD or M217 G.

TOOLCHANGE_FS_FAN.name: TOOLCHANGE_FS_FAN
TOOLCHANGE_FS_FAN.comment: Cool after prime to reduce stringing

TOOLCHANGE_FS_SLOW_FIRST_PRIME.name: TOOLCHANGE_FS_SLOW_FIRST_PRIME
TOOLCHANGE_FS_SLOW_FIRST_PRIME.comment: Use TOOLCHANGE_FS_PRIME_SPEED feedrate the first time each extruder is primed

TOOLCHANGE_FS_PRIME_FIRST_USED.name: TOOLCHANGE_FS_PRIME_FIRST_USED
TOOLCHANGE_FS_PRIME_FIRST_USED.comment:
Prime T0 the first time T0 is sent to the printer:
 [ Power-On -> T0 { Activate & Prime T0 } -> T1 { Retract T0, Activate & Prime T1 } ]
If disabled, no priming on T0 until switching back to T0 from another extruder:
 [ Power-On -> T0 { T0 Activated } -> T1 { Activate & Prime T1 } -> T0 { Retract T1, Activate & Prime T0 } ]
Enable with M217 V1 before printing to avoid unwanted priming on host connect.

TOOLCHANGE_MIGRATION_FEATURE.name: TOOLCHANGE_MIGRATION_FEATURE
TOOLCHANGE_MIGRATION_FEATURE.comment:
Tool Change Migration
This feature provides G-code and LCD options to switch tools mid-print.
All applicable tool properties are migrated so the print can continue.
Tools must be closely matching and other restrictions may apply.
Useful to:
  - Change filament color without interruption
  - Switch spools automatically on filament runout
  - Switch to a different nozzle on an extruder jam

MIGRATION_ZRAISE.name: MIGRATION_ZRAISE
MIGRATION_ZRAISE.comment:
Override toolchange settings
By default tool migration uses regular toolchange settings.
With a prime tower, tool-change swapping/priming occur inside the bed.
When migrating to a new unprimed tool you can set override values below.

MIGRATION_FS_WIPE_RETRACT.name: MIGRATION_FS_WIPE_RETRACT
MIGRATION_FS_WIPE_RETRACT.comment: (mm) Retract before cooling for less stringing, better wipe, etc.

MIGRATION_FS_FAN_SPEED.name: MIGRATION_FS_FAN_SPEED
MIGRATION_FS_FAN_SPEED.comment: Cool after prime to reduce stringing

TOOLCHANGE_PARK.name: TOOLCHANGE_PARK
TOOLCHANGE_PARK.comment:
Position to park head during tool change.
Doesn't apply to SWITCHING_TOOLHEAD, DUAL_X_CARRIAGE, or PARKING_EXTRUDER

ADVANCED_PAUSE_FEATURE.name: ADVANCED_PAUSE_FEATURE
ADVANCED_PAUSE_FEATURE.comment:
Advanced Pause for Filament Change
 - Adds the G-code M600 Filament Change to initiate a filament change.
 - This feature is required for the default FILAMENT_RUNOUT_SCRIPT.

Requirements:
 - For Filament Change parking enable and configure NOZZLE_PARK_FEATURE.
 - For user interaction enable an LCD display, HOST_PROMPT_SUPPORT, or EMERGENCY_PARSER.

Enable PARK_HEAD_ON_PAUSE to add the G-code M125 Pause and Park.

PAUSE_PARK_RETRACT_LENGTH.name: PAUSE_PARK_RETRACT_LENGTH
PAUSE_PARK_RETRACT_LENGTH.comment: (mm) Initial retract. This short retract is done immediately, before parking the nozzle.

FILAMENT_CHANGE_UNLOAD_FEEDRATE.name: FILAMENT_CHANGE_UNLOAD_FEEDRATE
FILAMENT_CHANGE_UNLOAD_FEEDRATE.comment: (mm/s) Unload filament feedrate. This can be pretty fast.

FILAMENT_CHANGE_UNLOAD_ACCEL.name: FILAMENT_CHANGE_UNLOAD_ACCEL
FILAMENT_CHANGE_UNLOAD_ACCEL.comment: (mm/s^2) Lower acceleration may allow a faster feedrate.

FILAMENT_CHANGE_UNLOAD_LENGTH.name: FILAMENT_CHANGE_UNLOAD_LENGTH
FILAMENT_CHANGE_UNLOAD_LENGTH.comment: (mm) The length of filament for a complete unload. For Bowden, the full length of the tube and nozzle. For direct drive, the full length of the nozzle. Set to 0 for manual unloading.

FILAMENT_CHANGE_SLOW_LOAD_LENGTH.name: FILAMENT_CHANGE_SLOW_LOAD_LENGTH
FILAMENT_CHANGE_SLOW_LOAD_LENGTH.comment: (mm) Slow length, to allow time to insert material. 0 to disable start loading and skip to fast load only

FILAMENT_CHANGE_FAST_LOAD_FEEDRATE.name: FILAMENT_CHANGE_FAST_LOAD_FEEDRATE
FILAMENT_CHANGE_FAST_LOAD_FEEDRATE.comment: (mm/s) Load filament feedrate. This can be pretty fast.

FILAMENT_CHANGE_FAST_LOAD_ACCEL.name: FILAMENT_CHANGE_FAST_LOAD_ACCEL
FILAMENT_CHANGE_FAST_LOAD_ACCEL.comment: (mm/s^2) Lower acceleration may allow a faster feedrate.

FILAMENT_CHANGE_FAST_LOAD_LENGTH.name: FILAMENT_CHANGE_FAST_LOAD_LENGTH
FILAMENT_CHANGE_FAST_LOAD_LENGTH.comment: (mm) Load length of filament, from extruder gear to nozzle. For Bowden, the full length of the tube and nozzle. For direct drive, the full length of the nozzle.

ADVANCED_PAUSE_CONTINUOUS_PURGE.name: ADVANCED_PAUSE_CONTINUOUS_PURGE
ADVANCED_PAUSE_CONTINUOUS_PURGE.comment: Purge continuously up to the purge length until interrupted.

ADVANCED_PAUSE_PURGE_FEEDRATE.name: ADVANCED_PAUSE_PURGE_FEEDRATE
ADVANCED_PAUSE_PURGE_FEEDRATE.comment: (mm/s) Extrude feedrate (after loading). Should be slower than load feedrate.

ADVANCED_PAUSE_PURGE_LENGTH.name: ADVANCED_PAUSE_PURGE_LENGTH
ADVANCED_PAUSE_PURGE_LENGTH.comment: (mm) Length to extrude after loading. Set to 0 for manual extrusion. Filament can be extruded repeatedly from the Filament Change menu until extrusion is consistent, and to purge old filament.

ADVANCED_PAUSE_RESUME_PRIME.name: ADVANCED_PAUSE_RESUME_PRIME
ADVANCED_PAUSE_RESUME_PRIME.comment: (mm) Extra distance to prime nozzle after returning from park.

ADVANCED_PAUSE_FANS_PAUSE.name: ADVANCED_PAUSE_FANS_PAUSE
ADVANCED_PAUSE_FANS_PAUSE.comment: Turn off print-cooling fans while the machine is paused.

FILAMENT_UNLOAD_PURGE_RETRACT.name: FILAMENT_UNLOAD_PURGE_RETRACT
FILAMENT_UNLOAD_PURGE_RETRACT.comment: Filament Unload does a Retract, Delay, and Purge first:

FILAMENT_UNLOAD_PURGE_DELAY.name: FILAMENT_UNLOAD_PURGE_DELAY
FILAMENT_UNLOAD_PURGE_DELAY.comment: (ms) Delay for the filament to cool after retract.

FILAMENT_UNLOAD_PURGE_LENGTH.name: FILAMENT_UNLOAD_PURGE_LENGTH
FILAMENT_UNLOAD_PURGE_LENGTH.comment: (mm) An unretract is done, then this length is purged.

PAUSE_PARK_NOZZLE_TIMEOUT.name: PAUSE_PARK_NOZZLE_TIMEOUT
PAUSE_PARK_NOZZLE_TIMEOUT.comment: (seconds) Time limit before the nozzle is turned off for safety.

FILAMENT_CHANGE_ALERT_BEEPS.name: FILAMENT_CHANGE_ALERT_BEEPS
FILAMENT_CHANGE_ALERT_BEEPS.comment: Number of alert beeps to play when a response is needed.

PAUSE_PARK_NO_STEPPER_TIMEOUT.name: PAUSE_PARK_NO_STEPPER_TIMEOUT
PAUSE_PARK_NO_STEPPER_TIMEOUT.comment: Enable for XYZ steppers to stay powered on during filament change.

FILAMENT_CHANGE_RESUME_ON_INSERT.name: FILAMENT_CHANGE_RESUME_ON_INSERT
FILAMENT_CHANGE_RESUME_ON_INSERT.comment: Automatically continue / load filament when runout sensor is triggered again.

PAUSE_REHEAT_FAST_RESUME.name: PAUSE_REHEAT_FAST_RESUME
PAUSE_REHEAT_FAST_RESUME.comment: Reduce number of waits by not prompting again post-timeout before continuing.

PARK_HEAD_ON_PAUSE.name: PARK_HEAD_ON_PAUSE
PARK_HEAD_ON_PAUSE.comment: Park the nozzle during pause and filament change.

HOME_BEFORE_FILAMENT_CHANGE.name: HOME_BEFORE_FILAMENT_CHANGE
HOME_BEFORE_FILAMENT_CHANGE.comment: If needed, home before parking for filament change

FILAMENT_LOAD_UNLOAD_GCODES.name: FILAMENT_LOAD_UNLOAD_GCODES
FILAMENT_LOAD_UNLOAD_GCODES.comment: Add M701/M702 Load/Unload G-codes, plus Load/Unload in the LCD Prepare menu.

FILAMENT_UNLOAD_ALL_EXTRUDERS.name: FILAMENT_UNLOAD_ALL_EXTRUDERS
FILAMENT_UNLOAD_ALL_EXTRUDERS.comment: Allow M702 to unload all extruders above a minimum target temp (as set by M302)

CONFIGURE_FILAMENT_CHANGE.name: CONFIGURE_FILAMENT_CHANGE
CONFIGURE_FILAMENT_CHANGE.comment: Add M603 G-code and menu items. Requires ~1.3K bytes of flash.

HOLD_MULTIPLIER.name: HOLD_MULTIPLIER
HOLD_MULTIPLIER.comment:
Trinamic Smart Drivers

To use TMC2130, TMC2160, TMC2240, TMC2660, TMC5130, TMC5160 stepper drivers in SPI mode:
 - Connect your SPI pins to the Hardware SPI interface on the board.
   Some boards have simple jumper connections! See your board's documentation.
 - Define the required Stepper CS pins in your `pins_MYBOARD.h` file.
   (See the RAMPS pins, for example.)
 - You can also use Software SPI with GPIO pins instead of Hardware SPI.

To use TMC220x stepper drivers with Serial UART:
 - Connect PDN_UART to the #_SERIAL_TX_PIN through a 1K resistor.
   For reading capabilities also connect PDN_UART to #_SERIAL_RX_PIN with no resistor.
   Some boards have simple jumper connections! See your board's documentation.
 - These drivers can also be used with Hardware Serial.

The TMCStepper library is required for other TMC stepper drivers.
  https://github.com/teemuatlut/TMCStepper
HOLD_MULTIPLIER.notes: Scales down the holding current from run current

EDITABLE_HOMING_CURRENT.name: EDITABLE_HOMING_CURRENT
EDITABLE_HOMING_CURRENT.comment: Add a G-code and menu to modify the Homing Current

INTERPOLATE.name: INTERPOLATE
INTERPOLATE.comment:
Interpolate microsteps to 256
Override for each driver with <driver>_INTERPOLATE settings below

TMC2240_RREF.name: TMC2240_RREF
TMC2240_RREF.comment: (Ω) 12000 .. 60000. (FLY TMC2240 = 12300) Max Current. Lower for more internal resolution. Raise to run cooler.

TMC2240_CURRENT_RANGE.name: TMC2240_CURRENT_RANGE
TMC2240_CURRENT_RANGE.comment: Slope Control: Lower is more silent. Higher runs cooler.

X_CURRENT.name: X_CURRENT
X_CURRENT.comment: (mA) RMS current. Multiply by 1.414 for peak current.

X_CURRENT_HOME.name: X_CURRENT_HOME
X_CURRENT_HOME.comment: (mA) RMS current for homing. (Typically lower than *_CURRENT.)

X_CHAIN_POS.name: X_CHAIN_POS
X_CHAIN_POS.comment: -1..0: Not chained. 1: MCU MOSI connected. 2: Next in chain, ...

X_INTERPOLATE.name: X_INTERPOLATE
X_INTERPOLATE.comment: Enable to override 'INTERPOLATE' for the X axis

X_HOLD_MULTIPLIER.name: X_HOLD_MULTIPLIER
X_HOLD_MULTIPLIER.comment: Enable to override 'HOLD_MULTIPLIER' for the X axis

PROBING_USE_CURRENT_HOME.name: PROBING_USE_CURRENT_HOME
PROBING_USE_CURRENT_HOME.comment:
Use the homing current for all probing. (e.g., Current may be reduced to the
point where a collision makes the motor skip instead of damaging the bed,
though this is unlikely to save delicate probes from being damaged.

TMC_HOME_PHASE.name: TMC_HOME_PHASE
TMC_HOME_PHASE.comment:
TMC Homing stepper phase.

Improve homing repeatability by homing to stepper coil's nearest absolute
phase position. Trinamic drivers use a stepper phase table with 1024 values
spanning 4 full steps with 256 positions each (ergo, 1024 positions).
Full step positions (128, 384, 640, 896) have the highest holding torque.

Values from 0..1023, -1 to disable homing phase for that axis.

EDGE_STEPPING.name: EDGE_STEPPING
EDGE_STEPPING.comment: Step on both rising and falling edge signals (as with a square wave).

TMC_DEBUG.name: TMC_DEBUG
TMC_DEBUG.comment:
Enable M122 debugging command for TMC stepper drivers.
M122 S0/1 will enable continuous reporting.

TMC_ADV.name: TMC_ADV
TMC_ADV.comment:
You can set your own advanced settings by filling in predefined functions.
A list of available functions can be found on the library github page
https://github.com/teemuatlut/TMCStepper

Example:
#define TMC_ADV() { *   stepperX.diag0_otpw(1); *   stepperY.intpol(0); * }

X_CS_PIN.name: X_CS_PIN
X_CS_PIN.comment:
Override default SPI pins for TMC2130, TMC2160, TMC2240, TMC2660, TMC5130 and TMC5160 drivers here.
The default pins can be found in your board's pins file.

TMC_USE_SW_SPI.name: TMC_USE_SW_SPI
TMC_USE_SW_SPI.comment:
Software option for SPI driven drivers (TMC2130, TMC2160, TMC2240, TMC2660, TMC5130 and TMC5160).
The default SW SPI pins are defined the respective pins files,
but you can override or define them here.

X_SLAVE_ADDRESS.name: X_SLAVE_ADDRESS
X_SLAVE_ADDRESS.comment:
Four TMC2209 drivers can use the same HW/SW serial port with hardware configured addresses.
Set the address using jumpers on pins MS1 and MS2.
Address | MS1  | MS2
      0 | LOW  | LOW
      1 | HIGH | LOW
      2 | LOW  | HIGH
      3 | HIGH | HIGH

Set *_SERIAL_TX_PIN and *_SERIAL_RX_PIN to match for all drivers
on the same serial port, either here or in your board's pins file.

SOFTWARE_DRIVER_ENABLE.name: SOFTWARE_DRIVER_ENABLE
SOFTWARE_DRIVER_ENABLE.comment:
Software enable

Use for drivers that do not use a dedicated enable pin, but rather handle the same
function through a communication line such as SPI or UART.

STEALTHCHOP_XY.name: STEALTHCHOP_XY
STEALTHCHOP_XY.comment:
TMC2130, TMC2160, TMC2208, TMC2209, TMC2240, TMC5130 and TMC5160 only
Use Trinamic's ultra quiet stepping mode.
When disabled, Marlin will use spreadCycle stepping mode.

CHOPPER_TIMING.name: CHOPPER_TIMING
CHOPPER_TIMING.comment:
Optimize spreadCycle chopper parameters by using predefined parameter sets
or with the help of an example included in the library.
Provided parameter sets are
CHOPPER_DEFAULT_12V
CHOPPER_DEFAULT_19V
CHOPPER_DEFAULT_24V
CHOPPER_DEFAULT_36V
CHOPPER_09STEP_24V   // 0.9 degree steppers (24V)
CHOPPER_PRUSAMK3_24V // Imported parameters from the official Průša firmware for MK3 (24V)
CHOPPER_MARLIN_119   // Old defaults from Marlin v1.1.9

Define your own with:
{ <off_time[1..15]>, <hysteresis_end[-3..12]>, hysteresis_start[1..8] }

MONITOR_DRIVER_STATUS.name: MONITOR_DRIVER_STATUS
MONITOR_DRIVER_STATUS.comment:
Monitor Trinamic drivers
for error conditions like overtemperature and short to ground.
To manage over-temp Marlin can decrease the driver current until the error condition clears.
Other detected conditions can be used to stop the current print.
Relevant G-codes:
M906 - Set or get motor current in milliamps using axis codes X, Y, Z, E. Report values if no axis codes given.
M911 - Report stepper driver overtemperature pre-warn condition.
M912 - Clear stepper driver overtemperature pre-warn condition flag.
M122 - Report driver parameters (Requires TMC_DEBUG)

HYBRID_THRESHOLD.name: HYBRID_THRESHOLD
HYBRID_THRESHOLD.comment:
TMC2130, TMC2160, TMC2208, TMC2209, TMC2240, TMC5130 and TMC5160 only
The driver will switch to spreadCycle when stepper speed is over HYBRID_THRESHOLD.
This mode allows for faster movements at the expense of higher noise levels.
STEALTHCHOP_(XY|Z|E) must be enabled to use HYBRID_THRESHOLD.
M913 X/Y/Z/E to live tune the setting

SENSORLESS_HOMING.name: SENSORLESS_HOMING
SENSORLESS_HOMING.comment:
Use StallGuard to home / probe X, Y, Z.

TMC2130, TMC2160, TMC2209, TMC2240, TMC2660, TMC5130, and TMC5160 only
Connect the stepper driver's DIAG1 pin to the X/Y endstop pin.
X, Y, and Z homing will always be done in spreadCycle mode.

X/Y/Z_STALL_SENSITIVITY is the default stall threshold.
Use M914 X Y Z to set the stall threshold at runtime:

 Sensitivity  TMC2209        Others
   HIGHEST        255         -64    (Too sensitive => False positive)
   LOWEST          0           63    (Too insensitive => No trigger)

It is recommended to set HOMING_BUMP_MM to { 0, 0, 0 }.

SPI_ENDSTOPS  *** TMC2130, TMC2240, and TMC5160 Only ***
Poll the driver through SPI to determine load when homing.
Removes the need for a wire from DIAG1 to an endstop pin.

IMPROVE_HOMING_RELIABILITY tunes acceleration and jerk when
homing and adds a guard period for endstop triggering.

Comment *_STALL_SENSITIVITY to disable sensorless homing for that axis.

SENSORLESS_STALLGUARD_DELAY.name: SENSORLESS_STALLGUARD_DELAY
SENSORLESS_STALLGUARD_DELAY.comment: (ms) Delay to allow drivers to settle

SENSORLESS_HOMING_TEST_MENU_ITEMS.name: SENSORLESS_HOMING_TEST_MENU_ITEMS
SENSORLESS_HOMING_TEST_MENU_ITEMS.comment: Convenient homing menu items next to Sensorless Homing edit items

I2C_MASTER_ID.name: I2C_MASTER_ID
I2C_MASTER_ID.comment:
I2C Master ID for LPC176x LCD and Digital Current control
Does not apply to other peripherals based on the Wire library.

EXPERIMENTAL_I2CBUS.name: EXPERIMENTAL_I2CBUS
EXPERIMENTAL_I2CBUS.comment:
TWI/I2C BUS

This feature is EXPERIMENTAL but may be useful for custom I2C peripherals.
Enable this to send and receive I2C data from slave devices on the bus.

; Example #1
; This macro send the string "Marlin" to the slave device with address 0x63 (99)
; It uses multiple M260 commands with one B<base 10> arg
M260 A99  ; Target slave address
M260 B77  ; M
M260 B97  ; a
M260 B114 ; r
M260 B108 ; l
M260 B105 ; i
M260 B110 ; n
M260 S1   ; Send the current buffer

; Example #2
; Request 6 bytes from slave device with address 0x63 (99)
M261 A99 B5

; Example #3
; Example serial output of a M261 request
echo:i2c-reply: from:99 bytes:5 data:hello

I2C_SLAVE_ADDRESS.name: I2C_SLAVE_ADDRESS
I2C_SLAVE_ADDRESS.comment: Set a value from 8 to 127 to act as a slave

PHOTO_GCODE.name: PHOTO_GCODE
PHOTO_GCODE.comment:
Photo G-code
Add the M240 G-code to take a photo.
The photo can be triggered by a digital pin or a physical movement.

PHOTO_POSITION.name: PHOTO_POSITION
PHOTO_POSITION.comment: A position to move to (and raise Z) before taking the photo
PHOTO_POSITION.notes: { xpos, ypos, zraise } (M240 X Y Z)

PHOTO_DELAY_MS.name: PHOTO_DELAY_MS
PHOTO_DELAY_MS.comment: (ms) Duration to pause before moving back (M240 P)

PHOTO_RETRACT_MM.name: PHOTO_RETRACT_MM
PHOTO_RETRACT_MM.comment: (mm) E retract/recover for the photo move (M240 R S)

PHOTOGRAPH_PIN.name: PHOTOGRAPH_PIN
PHOTOGRAPH_PIN.comment:
Canon RC-1 or homebrew digital camera trigger
Data from: https://web.archive.org/web/20250327153953/www.doc-diy.net/photo/rc-1_hacked/

PHOTO_SWITCH_POSITION.name: PHOTO_SWITCH_POSITION
PHOTO_SWITCH_POSITION.comment: Optional second move with delay to trigger the camera shutter

PHOTO_SWITCH_MS.name: PHOTO_SWITCH_MS
PHOTO_SWITCH_MS.comment: Duration to hold the switch or keep CHDK_PIN high

PHOTO_PULSES_US.name: PHOTO_PULSES_US
PHOTO_PULSES_US.comment:
PHOTO_PULSES_US may need adjustment depending on board and camera model.
Pin must be running at 48.4kHz.
Be sure to use a PHOTOGRAPH_PIN which can rise and fall quick enough.
(e.g., MKS SBase temp sensor pin was too slow, so used P1.23 on J8.)

 Example pulse data for Nikon: https://bit.ly/2FKD0Aq
                    IR Wiring: https://git.io/JvJf7

PHOTO_PULSE_DELAY_US.name: PHOTO_PULSE_DELAY_US
PHOTO_PULSE_DELAY_US.comment: (µs) Approximate duration of each HIGH and LOW pulse in the oscillation

SPINDLE_FEATURE.name: SPINDLE_FEATURE
SPINDLE_FEATURE.comment:
Spindle & Laser control

Add the M3, M4, and M5 commands to turn the spindle/laser on and off, and
to set spindle speed, spindle direction, and laser power.

SuperPID is a router/spindle speed controller used in the CNC milling community.
Marlin can be used to turn the spindle on and off. It can also be used to set
the spindle speed from 5,000 to 30,000 RPM.

You'll need to select a pin for the ON/OFF function and optionally choose a 0-5V
hardware PWM pin for the speed control and a pin for the rotation direction.

See https://marlinfw.org/docs/configuration/2.0.9/laser_spindle.html for more config details.

SPINDLE_LASER_ACTIVE_STATE.name: SPINDLE_LASER_ACTIVE_STATE
SPINDLE_LASER_ACTIVE_STATE.comment: Set to "HIGH" if SPINDLE_LASER_ENA_PIN is active HIGH

SPINDLE_LASER_USE_PWM.name: SPINDLE_LASER_USE_PWM
SPINDLE_LASER_USE_PWM.comment: Enable if your controller supports setting the speed/power

SPINDLE_LASER_PWM_INVERT.name: SPINDLE_LASER_PWM_INVERT
SPINDLE_LASER_PWM_INVERT.comment: Set to "true" if the speed/power goes up when you want it to go slower

SPINDLE_LASER_FREQUENCY.name: SPINDLE_LASER_FREQUENCY
SPINDLE_LASER_FREQUENCY.comment: (Hz) Spindle/laser frequency (only on supported HALs: AVR, ESP32, and LPC) ESP32: If SPINDLE_LASER_PWM_PIN is onboard then <=78125Hz. For I2S expander the frequency determines the PWM resolution. 2500Hz = 0-100, 977Hz = 0-255, ... (250000 / SPINDLE_LASER_FREQUENCY) = max value.

AIR_EVACUATION.name: AIR_EVACUATION
AIR_EVACUATION.comment: Cutter Vacuum / Laser Blower motor control with G-codes M10-M11

AIR_EVACUATION_ACTIVE.name: AIR_EVACUATION_ACTIVE
AIR_EVACUATION_ACTIVE.comment: Set to "HIGH" if the on/off function is active HIGH

AIR_EVACUATION_PIN.name: AIR_EVACUATION_PIN
AIR_EVACUATION_PIN.comment: Override the default Cutter Vacuum or Laser Blower pin

AIR_ASSIST.name: AIR_ASSIST
AIR_ASSIST.comment: Air Assist control with G-codes M8-M9

AIR_ASSIST_ACTIVE.name: AIR_ASSIST_ACTIVE
AIR_ASSIST_ACTIVE.comment: Active state on air assist pin

AIR_ASSIST_PIN.name: AIR_ASSIST_PIN
AIR_ASSIST_PIN.comment: Override the default Air Assist pin

SPINDLE_SERVO.name: SPINDLE_SERVO
SPINDLE_SERVO.comment: A servo converting an angle to spindle power

SPINDLE_SERVO_NR.name: SPINDLE_SERVO_NR
SPINDLE_SERVO_NR.comment: Index of servo used for spindle control

CUTTER_POWER_UNIT.name: CUTTER_POWER_UNIT
CUTTER_POWER_UNIT.comment:
Speed / Power can be set ('M3 S') and displayed in terms of:
 - PWM255  (S0 - S255)
 - PERCENT (S0 - S100)
 - RPM     (S0 - S50000)  Best for use with a spindle
 - SERVO   (S0 - S180)

CUTTER_POWER_RELATIVE.name: CUTTER_POWER_RELATIVE
CUTTER_POWER_RELATIVE.comment:
Relative Cutter Power
Normally, 'M3 O<power>' sets
OCR power is relative to the range SPEED_POWER_MIN...SPEED_POWER_MAX.
so input powers of 0...255 correspond to SPEED_POWER_MIN...SPEED_POWER_MAX
instead of normal range (0 to SPEED_POWER_MAX).
Best used with (e.g.) SuperPID router controller: S0 = 5,000 RPM and S255 = 30,000 RPM

SPINDLE_CHANGE_DIR.name: SPINDLE_CHANGE_DIR
SPINDLE_CHANGE_DIR.comment: Enable if your spindle controller can change spindle direction

SPINDLE_CHANGE_DIR_STOP.name: SPINDLE_CHANGE_DIR_STOP
SPINDLE_CHANGE_DIR_STOP.comment: Enable if the spindle should stop before changing spin direction

SPINDLE_INVERT_DIR.name: SPINDLE_INVERT_DIR
SPINDLE_INVERT_DIR.comment: Set to "true" if the spin direction is reversed

SPINDLE_LASER_POWERUP_DELAY.name: SPINDLE_LASER_POWERUP_DELAY
SPINDLE_LASER_POWERUP_DELAY.comment: (ms) Delay to allow the spindle/laser to come up to speed/power

SPINDLE_LASER_POWERDOWN_DELAY.name: SPINDLE_LASER_POWERDOWN_DELAY
SPINDLE_LASER_POWERDOWN_DELAY.comment: (ms) Delay to allow the spindle to stop

SPEED_POWER_INTERCEPT.name: SPEED_POWER_INTERCEPT
SPEED_POWER_INTERCEPT.comment:
M3/M4 Power Equation

Each tool uses different value ranges for speed / power control.
These parameters are used to convert between tool power units and PWM.

Speed/Power = (PWMDC / 255 * 100 - SPEED_POWER_INTERCEPT) / SPEED_POWER_SLOPE
PWMDC = (spdpwr - SPEED_POWER_MIN) / (SPEED_POWER_MAX - SPEED_POWER_MIN) / SPEED_POWER_SLOPE

DEFAULT_ACCELERATION_SPINDLE.name: DEFAULT_ACCELERATION_SPINDLE
DEFAULT_ACCELERATION_SPINDLE.comment: (°/s/s) Default spindle acceleration (speed change with time)

LASER_TEST_PULSE_MIN.name: LASER_TEST_PULSE_MIN
LASER_TEST_PULSE_MIN.comment: Define the minimum and maximum test pulse time values for a laser test fire function

LASER_TEST_PULSE_MAX.name: LASER_TEST_PULSE_MAX
LASER_TEST_PULSE_MAX.comment: (ms) Caution: Menu may not show more than 3 characters

LASER_SAFETY_TIMEOUT_MS.name: LASER_SAFETY_TIMEOUT_MS
LASER_SAFETY_TIMEOUT_MS.comment:
Laser Safety Timeout

The laser should be turned off when there is no movement for a period of time.
Consider material flammability, cut rate, and G-code order when setting this
value. Too low and it could turn off during a very slow move; too high and
the material could ignite.

LASER_POWER_SYNC.name: LASER_POWER_SYNC
LASER_POWER_SYNC.comment:
Enable M3 commands for laser mode inline power planner syncing.
This feature enables any M3 S-value to be injected into the block buffers while in
CUTTER_MODE_CONTINUOUS. The option allows M3 laser power to be committed without waiting
for a planner synchronization

LASER_POWER_TRAP.name: LASER_POWER_TRAP
LASER_POWER_TRAP.comment:
Scale the laser's power in proportion to the movement rate.

- Sets the entry power proportional to the entry speed over the nominal speed.
- Ramps the power up every N steps to approximate the speed trapezoid.
- Due to the limited power resolution this is only approximate.

I2C_AMMETER.name: I2C_AMMETER
I2C_AMMETER.comment: Laser I2C Ammeter (High precision INA226 low/high side module)

I2C_AMMETER_IMAX.name: I2C_AMMETER_IMAX
I2C_AMMETER_IMAX.comment: (Amps) Calibration value for the expected current range

FLOWMETER_PIN.name: FLOWMETER_PIN
FLOWMETER_PIN.comment: Requires an external interrupt-enabled pin (e.g., RAMPS 2,3,18,19,20,21)

FLOWMETER_PPL.name: FLOWMETER_PPL
FLOWMETER_PPL.comment: (pulses/liter) Flow meter pulses-per-liter on the input pin

FLOWMETER_INTERVAL.name: FLOWMETER_INTERVAL
FLOWMETER_INTERVAL.comment: (ms) Flow rate calculation interval in milliseconds

FLOWMETER_SAFETY.name: FLOWMETER_SAFETY
FLOWMETER_SAFETY.comment: Prevent running the laser without the minimum flow rate set below

LASER_SYNCHRONOUS_M106_M107.name: LASER_SYNCHRONOUS_M106_M107
LASER_SYNCHRONOUS_M106_M107.comment:
Synchronous Laser Control with M106/M107

Marlin normally applies M106/M107 fan speeds at a time "soon after" processing
a planner block. This is too inaccurate for a PWM/TTL laser attached to the fan
header (as with some add-on laser kits). Enable this option to set fan/laser
speeds with much more exact timing for improved print fidelity.

NOTE: This option sacrifices some cooling fan speed options.

COOLANT_CONTROL.name: COOLANT_CONTROL
COOLANT_CONTROL.comment:
Coolant Control

Add the M7, M8, and M9 commands to turn mist or flood coolant on and off.

Note: COOLANT_MIST_PIN and/or COOLANT_FLOOD_PIN must also be defined.

COOLANT_MIST.name: COOLANT_MIST
COOLANT_MIST.comment: Enable if mist coolant is present

COOLANT_FLOOD.name: COOLANT_FLOOD
COOLANT_FLOOD.comment: Enable if flood coolant is present

COOLANT_MIST_INVERT.name: COOLANT_MIST_INVERT
COOLANT_MIST_INVERT.comment: Set "true" if the on/off function is reversed

COOLANT_FLOOD_INVERT.name: COOLANT_FLOOD_INVERT
COOLANT_FLOOD_INVERT.comment: Set "true" if the on/off function is reversed

CNC_COORDINATE_SYSTEMS.name: CNC_COORDINATE_SYSTEMS
CNC_COORDINATE_SYSTEMS.comment:
CNC Coordinate Systems

Enables G53 and G54-G59.3 commands to select coordinate systems
and G92.1 to reset the workspace to native machine space.

FILAMENT_WIDTH_SENSOR.name: FILAMENT_WIDTH_SENSOR
FILAMENT_WIDTH_SENSOR.comment:
Filament Width Sensor

Measures the filament width in real-time and adjusts
flow rate to compensate for any irregularities.

Also allows the measured filament diameter to set the
extrusion rate, so the slicer only has to specify the
volume.

Only a single extruder is supported at this time.

 34 RAMPS_14    : Analog input 5 on the AUX2 connector
 81 PRINTRBOARD : Analog input 2 on the Exp1 connector (version B,C,D,E)
301 RAMBO       : Analog input 3

Note: May require analog pins to be defined for other boards.

FILAMENT_SENSOR_EXTRUDER_NUM.name: FILAMENT_SENSOR_EXTRUDER_NUM
FILAMENT_SENSOR_EXTRUDER_NUM.comment: Index of the extruder that has the filament sensor. :[0,1,2,3,4]

MEASUREMENT_DELAY_CM.name: MEASUREMENT_DELAY_CM
MEASUREMENT_DELAY_CM.comment: (cm) The distance from the filament sensor to the melting chamber

FILWIDTH_ERROR_MARGIN.name: FILWIDTH_ERROR_MARGIN
FILWIDTH_ERROR_MARGIN.comment: (mm) If a measurement differs too much from nominal width ignore it

MAX_MEASUREMENT_DELAY.name: MAX_MEASUREMENT_DELAY
MAX_MEASUREMENT_DELAY.comment: (bytes) Buffer size for stored measurements (1 byte per cm). Must be larger than MEASUREMENT_DELAY_CM.

FILAMENT_LCD_DISPLAY.name: FILAMENT_LCD_DISPLAY
FILAMENT_LCD_DISPLAY.comment: Display filament width on the LCD status line. Status messages will expire after 5 seconds.

POWER_MONITOR_CURRENT.name: POWER_MONITOR_CURRENT
POWER_MONITOR_CURRENT.comment:
Power Monitor
Monitor voltage (V) and/or current (A), and -when possible- power (W)

Read and configure with M430

The current sensor feeds DC voltage (relative to the measured current) to an analog pin
The voltage sensor feeds DC voltage (relative to the measured voltage) to an analog pin

POWER_MONITOR_VOLTS_PER_AMP.name: POWER_MONITOR_VOLTS_PER_AMP
POWER_MONITOR_VOLTS_PER_AMP.comment: Input voltage to the MCU analog pin per amp  - DO NOT apply more than ADC_VREF!

POWER_MONITOR_CURRENT_OFFSET.name: POWER_MONITOR_CURRENT_OFFSET
POWER_MONITOR_CURRENT_OFFSET.comment: Offset (in amps) applied to the calculated current

POWER_MONITOR_FIXED_VOLTAGE.name: POWER_MONITOR_FIXED_VOLTAGE
POWER_MONITOR_FIXED_VOLTAGE.comment: Voltage for a current sensor with no voltage sensor (for power display)

POWER_MONITOR_VOLTS_PER_VOLT.name: POWER_MONITOR_VOLTS_PER_VOLT
POWER_MONITOR_VOLTS_PER_VOLT.comment: Input voltage to the MCU analog pin per volt - DO NOT apply more than ADC_VREF!

POWER_MONITOR_VOLTAGE_OFFSET.name: POWER_MONITOR_VOLTAGE_OFFSET
POWER_MONITOR_VOLTAGE_OFFSET.comment: Offset (in volts) applied to the calculated voltage

EXPECTED_PRINTER_CHECK.name: EXPECTED_PRINTER_CHECK
EXPECTED_PRINTER_CHECK.comment:
Expected Printer Check
Add the M16 G-code to compare a string to the MACHINE_NAME.
M16 with a non-matching string causes the printer to halt.

VOLUMETRIC_DEFAULT_ON.name: VOLUMETRIC_DEFAULT_ON
VOLUMETRIC_DEFAULT_ON.comment:
Volumetric extrusion default state
Activate to make volumetric extrusion the default method,
with DEFAULT_NOMINAL_FILAMENT_DIA as the default diameter.

M200 D0 to disable, M200 Dn to set a new diameter (and enable volumetric).
M200 S0/S1 to disable/enable volumetric extrusion.

DEFAULT_VOLUMETRIC_EXTRUDER_LIMIT.name: DEFAULT_VOLUMETRIC_EXTRUDER_LIMIT
DEFAULT_VOLUMETRIC_EXTRUDER_LIMIT.comment:
Default volumetric extrusion limit in cubic mm per second (mm^3/sec).
This factory setting applies to all extruders.
Use 'M200 [T<extruder>] L<limit>' to override and 'M502' to reset.
A non-zero value activates Volume-based Extrusion Limiting.

M114_DETAIL.name: M114_DETAIL
M114_DETAIL.comment: Extra options for the M114 "Current Position" report
M114_DETAIL.notes: Use 'M114 D' for details to check planner calculations

M114_REALTIME.name: M114_REALTIME
M114_REALTIME.comment: Real current position based on forward kinematics

M114_LEGACY.name: M114_LEGACY
M114_LEGACY.comment: M114 used to synchronize on every call. Enable if needed.

AUTO_REPORT_FANS.name: AUTO_REPORT_FANS
AUTO_REPORT_FANS.comment:
Auto-report fan speed with M123 S<seconds>
Requires fans with tachometer pins

REPORT_FAN_CHANGE.name: REPORT_FAN_CHANGE
REPORT_FAN_CHANGE.comment: Report the new fan speed when changed by M106 (and others)

AUTO_REPORT_REDUNDANT.name: AUTO_REPORT_REDUNDANT
AUTO_REPORT_REDUNDANT.comment: Include the "R" sensor in the auto-report

CAPABILITIES_REPORT.name: CAPABILITIES_REPORT
CAPABILITIES_REPORT.comment:
M115 - Report capabilities. Disable to save ~1150 bytes of flash.
       Some hosts (and serial TFT displays) rely on this feature.

CUSTOM_MENU_MAIN_SCRIPT_RETURN.name: CUSTOM_MENU_MAIN_SCRIPT_RETURN
CUSTOM_MENU_MAIN_SCRIPT_RETURN.comment: Return to status screen after a script

CUSTOM_MENU_MAIN_ONLY_IDLE.name: CUSTOM_MENU_MAIN_ONLY_IDLE
CUSTOM_MENU_MAIN_ONLY_IDLE.comment: Only show custom menu when the machine is idle

MAIN_MENU_ITEM_1_CONFIRM.name: MAIN_MENU_ITEM_1_CONFIRM
MAIN_MENU_ITEM_1_CONFIRM.comment: Show a confirmation dialog before this action

MAIN_MENU_ITEM_1_IMMEDIATE.name: MAIN_MENU_ITEM_1_IMMEDIATE
MAIN_MENU_ITEM_1_IMMEDIATE.comment: Skip the queue and execute immediately. Rarely needed.

CUSTOM_MENU_CONFIG_SCRIPT_RETURN.name: CUSTOM_MENU_CONFIG_SCRIPT_RETURN
CUSTOM_MENU_CONFIG_SCRIPT_RETURN.comment: Return to status screen after a script

CUSTOM_MENU_CONFIG_ONLY_IDLE.name: CUSTOM_MENU_CONFIG_ONLY_IDLE
CUSTOM_MENU_CONFIG_ONLY_IDLE.comment: Only show custom menu when the machine is idle

CONFIG_MENU_ITEM_1_CONFIRM.name: CONFIG_MENU_ITEM_1_CONFIRM
CONFIG_MENU_ITEM_1_CONFIRM.comment: Show a confirmation dialog before this action

CONFIG_MENU_ITEM_1_IMMEDIATE.name: CONFIG_MENU_ITEM_1_IMMEDIATE
CONFIG_MENU_ITEM_1_IMMEDIATE.comment: Skip the queue and execute immediately. Rarely needed.

CUSTOM_USER_BUTTONS.name: CUSTOM_USER_BUTTONS
CUSTOM_USER_BUTTONS.comment:
User-defined buttons to run custom G-code.
Up to 25 may be defined.

BUTTON1_HIT_STATE.name: BUTTON1_HIT_STATE
BUTTON1_HIT_STATE.comment: State of the triggered button. NC=LOW. NO=HIGH.

BUTTON1_WHEN_PRINTING.name: BUTTON1_WHEN_PRINTING
BUTTON1_WHEN_PRINTING.comment: Button allowed to trigger during printing?

BUTTON1_DESC.name: BUTTON1_DESC
BUTTON1_DESC.comment: Optional string to set the LCD status

BUTTON1_IMMEDIATE.name: BUTTON1_IMMEDIATE
BUTTON1_IMMEDIATE.comment: Skip the queue and execute immediately. Rarely needed.

HOST_ACTION_COMMANDS.name: HOST_ACTION_COMMANDS
HOST_ACTION_COMMANDS.comment:
Host Action Commands

Define host streamer action commands in compliance with the standard.

See https://reprap.org/wiki/G-code#Action_commands
Common commands ........ poweroff, pause, paused, resume, resumed, cancel
G29_RETRY_AND_RECOVER .. probe_rewipe, probe_failed

Some features add reason codes to extend these commands.

Host Prompt Support enables Marlin to use the host for user prompts so
filament runout and other processes can be managed from the host side.

HOST_PAUSE_M76.name: HOST_PAUSE_M76
HOST_PAUSE_M76.comment: Tell the host to pause in response to M76

HOST_PROMPT_SUPPORT.name: HOST_PROMPT_SUPPORT
HOST_PROMPT_SUPPORT.comment: Initiate host prompts to get user feedback

HOST_STATUS_NOTIFICATIONS.name: HOST_STATUS_NOTIFICATIONS
HOST_STATUS_NOTIFICATIONS.comment: Send some status messages to the host as notifications

HOST_START_MENU_ITEM.name: HOST_START_MENU_ITEM
HOST_START_MENU_ITEM.comment: Add a menu item that tells the host to start

HOST_SHUTDOWN_MENU_ITEM.name: HOST_SHUTDOWN_MENU_ITEM
HOST_SHUTDOWN_MENU_ITEM.comment: Add a menu item that tells the host to shut down

I2C_POSITION_ENCODERS.name: I2C_POSITION_ENCODERS
I2C_POSITION_ENCODERS.comment:
I2C position encoders for closed loop control.
Developed by Chris Barr at Aus3D.

Wiki: https://wiki.aus3d.com.au/Magnetic_Encoder
GitHub: https://github.com/Aus3D/MagneticEncoder

Supplier: https://aus3d.com.au/products/magnetic-encoder-module
Alternative Supplier: https://reliabuild3d.com/

Reliabuild encoders have been modified to improve reliability.

I2CPE_ENCODER_CNT.name: I2CPE_ENCODER_CNT
I2CPE_ENCODER_CNT.comment: The number of encoders installed; max of 5 encoders supported currently.

I2CPE_ENC_1_AXIS.name: I2CPE_ENC_1_AXIS
I2CPE_ENC_1_AXIS.comment: Axis the encoder module is installed on.  <X|Y|Z|E>_AXIS.

I2CPE_ENC_1_TICKS_UNIT.name: I2CPE_ENC_1_TICKS_UNIT
I2CPE_ENC_1_TICKS_UNIT.comment: 1024 for magnetic strips with 2mm poles; 2048 for 1mm poles. For linear encoders this is ticks / mm, for rotary encoders this is ticks / revolution.

I2CPE_ENC_1_TICKS_REV.name: I2CPE_ENC_1_TICKS_REV
I2CPE_ENC_1_TICKS_REV.comment: Only needed for rotary encoders; number of stepper steps per full revolution (motor steps/rev * microstepping)

I2CPE_ENC_1_INVERT.name: I2CPE_ENC_1_INVERT
I2CPE_ENC_1_INVERT.comment: Invert the direction of axis travel.

I2CPE_ENC_1_EC_THRESH.name: I2CPE_ENC_1_EC_THRESH
I2CPE_ENC_1_EC_THRESH.comment: Threshold size for error (in mm) above which the printer will attempt to correct the error; errors smaller than this are ignored to minimize effects of measurement noise / latency (filter).

I2CPE_ENC_2_ADDR.name: I2CPE_ENC_2_ADDR
I2CPE_ENC_2_ADDR.comment: Same as above, but for encoder 2.

I2CPE_ENC_3_AXIS.name: I2CPE_ENC_3_AXIS
I2CPE_ENC_3_AXIS.comment: as above, or use defaults below.

I2CPE_DEF_TYPE.name: I2CPE_DEF_TYPE
I2CPE_DEF_TYPE.comment: Default settings for encoders which are enabled, but without settings configured above.

I2CPE_ERR_THRESH_ABORT.name: I2CPE_ERR_THRESH_ABORT
I2CPE_ERR_THRESH_ABORT.comment: Threshold size for error (in mm) error on any given axis after which the printer will abort. Comment out to disable abort behavior.

I2CPE_TIME_TRUSTED.name: I2CPE_TIME_TRUSTED
I2CPE_TIME_TRUSTED.comment: After an encoder fault, there must be no further fault for this amount of time (in ms) before the encoder is trusted again.

I2CPE_MIN_UPD_TIME_MS.name: I2CPE_MIN_UPD_TIME_MS
I2CPE_MIN_UPD_TIME_MS.comment:
Position is checked every time a new command is executed from the buffer but during long moves,
this setting determines the minimum update time between checks. A value of 100 works well with
error rolling average when attempting to correct only for skips and not for vibration.

I2CPE_ERR_ROLLING_AVERAGE.name: I2CPE_ERR_ROLLING_AVERAGE
I2CPE_ERR_ROLLING_AVERAGE.comment: Use a rolling average to identify persistent errors that indicate skips, as opposed to vibration and noise.

JOY_X_PIN.name: JOY_X_PIN
JOY_X_PIN.comment: RAMPS: Suggested pin A5  on AUX2

JOY_Y_PIN.name: JOY_Y_PIN
JOY_Y_PIN.comment: RAMPS: Suggested pin A10 on AUX2

JOY_Z_PIN.name: JOY_Z_PIN
JOY_Z_PIN.comment: RAMPS: Suggested pin A12 on AUX2

JOY_EN_PIN.name: JOY_EN_PIN
JOY_EN_PIN.comment: RAMPS: Suggested pin D44 on AUX2

INVERT_JOY_X.name: INVERT_JOY_X
INVERT_JOY_X.comment: Enable if X direction is reversed

INVERT_JOY_Y.name: INVERT_JOY_Y
INVERT_JOY_Y.comment: Enable if Y direction is reversed

INVERT_JOY_Z.name: INVERT_JOY_Z
INVERT_JOY_Z.comment: Enable if Z direction is reversed

JOY_X_LIMITS.name: JOY_X_LIMITS
JOY_X_LIMITS.comment: Use M119 with JOYSTICK_DEBUG to find reasonable values after connecting:
JOY_X_LIMITS.notes: min, deadzone start, deadzone end, max

FREEZE_FEATURE.name: FREEZE_FEATURE
FREEZE_FEATURE.comment:
Freeze / Unfreeze

Pause / Hold that keeps power available and does not stop the spindle can be initiated by
the FREEZE_PIN. Halts instantly (default) or performs a soft feed hold that decelerates and
halts movement at FREEZE_JERK (requires SOFT_FEED_HOLD).
Motion can be resumed by using the FREEZE_PIN.

NOTE: Controls Laser PWM but does NOT pause Spindle, Fans, Heaters or other devices.

SOFT_FEED_HOLD.name: SOFT_FEED_HOLD
SOFT_FEED_HOLD.comment:
Command P000 (REALTIME_REPORTING_COMMANDS and EMERGENCY_PARSER) or
FREEZE_PIN (FREEZE_FEATURE) initiates a soft feed hold that keeps
power available and does not stop the spindle.

The soft feed hold decelerates and halts movement at FREEZE_JERK.
Motion can be resumed with command R000 (requires REALTIME_REPORTING_COMMANDS) or
by using the FREEZE_PIN (requires FREEZE_FEATURE).

NOTE: Affects Laser PWM but DOES NOT pause Spindle, Fans, Heaters or other devices.

FREEZE_JERK.name: FREEZE_JERK
FREEZE_JERK.comment: (mm/s) Completely halt when motion has decelerated below this value

MAX7219_DEBUG.name: MAX7219_DEBUG
MAX7219_DEBUG.comment:
MAX7219 Debug Matrix
